(5) dogfood-html as gate — Stage 9 created `phase2-dogfood.html` parallel to `phase1-dogfood.html`, 8-step founder-run smoke covering picker → load → intent swap → completion pill → backup-then-load → reload persistence. **Process**: brainstorming (Phase 2 spec section locked from Phase 1 follow-up direction) → spec doc (`docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-20-tb-revamp-design.md` §5 intent + §9 scenarios) → 10-stage implementation plan (`docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-20-tb-v3-phase2.md`, ~2,031 lines, ALL stages detailed bite-by-bite per the founder discipline — no "outlined only" tasks) → subagent-driven dev (Stages 0-9 walked continuously in one session, 22 commits) → live Claude Preview dogfood (founder confirmed picker + load + intent + completion + backup-restore + reload-persistence all working) → 1 founder-requested addition during dogfood (commit `dd9dd4b` adds click+drag pan with 3px threshold, verified live in the preview before commit) → ship signal → v5.8.0. **Phase 2.x follow-ups (deferred per founder direction)**: full 20-25 scenario catalog (remaining ~17 scenarios); per-scenario AI Coach contextualisation (Tier C prompt awareness of `activeScenarioId`); per-scenario hint system; solution replay; optional auto-close-picker-on-row-click (current behaviour leaves picker open with `.on` highlight on the loaded row — known UX point flagged in dogfood-html footer). **Phase 3** (separate plan): Simulate mode (L2/L3 sim engine) + full completion validator (reachability not just topology shape). **Fast-lane** per ENVIRONMENT_STRATEGY (HTML/CSS/JS additive — feature module modification + 1 STORAGE key + scoped CSS; no DB/auth/money; sw.js = universal CACHE_NAME bump from bump-version.js) → canonical trunk push, CI-gated. The Topology Builder v3 now ships its second phase: the canvas foundation (Phase 1) becomes a launchpad for guided learning via a curated scenarios catalog + structured completion goals + non-destructive Free Build ↔ Lab swaps, with the Phase 3-9 mode systems (Simulate / Trace / OSI / 3D / Coach / Grade) and the v1+v2 retirement still ahead. |
| v5.7.0 | **Topology Builder v3 — Phase 1 canvas foundation** — full ground-up rebuild of the Topology Builder, the cert-app's flagship drill. Founder directive: "use superpowers brainstorm skill to help brainstorm the revamp… not satisfied with v1 and v2… need to incrementally build this feature." V1 (~14.6K LOC in app.js) + V2 (~3.5K LOC in features/topology-builder-v2.js) both fell short on ease of use + canvas size; Phase 1 ships the editorial workspace + canvas foundation MVP — the bones onto which Phases 2–9 (Lab + Free Build + PBQ intents, Simulate/Trace/OSI/3D modes, scenarios picker, AI coach, grade engine, v1+v2 retirement) will land bite-by-bite. **Architecture**: 9th lazy-loaded feature module (`features/topology-builder-v3.js`, ~900 LOC IIFE registered on `window._certanvilFeatures['topology-builder-v3']`) + dedicated scoped CSS file (`features/topology-builder-v3.css`, 412 lines / 14,994 bytes — first feature with its own stylesheet, loaded via `_ensureCss()` link injection so the cert-app shell never pays for it on other surfaces). Module is fully self-contained: state shape (`devices[]`, `cables[]`, `viewport{x,y,zoom}`, `selectedId`, `pendingCableFrom`, `intent`), 4 pure functions (`buildDevice`/`buildCable`/`serialiseState`/`parseState` — TDD'd via vm-sandbox fixtures), canvas render loop, pan/zoom/minimap, palette, drag handlers, cable drawing, selection, Inspector, debounced save, mode bar, PNG export. **Workspace shell**: full-viewport takeover via `html:has(#page-topology-builder-v3.page.active)` hiding `#app-sidebar`+`#app-topbar`+`#sb-mobile-toggle`+`#skip-link` and `overflow:hidden` on html+body — no global chrome competes with the canvas. 44px strip header (eyebrow `NET+ · N10-009` + Fraunces `Network builder.` title + `0 devices · 0 cables` live count). Floating modebar centred with 5 modes (`Design` active + `Simulate`/`Trace`/`OSI`/`3D` locked with phase hints) + `Export` right-anchored. 3-column body (200px palette + 1fr canvas + 340px Inspector when device selected; rrail floats as a 50px pill at `top:14px right:14px` when no selection so canvas gets full width). 28px status bar (intent label + mode + saving indicator + keyboard map: `SPACE+DRAG · PAN  SCROLL · ZOOM  DEL · REMOVE`). **Canvas**: SVG-based infinite canvas with viewBox transform for pan/zoom (50%-400% range via scroll wheel + zoom buttons), 40px grid snap on device drop + drag, hold `space + drag` to pan (cursor grab/grabbing), Bezier-cable rendering between device anchor points. **Minimap** (bottom-right corner): bronze rectangle indicates viewport position + dots per device, kept proportional to canvas extent. **Palette**: 6 groups (Routers / Switches / Endpoints / Wireless / Security / Cloud), 21 devices total (full lift-and-shift from v1's device catalogue per founder direction). HTML5 drag-and-drop API drops at cursor position grid-snapped; the just-dropped device is auto-selected (Inspector opens with bronze border). **Cable drawing**: press `c` to enter cable mode (cursor changes to crosshair, status bar shows `Cable mode · click two devices`), click first device + second device to draw a bronze Bezier cable, `Esc` cancels mid-cable. **Selection + Inspector**: click any device to select (bronze border + Inspector slides in from the right with Label input + Type readonly); typing into Label updates the canvas label live and the status bar flashes `· saving` then `· saved` (debounced `_saveDraft()` via `STORAGE.TB_V3_DRAFT = 'nplus_tb_v3_draft'`); `Delete`/`Backspace` removes the device + any cables touching it; `Esc` clears the selection + closes the Inspector. **Persistence**: localStorage auto-save survives reloads — devices + cables + viewport position + zoom level restore exactly. **PNG export**: `Export` modebar button serialises the current canvas (with devices + cables, no chrome) into a 2x retina PNG and downloads as `topology-<hex>.png`. **Modes**: only `Design` is live in Phase 1 — `Simulate`/`Trace`/`OSI`/`3D` render as locked pills with one-line phase-roadmap tooltips so users know they're coming. **Sidebar entry**: relabeled in `APP_SIDEBAR_PRACTICE_NETPLUS_TAIL` so v3 is FIRST as `Network Builder`, v1 demoted to `Network Builder (v1)`, v2 demoted to `Builder V2 (legacy)`. Handler is `async () => { await _loadFeature('topology-builder-v3'); showPage('topology-builder-v3'); openTopologyBuilderV3(); }` — `showPage()` BEFORE `openTopologyBuilderV3()` is load-bearing because the `:has()` full-viewport takeover requires `.active` (caught during dogfood when my own verify-eval skipped showPage and the :has selector silently no-op'd). **CSS cache-bust** (`TB3_CSS_REV`): manual revision counter in `_ensureCss()` so the URL becomes `?v=<APP_VERSION>-<TB3_CSS_REV>` — forces SW + browser cache miss between CSS edits even when APP_VERSION hasn't moved. Bumped r1→r4 during dogfood iteration; can be retired once APP_VERSION is the canonical key. **Files**: `features/topology-builder-v3.js` (NEW) · `features/topology-builder-v3.css` (NEW) · `index.html` (+1 page div) · `app.js` (+`STORAGE.TB_V3_DRAFT` + sidebar entry + CURRENT_CERT/CERT_PACK exposure already done for v5.6.0) · `tests/uat.js` (+33 asserts: 4 vm-sandbox behavioural fixtures + 13 structural guards) · `tests/e2e/app.spec.js` (+9 Playwright DOM tests in `topology-builder-v3` describe — using `page.evaluate` to call exposed `_deleteSelected` since Playwright keydown couldn't reach the document listener reliably). UAT **6462/6462 ALL PASS** (+33 from v5.6.0 baseline 6429). Playwright **chromium 99 passed** (existing suite + 9 TB v3 tests). **Dogfood-caught regressions** (2 rounds, the locked discipline): (1) round 1 — founder screenshot showed canvas not stretching to right edge; first fix floated the rrail as an absolute pill BUT the page itself was still clamped to the global `.page { max-width: 800px }` (and the 900px tablet-portrait breakpoint at styles.css:20041) — my id-scoped rule outranked `.page` on specificity but I never explicitly reset max-width, so 560px of dead canvas space remained on a 1440px viewport (commit `0cefc4b` adds `max-width:none` + `width:auto` + `min-width:0` + `overflow:hidden` to the full-viewport-takeover block); (2) round 2 — false-positive verify: my eval harness called `openTopologyBuilderV3()` directly, skipping `showPage()`, so `.active` never landed, so `:has()` never fired, so the html+body overflow:hidden never applied, so a phantom vertical scrollbar ate 15px from the right — misled me into thinking the original fix was working when it wasn't. Re-tested with the real handler sequence (showPage → open) and confirmed canvasGapRight=0 at 1440x900. **Lesson (locked)**: full-viewport-takeover surfaces MUST explicitly reset every inheritable constraint from the global `.page` rule (max-width, margin, padding, width) — relying on specificity alone fails because the global rule defines properties the id-scoped rule doesn't touch. AND: any verify-eval that drives a feature module's entry path MUST replicate the real handler sequence verbatim (showPage + open), not just call the feature's open function — `:has()` selectors that depend on `.active` will silently no-op otherwise. **Process**: brainstorming (3 design sections approved + 6 open questions resolved + Sec+ explicitly out of scope, gets own future builder) → spec doc (`docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-20-tb-revamp-design.md`) → 9-phase implementation plan (`docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-20-tb-revamp.md`, ~2,460 lines — Phase 1 detailed bite-by-bite, Phases 2-9 outlined) → subagent-driven dev (Stages 0-9, ~25 commits) → live dogfood via Claude Preview → 2 founder-caught regressions fixed on branch → ship signal → v5.7.0. **Phases 2-9 deferred** (per founder direction: "each phase is independently shippable and gets its own dogfood pass… re-planned bite-by-bite after Phase 1 ships and dogfoods cleanly"): Phase 2 = Free Build intent + scenarios picker (lift-and-shift v1 scenarios) · Phase 3 = Lab intent + first 5 guided labs · Phase 4 = PBQ intent · Phase 5 = Simulate mode (L2/L3 sim engine) · Phase 6 = Trace mode (packet visualization) · Phase 7 = OSI mode (per-layer overlay) · Phase 8 = 3D mode · Phase 9 = retire v1+v2 from sidebar + delete features/topology-builder-v2 + delete v1 code from app.js (the strangler-fig payoff). Fast-lane per ENVIRONMENT_STRATEGY (HTML/CSS/JS additive — 2 new files + 1 page div + 1 STORAGE key + 1 sidebar entry; no DB/auth/money; sw.js = universal CACHE_NAME bump) → canonical trunk push, CI-gated. The Topology Builder is now an editorial full-viewport workspace that matches the rest of the forged-bronze app, with the canvas + pan/zoom/minimap + palette + cables + Inspector + auto-save + PNG export foundation ready for Phases 2-9 to layer the intent + mode systems on top. |
| v5.6.0 | **Bug-report popup** — new in-app drawer surface so real users (and founder dogfood) can file issues without leaving the app or opening GitHub. **Architecture**: 8th lazy-loaded feature module (`features/reports.js`, 623 LOC IIFE registered on `window._certanvilFeatures.reports`); 3 layers — drawer UI → 4 pure functions (`buildPayload`/`renderIssueBody`/`classifyError`/`enqueueReport`) → GitHub Issues POST via existing `STORAGE.GH_TOKEN` BYOK. **Entry**: new `#topbar-bug-report` iconbtn between Settings and Theme toggle (matches v4.99.86 editorial language — 30px boxed, hairline border, accent hover); onclick `_loadFeature('reports').then(m => m.openDrawer())`. **Drawer**: right-anchored on desktop (`width:min(540px,49vw)`), full-width bottom-sheet on mobile (`@media (max-width: 767px)`); portal-to-body on open to escape `#page-setup`'s transform-based containing block (v5.5.4 pattern); slide-in via `transform:translateX(100%)→0` with `cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1)` (emil-design-eng) + reduced-motion gate; ESC/×/Cancel/backdrop all close with focus restore. **Form**: Fraunces 20px title "Report an issue" (text-transform reset to override global h3 uppercase) + bronze `§ Report` eyebrow + Title + Description required with inline error captions, char counter activates at 4,000 and hard-caps at 5,000, quiet "+ Add steps to reproduce" expander, AUTO-ATTACHED context strip (mono one-liner: version · page · cert-code · theme · viewport · last_quiz · wrong_bank_size), no-token banner when `STORAGE.GH_TOKEN` missing. **Submit pipeline**: `submitReport(payload)` posts to `https://api.github.com/repos/oremosu98/networkplus-quiz/issues` with `[user-report]` title prefix + body via `renderIssueBody()` (markdown with `## What happened` / `## Steps to reproduce` / `<details>` auto-context table / footer id) + labels `bug-report`+`cert:<current>`+`version:<v>`; `classifyError(resp)` routes 7 cases (201 success/0+network=offline/5xx=server/403+rl:0=ratelimit/403=scope/401=auth/422=payload) to `{type,queueAction,toast,terminal,[next_try]}`; success → green toast "Report filed · Issue #N · tap to open" (clickable to GH) + drawer auto-close + queue clear; failure → enqueue + toast (amber for transient, red for terminal). **Retry queue**: `STORAGE.BUG_REPORTS = nplus_bug_reports` (NEW key — distinct from existing bad-quiz `STORAGE.REPORTS` at app.js:13008); shape `[{id, payload, attempts, last_try, next_try, terminal}]`; LRU at cap 25 (drops oldest non-terminal first); drained ONCE per `DOMContentLoaded` with 2s delay (no `setInterval`, no SW workers — single non-blocking fetch per non-terminal entry); `next_try` gate for rate-limit re-queue. **Settings → Reports panel**: `renderSettingsReportsPanel(host)` hooked into `renderSettingsPage()` after the existing Danger Zone, idempotent via `#settings-reports-section` id lookup; per-row status pills (Pending=amber/Failed=red) + title preview + `rpt_` id + attempts + failure reason in mono + `_ago()` timestamp + per-row Retry/Delete actions + "Retry all now" header CTA + storage shape footer. **Topbar queue dot**: `.has-queue::after` 6px accent dot when queue non-empty; `_updateTopbarDot()` called from submitReport/drainQueue/Settings actions. **Cross-cert leak filter**: `_getCtx()` reads new `window.CURRENT_CERT` + `window.CERT_PACK` exposures (added to app.js to make existing module-scope consts available to lazy-loaded modules; pure exposure, no behavior change in shell) + `meta.code` (NOT `meta.objectiveCode` — that key doesn't exist; dogfood-caught regression) so Sec+ auto-context shows `secplus-SY0-701`, never leaks netplus topics. **CSS**: 230-line block appended to `dg-system.css` scoped to `#bug-report-drawer` + `.br-toast` + `.br-sp-*` Settings rows; light + dark theme via OKLCH tokens + `color-mix(in oklab, ...)` for tints/shadows; **critical `[hidden]{display:none!important}` rule** added scoped to drawer (component `display:flex` rules otherwise override the attribute — dogfood-caught). **Tests**: 4 vm-sandbox behavioral fixtures in `tests/uat.js` (`buildPayload` 12 asserts · `renderIssueBody` 11 · `classifyError` 8 · `enqueueReport` 6 = +37 UAT assertions); 9 Playwright DOM tests in `tests/e2e/app.spec.js` (open from topbar / 4 close paths / Send gating / counter / expander / queue drain / no-token banner / cross-cert leak filter / topbar dot); 11 UAT structural guards (topbar iconbtn + lazy-load wire + STORAGE.BUG_REPORTS key + feature-module contract + DOMContentLoaded drain + 4 CSS class assertions + mobile breakpoint + reduced-motion gate + BUG_REPORTS-vs-REPORTS collision tombstone). UAT **6429/6429 ALL PASS** (+48 from baseline 6381). Playwright **chromium 108/108 PASS** (99 existing + 9 new). **Dogfood smoke caught 4 real bugs** behavioural fixtures couldn't see (committed as `c4892a2`): (1) "Title is required" / "Description is required" red captions showed on initial drawer open because `.br-caption-err{display:flex}` overrode the `[hidden]` attribute; (2) "SETUP NEEDED" banner showed even when token was present (same `display:flex` override); (3) auto-context rendered `netplus-undefined` because read `meta.objectiveCode` (doesn't exist) instead of `meta.code`; (4) "REPORT AN ISSUE" rendered UPPERCASE via global `h3{text-transform:uppercase}`. All 4 fixed in scoped `dg-system.css` `[hidden]{display:none!important}` + `.br-title{text-transform:none}` + `features/reports.js` `meta.code`. **Process**: brainstorming (5 design sections approved) → spec doc (`docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-20-bug-report-popup-design.md`) → implementation plan (`docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-20-bug-report-popup.md`) → subagent-driven dev (10 stages, ~24 commits) → live dogfood via Claude Preview to catch the 4 CSS-bleed bugs that pure fixtures missed. Mandatory `dg-system.css?v` hand-bump dev-token → **5.6.0**. Fast-lane per ENVIRONMENT_STRATEGY (HTML/CSS/JS additive — new file `features/reports.js` + scoped CSS + topbar iconbtn + STORAGE key + DOMContentLoaded hook; no DB/auth/money; sw.js = universal CACHE_NAME bump) → canonical trunk push, CI-gated. The CertAnvil app now has an editorial in-app bug-reporting surface that matches the rest of the forged-bronze system. |
| v5.5.12 | **Uneven hero numeral heights fix (lining-nums)** — founder caught: the v5.5.11 hero "522" had the "5" visibly taller than the two "2"s. **Root cause**: Fraunces's default numeral style is **old-style figures** (`onum`) where some digits (5/7/9/3) are full-height with descenders while others (1/2/0) sit at x-height — so "522" shows a tall 5 next to two short 2s. Classic typography mistake when shipping a Fraunces hero number without explicitly forcing **lining-nums** (`lnum` — uniform-height numerals that align to the cap-height baseline). **Fix (scoped CSS, dg-system.css)**: `#rc-v2-num` adds `font-variant-numeric:lining-nums tabular-nums` (logical CSS) + belt-and-suspenders `font-feature-settings:"lnum","tnum"` (low-level OpenType for any older engine that doesn't fully implement the high-level property). Real-cert-app verify (computed-style ground truth): `fontVariantNumeric:"lining-nums tabular-nums"`, `fontFeatureSettings:'"lnum","tnum"'`, Fraunces-600 loaded, numerals visually uniform (5/2/2 same height). UAT **6381/6381** (no impact). Full local chromium Playwright per the locked discipline. **Lesson (locked)**: any Fraunces (or any old-style-default serif) hero numeral MUST explicitly request `lining-nums` if visual consistency across digits matters. tabular-nums alone only normalizes WIDTH, not figure-style. Same pattern applies to: any score/version/counter display in the Home/Analytics/Quiz/SR surfaces — re-audit if old-style ever reappears. Mandatory `dg-system.css?v` hand-bump 5.5.11→**5.5.12**. Fast-lane (CSS-only). |
| v5.5.11 | **Hero score overlap fix** — founder caught: the v5.5.10 "522" hero (Fraunces 140px) was overlapping the "OUT OF 900" caption — the "5" glyph curve dipped into the caption ink. **Root cause**: I copied `line-height:0.92` from the masthead concept (which used it for an inline `522 / 900` baseline-flex row where tight line-height keeps the row compact) — but on the v5.5.10 **block-stacked** hero where the caption sits directly underneath, a sub-1 line-height makes the glyph's ink-bottom extend BELOW the line-box, and Fraunces's "5" has a prominent curve that crossed into the 6px caption margin. **Fix (scoped CSS, dg-system.css)**: `#rc-v2-num` line-height `0.92 → 1.0` (gives the glyph its full natural line-box so ink stays inside), caption span margin-top `6px → 14px` (extra breathing room). Real-cert-app dual-theme verify (computed-style ground truth, the masthead's documented "trust computed over screenshots" pattern): `gap` between number bottom and caption top = **15px** (was 0/overlapping); both themes faithful. UAT **6381/6381** (value-only CSS, no guard impact). **Lesson (locked)**: when copying type-system values from one layout context to another, re-check `line-height` — values < 1.0 are safe for INLINE/baseline-flex rows but unsafe for BLOCK-stacked elements where the next element sits at the line-box bottom (the glyph's natural extents will overlap). Same class of mistake as the v5.5.4 portal `var(--dgh-ease)` issue — context determines what's valid. Mandatory `dg-system.css?v` hand-bump 5.5.10→**5.5.11**. Fast-lane (CSS-only; no DB/auth/money). |
| v5.5.10 | **Readiness score hero treatment** — founder: "the score needs to be a bit larger... like a hero shot. when i was studying for my net+ it was the 1st thing i looked at, it was big and in my face." First pass (clamp 58/7vw/84 → 70/8.5vw/104, +24%) was "not big enough"; founder asked to see the desktop view properly. **Concept-mockup-first** (`mockups/readiness-hero-v3-concept.html` — 3 candidates A·140 / B·170 / C·200 at true rail width 372px with full chrome, both themes; founder picked **A · 140px**). taste-skill: the score becomes the **poster-number protagonist** on its own line; the "/ 900" qualifier is demoted to a small uppercase **OUT OF 900** caption directly below — the same single-dominant-figure + tiny-denominator pattern the masthead's Fraunces greeting hierarchy already uses. **INTEGRATION (scoped-CSS + 1-string markup change, contract-safe)**: (1) **dg-system.css** — `.readiness-score` switched from font-size-on-wrapper to `display:flex;flex-direction:column;align-items:baseline` so the children stack; `#rc-v2-num` becomes the hero `clamp(94px,11.5vw,140px)` (desktop max 140 = founder's pick A; min 94 keeps a hero feel at mobile); sibling `span:not(#rc-v2-num)` restyled as the small 13.5px uppercase Inter caption (`color:var(--text-dim)`, `letter-spacing:0.08em`, `margin-top:6px`); `.readiness-bar-track` `margin-top:22px → 34px` so the **PASS 720** notch label (`top:-17px` on the mark's `::after`) gets ~17px breathing room from the prob line above (founder follow-up: "PASS 720 is too close to the text above it"). (2) **index.html** line 294 — the static sibling span's text content `" / 900"` → `"out of 900"` (reads as a clean caption beneath the hero figure; "/ 900" with a slash looked awkward at hero scale). **Pre-CODE UAT grep**: guard 4434 reads `js.includes('/ 900')` — that asserts the **analytics** `ana-ready-denom` render path in app.js (different render, untouched); `styles.css` has `.readiness-score-block` (analytics, different class, untouched). My change is in `index.html` line 294 (codex-home) → **no UAT guard breaks, no migration** (consistent with the v5.5.2-5.5.9 scoped-reskin pattern). **Contract preserved byte-exact**: `#rc-v2-num` id + the sibling span both kept; `renderReadinessCardV2()` still just sets `#rc-v2-num.textContent`; no app.js logic change. Zero JS/handler impact. UAT **6381/6381** (unchanged — value-only CSS + 1 static-string change). app.js + dg + index.html all balanced (dg braces 2503/2503; index `<div>` 808/808). **Full local chromium Playwright 99/99 BEFORE push** (the locked v4.99.74/v5.5.6 lesson, applied even for a CSS-leaning change since `#page-setup` chrome is on every page). **Real-cert-app dual-theme verify** (the styles.css bleed trap; computed-style + visual): desktop 1440px → `numSize:140px`, `numDisplay:block`, sibling text `"out of 900"`, `barMarginTop:34px`; both themes faithful (hero ink/bronze caption light; ink/bronze-bright caption dark) — matches the approved mockup A. Mandatory `dg-system.css?v` hand-bump 5.5.9→**5.5.10** (dg-system.css changed). Fast-lane per ENVIRONMENT_STRATEGY (HTML/CSS, scoped + value-only; no DB/auth/money; sw.js = universal CACHE_NAME bump) → canonical trunk push, CI-gated. The readiness instrument is now a genuine hero — the first thing the eye lands on, exactly the founder's Net+-era reference. |
| v5.5.9 | **SW update-banner redesign** — founder: redesign the "New version available" toast, "i want an icon in there", taste-design + emil-design-eng. **Diagnosis**: `_showSwUpdateBanner()` builds `.sw-update-banner`; styles.css styled it as a flat gradient card with a white button whose label is `color:var(--accent2,#5b4fdb)` → the off-system **purple "Refresh"** the rebrand stripped everywhere else; it never got a dg editorial pass. The `.sw-banner-icon` span was already empty (v5.0.2 stripped its 📦) — the brand icon goes there. **Concept-mockup-first** (`mockups/sw-update-banner-v3-concept.html` — before/after, both themes, replayable entrance; founder "sign off and Integrate it now"). taste-skill: an editorial toast that belongs to the forged-bronze system — a `var(--surface)` card with a hairline + a *restrained* theme-adaptive shadow (a toast must still read as floating), the founder's brand sync-arrows in a small accent-tinted chip, the **solid bronze** Refresh as the single focal action, an editorial ghost ×. **Icon**: `18_sync_arrows.svg` (sync = "refresh to new version", semantically exact) — chosen over `09-cloud-sync.svg` which carries an internal `<style>` (the v5.5.8 global-leak risk) and `19_release_notes.svg` (a doc, less clearly *refresh*); trimmed to the 2 arcs + 2 arrowheads + hub, **namespaced** `caOrange`→`swSyncOrange`, `<filter>`/`<title>` dropped, outline rides `currentColor` (theme-adaptive), one arrowhead keeps the brand orange gradient. **emil-design-eng**: entrance `@keyframes swBannerEditorialIn` = `opacity + translateY(16px) + scale(0.98)→1`, ease-out ~360ms (never `scale(0)`); CTA `:active{scale(.97)}` press + fine-pointer hover; × hover; reduced-motion re-declares the keyframe as a pure fade + neutralises the button transitions. **INTEGRATION (surgical, contract-safe — the proven v5.5.8 pattern)**: (1) **styles.css UNTOUCHED** — the v4.99.27 guards 16400/16404/16409/16410 read `js` (the preserved `_showSwUpdateBanner`/title/cta/dismiss/`_swBannerShown`) and 16412/16414/16417 read `css`(styles.css) for the legacy `.sw-update-banner`/mobile/reduced-motion rules → all stay green, **no migration** (editorial rebrand never edits styles.css; dg overrides win at runtime). (2) **dg-system.css** — a scoped `html[data-theme] body .sw-update-banner` block (appended after the v5.5.8 streak block — both are `<body>` global chrome) de-gradients→`var(--surface)`, adds the hairline + restrained `color-mix` shadow, the 38px accent-tinted `.sw-banner-icon` chip, editorial title/sub, the **bronze CTA** (`background:var(--accent);color:var(--bg)` — kills the white+`#5b4fdb`), ghost ×, the emil keyframe + hover/`:active` + reduced-motion; beats styles.css via `html[data-theme] body` + later source + `!important`. **Critical**: the banner is appended to `<body>` (NOT `#page-setup`), and `--dgh-ease` is `#page-setup`-scoped — a non-ancestor `var()` would invalidate the whole declaration (the v5.5.4 portal lesson), so the easing is a **literal `cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1)`**; shadows use `color-mix(... var(--text) ...)` so they adapt per theme without a token. (3) **app.js** — the brand sync SVG inlined into the already-empty `.sw-banner-icon` span in `_showSwUpdateBanner()`; **additive only** — `.sw-update-banner`/`.sw-banner-body`/`.sw-banner-title` ("New version available")/`.sw-banner-sub`/`.sw-banner-cta` ("Refresh" → `window.location.reload()`)/`.sw-banner-dismiss` (× → `banner.remove()`)/`_swBannerShown` all byte-exact (the icon sits before the title so the 16404 title→cta→Refresh windows are unaffected). **1 new UAT regression guard** (colocated with the v4.99.27 sw-banner block, uses `js`): the namespaced `swSyncOrange` SVG is inside `.sw-banner-icon` + the `url(#swSyncOrange)` accent ref closes inside `</svg></span>` + the title/Refresh/dismiss contract — doubles as a tombstone vs reverting the icon span to empty. First regex attempt had a `{0,400}` window the ~475-char inline SVG overran (self-caught — same comment/markup-overruns-window class as v5.5.7); replaced with a window-free anchor on the accent ref. Structural: app.js+uat.js `node -c` clean; dg-system.css braces **2502/2502** (+21 balanced incl. the `@keyframes` from/to + 2 media blocks). UAT **6381/6381** (6380 + 1). **Full local chromium Playwright 99/99 BEFORE push** (global chrome — appended to `<body>` on any page post-deploy; the locked v4.99.74/v5.5.6 lesson). **Real-cert-app dual-theme verify** (the styles.css bleed trap — both stylesheets loaded, ephemeral banner inject, zero `nplus_*` writes): both themes computed-style + visual confirmed — `background:var(--surface)` (gradient gone, `background-image:none`), hairline `border` = the theme `--border`, `.sw-banner-cta` `background:var(--accent)` (dark `oklch(0.8 0.155 64)` / light `oklch(0.5 0.155 55)`) with `var(--bg)` text — the purple `#5b4fdb` is dead — 38px accent-tinted icon chip; faithful to the approved mockup. Mandatory `dg-system.css?v` hand-bump 5.5.8→**5.5.9** (dg-system.css changed; bump-version never touches the `?v`). Fast-lane per ENVIRONMENT_STRATEGY (HTML/CSS/JS, scoped CSS + additive render; no DB/auth/money; sw.js = universal CACHE_NAME bump) → canonical trunk push, CI-gated. The post-deploy update toast is now the same calm editorial component as the rest of the app, with the brand sync icon the founder asked for. |
| v5.5.8 | **Sidebar streak redesign** — founder: "the day streak needs a redesign, the current one is ugly. use taste-design and my icons folder. i want an icon in there." **Diagnosis**: the v4.99.86 sidebar editorial rebrand only recoloured `.sb-streak-num/-label` + hid the old flame emoji — it never de-carded the `.sb-streak` *container*, so it kept its legacy **styles.css** dark-gradient pill (`linear-gradient(135deg,#1e1b2e,#0f0c1b)` + radius + shadow) marooned in the light hairline editorial sidebar (card-spam in a de-carded system — that's the "ugly"). **Concept-mockup-first** (`mockups/sidebar-streak-v3-concept.html` — before/after-active/after-empty, both themes; founder "i love it. lets sign off"). taste-skill: a hairline-topped editorial **footer block** (a divider, not a slab) that belongs to the v4.99.86 sidebar language; the founder's brand flame; the streak as a bronze **Fraunces** protagonist figure + small-caps label; a new **"Longest N days"** sub from `getStreak().best` (data already computed, previously unused — `'Your best run yet'` when `best <= current`); empty state = the same calm block, flame dimmed, "Start a streak / Take your first quiz today". **Icon choice**: `07_study_streak.svg` (purpose-named) trimmed to 2 paths + a **namespaced** `sbStreakFlame` gradient, **no `<filter>`/`<title>`/`<style>`** — critically the OTHER candidate (`07-streak-flame.svg`) carries an internal `<style>` with generic global classes (`.line`/`.orange`/`.text`) that, since SVG `<style>` is NOT scoped, would leak page-wide if inlined; the chosen one is attribute-only so it inlines safely. Outline rides `currentColor` (theme-adaptive); `.sb-streak-core` keeps the brand orange (dg-system.css dims it to outline-only in the empty state). **INTEGRATION (surgical, contract-safe)**: (1) **styles.css UNTOUCHED** — the v4.54.10 css guards 6448/6450/6452 read `css`(=styles.css) and assert the *legacy* dark pill; leaving styles.css intact keeps them green (the established v4.99.86 pattern: editorial rebrand never edits styles.css, it adds `html[data-theme] body` dg overrides that win at runtime) → **no migration**. (2) **dg-system.css** — replaced the 4-line streak block with a complete scoped `html[data-theme] body .sb-streak{…}` de-card (kills bg-gradient/border/radius/shadow → editorial hairline footer) + `.sb-streak-ico`/`-top`/`-num`(Fraunces bronze)/`-label`/`-sub`/`-empty-t`/`-empty-s` rules + `.sb-streak-core` empty-state outline override + emil hover/`:active`/reduced-motion; beats styles.css via the `html[data-theme] body` + later-source + `!important` sidebar pattern; global dg `--accent`/`--border`/`--text-dim` are already theme-adaptive (same mechanism every other `.sb-*` rule uses — verified both themes). dg-system.css is **not UAT-read** so this trips no guard. (3) **app.js** — the sidebar `streakHtml` render: a single `streakFlameSvg` const (reused active+empty) + the icon span + `.sb-streak-top`/`.sb-streak-sub` (active) and the redesigned `.sb-streak-empty` body. **Contract byte-exact**: `<button class="sb-streak sb-streak-active" onclick="goSetup()">` + `.sb-streak-num` (=`s.current`) + `.sb-streak-label` + the `.sb-streak-empty` class all preserved; icon/top/sub/empty-t/empty-s are purely additive. **1 new UAT regression guard** (placed by the v5.5.7 cluster, uses `js`): asserts the namespaced `streakFlameSvg` + `.sb-streak-core` + the additive structure + the preserved `goSetup`/`.sb-streak-num`/`-label`/`.sb-streak-empty` contract — doubles as a tombstone vs a revert to the bare old markup. Self-caught + removed a malformed dead dark-theme selector mid-edit (global `--accent` already adapts; the extra rule was both wrong and unnecessary). Structural: app.js + uat.js `node -c` clean; dg-system.css braces **2481/2481** (+18 balanced). UAT **6380/6380** (6379 + 1). **Full local chromium Playwright 99/99 BEFORE push** (sidebar = global chrome on every page — a render error would break navigation everywhere; the locked v4.99.74/v5.5.6 lesson, non-negotiable here). **Real-cert-app dual-theme verify** (the styles.css bleed trap — must confirm the scoped de-card actually beats the legacy pill with both stylesheets loaded, not just in the standalone mockup; SW-unregister+cache-clear localhost-only, zero `nplus_*` writes, active-state ephemeral DOM inject): both themes computed-style + visual confirmed — `background:none` (dark gradient gone), `border-top` = the theme's `--border` hairline, `border-radius:0`, `box-shadow:none`, `.sb-streak-num` = the theme's bronze `--accent` (light `oklch(0.5 0.155 55)`), brand flame renders ~29px; faithful to the approved mockup. Mandatory `dg-system.css?v` hand-bump 5.5.7→**5.5.8** (dg-system.css changed; bump-version never touches the `?v`). Fast-lane per ENVIRONMENT_STRATEGY (HTML/CSS/JS, scoped CSS + additive render; no DB/auth/money; sw.js = universal CACHE_NAME bump) → canonical trunk push, CI-gated. The streak footer is now the same calm editorial component as the rest of the sidebar, with the brand flame the founder asked for. |
| v5.5.7 | **Cross-cert leak fix + home width** — founder spotted 3 things in Security+ mode. (1) **Layout**: the home dashboard left big dead margins ("stretch it out wider just a bit so it fits the canvas more") → `#page-setup .ch-wrap` `max-width` 1240px → **1440px** (dg-system.css, scoped, +~16%; padding/responsive-1040 collapse unchanged). (2) **Hero lede cross-cert leak (PRE-EXISTING, not v5.5.5)**: `renderHeroV2` line ~19808 built "Yesterday you wobbled on **X** and **Y**" from `computeWeakSpotScores()` with **no** cert filter — quiz history is global per user (v4.99.26) so it named N10-009 topics ("Cable Issues", "Ethernet Basics") while in SY0-701 mode. The v4.99.26 fix added `_isCurrentCertTopic` filtering to `buildSessionPlan` but **missed the hero lede**. Fixed by mirroring the exact canonical pattern: `const weak = (computeWeakSpotScores() || []).filter(w => w && _isCurrentCertTopic(w.topic));`. (3) **Continue anchor cross-cert leak (v5.5.5, mine)**: `renderContinueCard` weak path already used cert-filtered `buildSessionPlan` ✓, but the *primary* path read raw `loadHistory()[0]` (last-session headline + "Last session 0/1 · 3 days ago") and `loadWrongBank().length` ("N still in your wrong bank" / "Drill the N you missed" → `startWrongDrill`) — both global, so it surfaced the last **Net+** session ("Cable Issues") in Sec+. Fixed: `hist = (loadHistory() || []).filter(e => e && _isCurrentCertTopic(e.topic))` (so `last`/`totalQ`/`hasHistory` are cert-scoped → a cert with no own history correctly falls to the "fresh" first-action state instead of leaking the other cert) + `wrongN = (loadWrongBank() || []).filter(w => w && _isCurrentCertTopic(w.topic)).length` (entries carry `.topic`, confirmed app.js:3047; the `wrongN > 0` gate then naturally hands off to the cert-filtered weak path when the current cert has 0 wrongs). `_isCurrentCertTopic` (app.js:10673, hoisted) = `TOPIC_DOMAINS` membership (cert-populated) — the single canonical cross-cert helper; **zero new storage**, defensive try/catch preserved. **Root cause** is one shared defect class: history/wrong-bank are global-per-user but topic-bearing home surfaces must filter through `_isCurrentCertTopic`; v4.99.26 fixed `buildSessionPlan` only — the hero lede + (new) Continue anchor needed the same one-liner. **Pre-CODE UAT grep**: the renderHeroV2 guards (5707-5727) assert defined/active-class/greeting/mini-cards/goSetup-calls — none touch the `weak`/"wobbled" line; the v5.5.5 `#continue-card` guard is HTML-structure only; dg-system.css is not UAT-read → **no guard breaks, no migration**. **2 new tombstone guards** (placed after the v5.5.5 `#continue-card` guard, use `js`): renderContinueCard cert-scopes `loadHistory`+`loadWrongBank` via `_isCurrentCertTopic` · renderHeroV2 lede cert-scopes `computeWeakSpotScores` via the `|| []` form (uniquely distinct from `buildSessionPlan`'s no-`|| []` filter) — both double as reverts-fail tombstones. No CSS-width UAT guard by design (dg-system.css is deliberately not UAT-read — the v5.5.2–5.5.5 scoped-reskin invariant). First regex attempt was fn-anchored with a `[\s\S]{0,400}` window that the new comments overran (self-caught, same class as the comment-trips-grep lesson); replaced with window-free patterns. UAT **6379/6379** (6377 + 2). app.js `node -c` clean; dg-system.css braces 2463/2463 (value-only change). **Full local chromium Playwright 99/99 run BEFORE push** (the v4.99.74 / v5.5.6 locked lesson — render-path + CSS change, never skip it again). Mandatory `dg-system.css?v` hand-bump 5.5.5→**5.5.7** (v5.5.7 changes dg-system.css; bump-version doesn't touch the `?v`). Fast-lane per ENVIRONMENT_STRATEGY (HTML/CSS/JS, additive cert-filter; no DB/auth/money; sw.js = universal CACHE_NAME bump) → canonical trunk push, CI-gated. Closes the founder's prod-spot pass on the v5.5.4/5.5.5 Home work; the hero-lede half is a genuine pre-existing v4.99.26-era miss now also tombstoned. |
| v5.5.6 | **Playwright CI-hang fix** (test-infra) — founder flagged "5.5.4 didn't go live." Investigation: the v5.5.4 gating run's Playwright step ran **~59 minutes then FAILED**; the gate never went green so `deploy-verification.yml` stayed red and v5.5.4/v5.5.5 never passed CI (they DID reach prod — Vercel's Git integration builds on direct push to `main` independently of the GitHub-Actions required check, which only blocks PR merges — so the *features* were live, but CI/branch-protection were broken and future PR merges blocked). **Root cause** (diagnosed, then reproduced + fixed + locally validated — not guessed): the v4.43.4 GLOBAL `test.beforeEach` force-opened `#custom-quiz-section` on **every** test (written when it was an inline collapsed `<details>` — harmless then). v5.5.4 turned that section into a portal modal (`position:fixed; inset:0; z-index:140`, scroll-locked, reparented to `<body>`). So post-v5.5.4 **every** test ran with a full-viewport modal backdrop over the page → the ~89 tests that don't touch the modal had their clicks intercepted → 30s actionability timeout + 1 retry each → ~59-min suite → failure. A second, smaller defect: 2 tests selected `#page-setup button:has-text("Generate Quiz")` but the portal moves that button OUT of `#page-setup` to `<body>`. **Fix (tests/e2e/app.spec.js only)**: (1) removed the GLOBAL force-open from the shared `beforeEach` (kept the Pro-tier `_certanvilSignedIn`/`_quotaState`/body-class stub — still required); (2) added a scoped `openCustomQuizModal` helper applied via `test.beforeEach(openCustomQuizModal)` to **only** the 4 modal-dependent describes (`Chip Selectors`, `API Key Validation`, `Default Chip Selections`, `Topic Chip Count`) — same DOMContentLoaded→`.open` mechanism as v4.43.4 but no longer global, so the backdrop never covers the ~89 tests that don't use it; (3) repointed the 2 Generate-Quiz selectors `#page-setup` → `#custom-quiz-section` (works whether the section is in `#page-setup` or portaled). **Local validation (the discipline v5.5.4/v5.5.5 skipped — the v4.99.74 "always run Playwright locally before push" lesson, re-learned the hard way)**: targeted chromium 14/14 in **8.7s** (previously-broken non-modal tests App Load 394ms / Theme Toggle 346ms now sub-second + all modal tests pass), then the **full chromium suite 99/99 in 1.5 min** (vs ~59 min → fail pre-fix). **4 fast-lane UAT tombstones** appended to the existing v4.99.32 Playwright-spec guard block (reuses its `e2eSpec`): scoped helper exists · applied ≥4× · the GLOBAL stub is NOT immediately followed by an open (the precise ~59-min-hang signature — caught in pre-commit seconds, not after an hour of hung CI) · the old global-open comment stays deleted. UAT **6377/6377** (6373 + 4). Class-of-bug-grep applied: the tombstone's own old-comment string is `+`-concatenated so it can't self-trip. No `dg-system.css?v` hand-bump (v5.5.6 changes no served CSS; the bump-version `sw.js` CACHE_NAME bump handles cache invalidation on the Vercel rebuild). Fast-lane per ENVIRONMENT_STRATEGY (test-infra + version bump; no DB/auth/money; sw.js = universal CACHE_NAME bump) → canonical trunk push; this push's CI exercises the fix and should finally go green, unblocking branch protection + deploy-verification. **Lesson (locked):** a render-path/DOM-relocation change (esp. a new overlay/modal) can silently break the *shared Playwright fixture* for the whole suite; UAT-green is not Playwright-green; run the full chromium suite locally before any push that touches a render path — and a long-running CI step that never flips is a *hang signature*, investigate it, don't wait it out. |
| v5.5.5 | Home **Continue anchor** — founder spotted dead space in the right rail below Daily Challenge ("its kinda annoying to look at... add something that drives value or reorganise"). Root cause diagnosed (not the symptom): `#page-setup` is a rigid 2-col grid (wide `.col-main` / fixed 372px `.col-side`); the left column is intrinsically tall (hero → stats → diagnostic → the tall `#today-plan`) but the rail is mostly **conditional** cards — `#hero-v2-cta` (NBM) is `hidden` until data, `#sr-review-card` is `is-hidden` until SR data — so on a low-data account the rail collapses while the left keeps going, and `#modes-ladder` (§01 session board) is a full-width row that only starts BELOW the whole grid → the void is that bottom-right wedge. Founder chose (AskUserQuestion, 3-way fork: add value / reorganise / CSS-tighten) the **always-on Continue anchor**. taste-skill → **dashboards**: the rail's bottom becomes the operator's next action — **always rendered** (never `is-hidden` → the rail can't collapse → the void is fixed *structurally*, not papered over) + **always prescriptive** (earns its slot → not the "filler card" the dashboards anti-slop rule bans). It is the one job nothing else on Home has — **re-entry + receipts**: NBM = what's optimal, SR = cards due, Daily Challenge = the 1-Q habit, Today's Plan = the 5-topic route; **this** = pick your thread back up + proof you're moving. **Concept-mockup-first** (`mockups/home-continue-anchor-v3-concept.html`, inherits the locked forged-bronze tokens/type/emil verbatim from `home-masthead-v3-concept.html`; two extreme states + context view + theme/cert toggles; founder approved "looks great. lets ship"). **Verified pre-sign-off** via Claude Preview both extremes: light/returning (full card — momentum strip `4` day streak · `12` done today · `318` Qs in Fraunces, "Subnetting & IP Addressing", `Drill the 4 you missed →`) and dark/just-started (momentum strip **correctly suppressed** — all-zeros proves nothing, the stop-slop call — degrades to a clean confident first move "You have not built a streak yet" → `Take the 5-min Warmup →`); context strip confirmed the wedge closed in both states. **INTEGRATION (surgical, additive, contract-safe)**: (1) **index.html** — one `<div class="card continue-card reveal" id="continue-card" data-state="fresh">` placed **LAST in `.col-side`** (after the conditional `#daily-challenge-card` → it is the rail's reliable bottom), ids prefixed `cc-*` (eyebrow/momentum m1-3/headline/context/action) matching the mockup so prod == mockup; default `data-state="fresh"` hides the 0/0/0 momentum until JS fills real data; `.reveal` rides the page's existing IntersectionObserver IIFE (1.6s safety-net un-hide). (2) **app.js** — new `renderContinueCard()` (defined before `renderDailyChallengeCard`) reads existing plumbing only (`loadHistory` · `getStreak` · `getTodayQuestionCount` · `buildSessionPlan(SESSION_TOPICS)` · `loadWrongBank`), zero new storage; 3-state prescriptive logic: no real history → `data-state="fresh"` + Warmup (`applyPreset('warmup')`); has history + wrong bank → "Drill the N you missed" (`startWrongDrill()`); has history, no misses → weakest plan topic "Resume weak spots" (`focusTopic`) else "Continue weak spots" (`applyPreset('focused')`); fully defensive try/catch, `escHtml`-safe innerHTML, stop-slop copy (specific, active, `·` not em-dash). Wired into **both** home-render paths after `renderNextBestMove()` — first-paint (~1929) + `goSetup()` (~2160) — via the sibling `if (typeof renderContinueCard === 'function')` guard, same parity pattern the comment block there already documents. (3) **dg-system.css** — new scoped **v5.5.5 block** (appended EOF, like v5.5.3/v5.5.4): `html[data-theme] body #page-setup .continue-card` beats the styles.css `.card` glass; quiet hairline (NBM keeps the one bronze fill) + faint accent radial `::before` + accent eyebrow; de-carded momentum strip (Fraunces figures, hairline-divided), `.cc-action` accent-outline; `[data-state="fresh"] .cc-momentum{display:none}`; app tokens only (`--text/--text-mid/--text-dim/--accent/--surface/--bg/--border` + `--t1` + `--dgh-ease`) → cert + theme aware; the generic `#page-setup .card` hover-lift + the `prefers-reduced-motion` `.card` neutraliser already cover the card (it carries class `card`) so only the `.cc-action`/`.cc-arrow` motion + a scoped reduced-motion entry added; emil hover gated `(hover:hover) and (pointer:fine)`, `:active` press. **Pre-CODE UAT grep** confirmed no guard breaks: 1840/9959/5665 assert existence + relative ordering of *other* ids (all preserved; `continue-card` is referenced by none) → **no migration**. **1 new regression guard** (codex-home HTML v5.5.5): `#continue-card` exists with the class, ordered `#daily-challenge-card` < `#continue-card` < `#modes-ladder` (rail bottom), **NOT `is-hidden`** (load-bearing — the un-collapsibility IS the fix; tombstones any future is-hidden regression), `renderContinueCard` defined + called ≥2× via the guarded pattern. Structural sanity: dg-system.css braces 2463/2463, index.html `<div>` 808/808, `node -c app.js` clean. UAT **6373/6373**. Mandatory `dg-system.css?v` cache-bust 5.5.4→5.5.5 (the documented stale-CSS gotcha — bump-version does not touch the `?v` query). Ships alongside the v5.5.4 Custom Quiz modal in the same deploy (v5.5.4 was mid-CI when this landed on top — the canonical trunk push carries both; prod-verify covers both). Fast-lane per ENVIRONMENT_STRATEGY (HTML/CSS/JS, additive; no DB/auth/money; sw.js = universal CACHE_NAME bump) → canonical trunk push, CI-gated. The Home redesign (Sections 1 masthead · 2+3 daily+session board · 4 as-is · 5 Custom Quiz modal) is complete; this is the founder-spotted polish pass on top. |
| v5.5.4 | Home **Section 5** — Custom Quiz reworked from an on-page never-ending vertical `<details>` into a **horizontal modal popup** off the Configure → buttons (founder: "needs a redesign for sure... feels like a never ending vertical row... lets try experiment with horizontal... should ideally load up as a pop up"). **A CSS-only modal was attempted first and abandoned**: `<details id="custom-quiz-section">` lives inside `#page-setup`, whose page-nav system puts a `transform` on the active `.page` — that makes #page-setup the containing block for `position:fixed`, so the modal sized/centred against the ~2800px page not the viewport (clipped, off-screen). A `:has()` transform-neutralise got further but the diagnostics kept contradicting (the cert-app SW re-registers + re-caches `dg-system.css` every localhost load — the documented stale-CSS gotcha — so each probe saw a different CSS state). Founder picked the robust path: **JS portal**. **app.js**: `_jumpToCustomQuiz()` drops the now-pointless `scrollIntoView` (keeps `cq.open=true`); a new `_cqModalInit` IIFE listens for the `<details>` `toggle` event (fires for EVERY open/close path — Configure→ / `drillTopic`@2771 / the 5804 flow / native `<summary>` × / Esc / backdrop) and on open stashes a placeholder comment + `document.body.appendChild(cq)` (escapes the page-nav transform → `position:fixed` resolves to the viewport) + locks `<html>` overflow; on close restores cq to the exact original `.ch-wrap` spot + unlocks. DOM-readiness- and entry-point-agnostic (listeners on `document`, elements resolved at event time). **dg-system.css**: re-scoped the existing v4.99.x custom-quiz editorial block `#page-setup #custom-quiz-section` → `#custom-quiz-section` (45 occ, replace_all — the section is unique by id; works whether in #page-setup OR portaled to `<body>`); colour tokens are global on `html[data-theme]` so they inherit on `<body>`, but the `--t-1..--t4`/`--dgh-ease` type-scale vars live on `#page-setup` so they were **re-declared on `#custom-quiz-section`** AND the modal motion was switched to a **literal `cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1)`** (a portaled element can't depend on a `var()` defined on a non-ancestor — `var(--dgh-ease)` was unresolving and invalidating the whole `transition`, classic `all 0s` fallback). Modal CSS: closed `display:none` (no longer on the page); `[open]` = fixed full-viewport flex backdrop + blur; `<summary>` header (Fraunces, × ::after) + `.custom-quiz-body` panel as one card; **`#topic-group` → 2-col domain grid** (horizontal, was a tall stack); `.cq-options-grid` → **2 equal full-width columns** (founder follow-up: DIFFICULTY/QUESTIONS were "squeezed together" bunched left → `grid 1fr 1fr` + generous gap + `#diff-group .chip flex:1 1 calc(50%-5px)` + `#count-group .chip-count flex:1 1 0`); `.cq-summary-bar` sticky footer. **emil-design-eng** (founder: "i see zero animation here or hover or button movement"): the v4.99.x rebrand had set `transform:none` on these for editorial calm — restored tactile feedback inside the modal (a focused configurator you actively click): `cqModalPanel` entrance scale(0.96)→1 + opacity (never scale(0), transform-origin centre — modals stay centred), `cqModalFade` backdrop; chips/cards/pills/CTA/domain-summaries get crisp hover (fine-pointer-gated) + `:active{scale(0.97)}` press feedback, scoped `html[data-theme] body #…[open]` to outrank the rebrand's `transform:none` without `!important`; transform/opacity only, reduced-motion neutralised. **Contract byte-exact**: `<details id="custom-quiz-section">` + every internal id (`#topic-group`/`#diff-group`/`#count-group`/`#cq-summary-bar`/`#cq-summary-prose`) / `.cq-*` / `.chip*` / `.cq-mode-card` / `.topic-domain-group[data-domain-idx]` / `.tdp-pill` / `data-v` / `prefillDomainTopics`/`startQuiz` onclicks preserved; **runtime reparent only, index.html source unchanged** → UAT guards 1876/1877-1883 (`indexOf` child-ordering in SOURCE) + 425/3430/3440 (`data-v`) + 5027/6285/6307 (cq-summary, read styles.css — untouched) + 1896/9937 (`_jumpToCustomQuiz` fn + `custom-quiz-section` string) + the **10182 cq-collapsed tombstone (which explicitly says "the right path is a modal")** all stay green. `_renderTopicChipsForActiveCert` untouched → Sec+ cert-aware swap still works inside the modal (verified live: 39 SY0-701 chips). No UAT migration. **Local-verified** on the real cert-app (after SW-unregister + cache-clear, the documented localhost technique): portal opens viewport-centred (`detailsBox 1346×966 @ 0,0`, `isViewportModal:true`, body fully in viewport), 2-col domain grid + spread DIFFICULTY/QUESTIONS, Esc/backdrop/× all close → restore to `.ch-wrap` + unlock scroll + remove placeholder, `display:none` closed, page scrollable, clean console, faithful dark + light. UAT **6372/6372**, dg-system.css braces 2437/2437. Mandatory `dg-system.css?v` cache-bust 5.5.3→5.5.4. The CSS-only-attempt cul-de-sac + the SW-stale-cache-makes-localhost-verification-unreliable lesson re-confirmed: portal is the correct pattern for any modal inside a transform-driven page system; prod is the reliable verification surface (every deploy bumps CACHE_NAME). Fast-lane per ENVIRONMENT_STRATEGY (HTML/CSS/JS; no DB/auth/money; sw.js = universal CACHE_NAME bump) → canonical trunk push, CI-gated. Completes the Home redesign: Sections 1 (masthead) + 2+3 (daily cards + horizontal session board) shipped v5.5.2/5.5.3; Section 4 founder-approved as-is; Section 5 this ship. |

Fix = the same full-width treatment v5.4.0 gave the By-Domain matrix. **DOM move** (`index.html`): `#modes-ladder` cut out of `.col-side`, the column now closes cleanly after `#daily-challenge-card`, and `#modes-ladder` re-added as a full-width `.ch-wrap` sibling after `.home-grid` (before `#domain-grid-section`) — internals restructured into a `.dgh-board` (3 commitment tiers Quick→Practice→Exam as a left→right effort ladder), a `.dgh-dbd-wrap` 5-up Drill-by-domain strip, and a `.dgh-foot` (Strict Mode + Custom Quiz). **Every contract preserved byte-exact**: all session-launch onclicks (`applyPreset`/`applyDomainPreset`/`startExam`/`startDailyChallenge`/`startWrongDrill`/`_jumpToCustomQuiz`), the ids (`#modes-dc-tile`/`#modes-dc-sub`/`#modes-wrong-tile`/`#modes-wrong-sub`/`#modes-strict-checkbox` + strict→`hardcore-checkbox` onchange wiring), the 5 `.dgh-db[data-domain-idx]` + `.dbk`/`.dbn`/`.dbm` cert-aware contract (`_renderTopicChipsForActiveCert` untouched, verified live still swapping SY0-701 domains on the moved buttons), exactly 3 `.dgh-grp` + the 3 verbatim tier strings (`Quick &middot; 3-5 min` / `Practice &middot; 10-30 min` / `Exam simulation &middot; 60-90 min`), `col-side-card` class dropped; `app.js` position-free (`getElementById`) so zero render-logic change (proven by the v5.4.0 domain-grid move). **stop-slop pass** on the static session copy (changeable in index.html): `.dgh-sub` "Start where your time and energy are right now" → "A quick habit, focused study, or a full exam. Pick what fits the time you have." (kills the pull-quote, names the three); "Daily habit · quick wins" → "· keeps your streak"; "Focused study · build understanding" → "· fix your weak spots" (jargon→concrete mechanics); rubric 30/50→44/50. The Today's-Plan/Daily-Challenge render-fn copy (`renderTodayPlan`/`renderDailyChallengeCard` in app.js) is a guard-safe follow-up copy pass (no UAT asserts those strings; flagged, not silently scoped in). **CSS** (`dg-system.css`, scoped `#page-setup #modes-ladder` — id-pair specificity (2,1,0) beats the base `html[data-theme] body #page-setup .dgh-*` without `!important`): full-width board container, `.dgh-board` 3-col grid w/ hairline dividers, `.dgh-grp` columns, `.dgh-opts` flex-column (overrides base `repeat(3,1fr)`), `.dgh-opt` editorial rows, `.dgh-dbd-wrap`/`.dgh-dbd` 5-up carded strip, `.dgh-foot` bar, Fraunces section title; locked bronze + app tokens; emil motion (hover **gated `(hover:hover) and (pointer:fine)`** = slide+accent on opts, lift on domain cards; `:active` press-scale; entrance rides the page's existing `.reveal` IIFE; reduced-motion neutralised). The dead `.col-side .dgh-*` overrides now match nothing (left inert, zero risk). **Scope discipline**: Today's Plan + Daily Challenge restyled IN PLACE on their existing rebrand `.tplan-*`/`.dc-*` rules (NOT structurally relocated — moving them out of the just-shipped Section-1 col-main/col-side masthead grid would destabilise it for no functional gain; the mockup's action-row was illustrative). **UAT guard 9952 MIGRATED** (never bypassed) from "(#modes-ladder + .dgh-sess in .col-side[col-side-card])" → asserts the new full-width board (`.dgh-sess` + `#modes-ladder` + `.dgh-board` + NOT `col-side-card` + ordered `#daily-challenge-card` < `#modes-ladder` < `#domain-grid-section`) — equal regression strength + tombstones the old vertical-rail placement. Self-caught: the migrated guard first failed on the *integration comment* containing the literal `col-side-card` (same class-of-bug-grep lesson as the `:root`-in-comment case — reworded the comment, not weakened the guard). Guards 9954 (3 tier strings) / 12021 (5 `.dgh-db`) / 529 / 9955 / 9956 / 10107 / 10109 / 1840 / 5665 all stay green (structure/ids/strings preserved). index.html `<div>` balance 799/799; dg-system.css braces 2393/2393. **UAT 6372/6372**. Local-verified on the real cert-app (styles.css + dg-system.css loaded): `inColSideRail:false`, board `grid-template-columns: 217.7px ×3`, 3 tiers, 5 cert-aware domain btns, 10 wired opts, strict wired, Fraunces title, clean console, faithful light + dark. Mandatory `dg-system.css?v` cache-bust 5.5.2→5.5.3 (stale-CSS gotcha). | v5.5.2 | Home **Section 1 (masthead)** v3 reimagining — first section of a founder-directed section-by-section full reimagining of `#page-setup` (the cert-app Home / codex-home) in the locked forged-bronze editorial system, routed taste-skill → **dashboards** + stop-slop + **emil-design-eng** (the real Kowalski skill, cloned from the founder-supplied `github.com/emilkowalski/skill.git`, byte-identical to the on-disk copy) + the icon folder. Founder picked section-by-section + full-reimagining + **add Fraunces** to the cert-app (a deliberate reversal of the prior cert-app "keep the font" constraint — surfaced via AskUserQuestion as a shape-of-solution fork before any code, since the cert-app had **0** Fraunces and the whole prior cert-app rebrand was app-font-only; guessing wrong would have meant redoing all 5 sections in the wrong face). Concept-mockup-first (`mockups/home-masthead-v3-concept.html`, verified light/dark + both certs, founder-approved "sign off and integrate"). **The reimagining**: the cert status banners → one refined centered status pill (bronze icon chip); the hero → editorial voice (date eyebrow + drawn rule, Fraunces serif display greeting w/ accent name, coach lede w/ accent-underlined emphasis); Exam Readiness → a measured **instrument** (huge Fraunces score, gradient+radial-tint bespoke card, the `.readiness-bar-mark` carries a labelled "PASS 720" notch, tier pill + probability + delta + trajectory as instrument lines); the two stat cards → ONE de-carded divided strip (dashboards anti-slop: no metric-card spam); Next Best Move → the one focal accent action card (Fraunces title, radial accent, icon eyebrow). emil motion: the v5.4.0 reveal-IIFE / hover-lift / bar-fill-width-transition infra is already emil-compliant and was preserved; the one genuinely-missing piece added = button **`:active{transform:scale(0.97)}`** press feedback (rides the existing `transform .16s` transition + reduced-motion gate). **INTEGRATION (surgical, scoped)**: edits ONLY in `dg-system.css` scoped `#page-setup` masthead component rules (banners/hero/card/stat/diag/buttons/readiness/nbm/sr) — every render-fn id + onclick contract + the `.reveal` IIFE + the **`[data-cert]` banner visibility gate** preserved EXACTLY (the gate has a hard-won warning comment; verified 3/3); app token convention kept (`var(--bg/--surface/--border/--text/--text-mid/--text-dim/--accent/--green/--red/--yellow)` + `--t*` scale + `--dgh-ease`); `html[data-theme] body #page-setup` load-bearing specificity kept; zero `:root`; **zero index.html DOM / app.js / styles.css change** (the only index.html edit = additive `<head>` Fraunces `<link>` + the mandatory `dg-system.css?v` cache-bust 4.99.80→5.5.2 — the documented stale-CSS gotcha). **Decisive UAT finding**: no UAT guard reads `dg-system.css` (UAT reads only `html`=index.html, `js`=app.js, `css`=styles.css); the v4.54.0 CSS guards grep **styles.css** (untouched) for the legacy `.setup-hero-v2`/`.rc-v2-*` classes (still present, dead-but-kept) and the codex-home guards are HTML-structure on the unchanged DOM → a dg-system.css-only scoped reskin trips **NO guard, NO migration** (same clean pattern proven on analytics v3). **UAT 6372/6372**. Local-verified on the real cert-app (styles.css + dg-system.css both loaded — the bleed trap): Fraunces actually loads + applies (`document.fonts.check`=true, hero/score computed `Fraunces, Georgia, serif`), clean console, reveal fires, no bleed, faithful light + dark + cert-gate. Post-integration founder enlarge-experiment (hero/instrument scaled ~18%) was tried then fully reverted on founder call ("back to the original") — the scale-up round-tripped to zero, masthead values confirmed back at the approved originals. | v5.5.1 | Cross-cert analytics v3 redesign — landing `/analytics` ("The Transfer Ledger"). The v4.99.93 v2 reskin was founder-rejected ("this doesnt look good") after viewing the LIVE Skill-overlap panel: it read as a generic flat-row accordion + grey 2-col detail dump (the dashboards anti-slop failure — "a dashboard made only of repetitive rows, no data role"). Full ground-up redesign via taste-skill → **dashboards** + stop-slop + emil + the icon folder, concept-mockup-first (`mockups/cross-cert-analytics-v3-concept.html`, built + verified light/dark, founder approved "This is perfecto. good to integrate"). **The redesign**: the page's subject IS movement between certs, so it uses the dashboards "push further" levers instead of repeating metric rows. **Panel 1 "Where you stand"** — a standings chart: every cert plotted against ONE shared pass-cutoff marker, active cert the protagonist (bronze accent-bar + faint tint + Fraunces readiness figure + gap-to-pass), passed quiet-green/settled, soon ghosted. **Panel 2 "How your knowledge transfers"** (the rejected panel, rebuilt) — a transfer map: two cert NODES joined by a drawn conduit, the % as a large Fraunces protagonist figure that owns the row; expanded = a knowledge ledger (what already carries = quiet green "the win", a refresher sub-band, the genuinely-new work = bronze/emphasised "the real focus"), prep-saved rendered as a confident Fraunces figure not a faint grey pill. **Panel 3 "Your next move"** — one bronze-edged focal recommendation + evidence reasons + the page's clearest CTA, then quiet numbered alternates. Locked forged-bronze system (Fraunces + Inter, scoped OKLCH, hairlines, one accent, one folder-icon per section head), dark + light. **INTEGRATION (surgical, scoped, CSS-only)**: replaced ONLY the `#analytics-page` rules inside `<style id="ccax-styles">` in `landing/analytics.html` with the verified v3 CSS; the shipped `#site-topbar` block kept verbatim; **DOM + JS byte-for-byte unchanged** (the existing `<img src="assets/analytics/*.svg">` icon slots reused — CSS handles `img`); zero `:root` touch (zero-leak preserved); the CSS targets the EXACT `lib/cross-cert-analytics.js` injected contract (`.cca-pr-*` / `.cca-so-*` incl. `.cca-so-detail*`/`-callout`/`-saving-pill`/`-topic-pill{mastered,shared,refresher,new}` / `.cca-wn-*`). Collapse/expand uses the real JS contract (`row.classList.toggle('is-open')`) so it is pure CSS on `.cca-so-row.is-open`. **Mockup-audit caught + fixed 2 real bugs pre-ship**: (1) Panel-1 readiness score collided with the cutoff caption → replaced the "PASS" text label with a clean dot-headed cutoff marker (redundant with the legend anyway); (2) collapsed pairs showed their detail (styled `.is-open` but never hid the closed `.cca-so-detail`) → added the collapse rule on the exact JS-toggled class. **Local-verify on the REAL page (with `styles.css` + `dg-system.css` loaded, which the standalone mockup did NOT have — exactly the v4.99.93 WN-bleed trap) caught the prod-hotfixed bug class recurring**: `dg-system.css` still bleeds `.cca-wn-*` `color` (bare CTA `color` computed bronze, not cream); the text only rendered correctly because `-webkit-text-fill-color` happened to win. Per the class-of-bug-grep discipline, restored the proven v2 `!important` hardening (every WN colour forced + `#analytics-page .cca-wn-hero *{-webkit-text-fill-color:currentColor!important}` + explicit CTA `-webkit-text-fill-color:var(--on-accent)!important`) — re-verified: `ctaColor` forced cream `oklch(0.985 0.01 85)` on bronze, reasons `oklch(0.36 …)` / title `oklch(0.22 …)` correct contrast, clean console (0 JS errors), faithful in BOTH themes end-to-end (pagehead → standings → transfer map + ledger → what-next). **UAT 6372/6372 — NO migration** (CSS-only; all 4 analytics guards read the preserved `<section class="cca-panel" id="cca-panel-wn">` markup + untouched `cross-cert-analytics.js`; style-block braces 226/226 balanced; 10 contract ids ×1; `#site-topbar` preserved). Fast-lane per ENVIRONMENT_STRATEGY (landing-site CSS-only; no DB/auth/money; sw.js = universal per-deploy CACHE_NAME bump) → canonical trunk push, CI-gated. |
| v5.5.0 | Fix: session-picker "Drill by domain" buttons cert-aware. The 5 `.dgh-db` buttons inside `#modes-ladder` hardcoded Network+ domain names, weights, and `applyDomainPreset()` keys even when viewing Security+ mode. **Root cause**: `_renderTopicChipsForActiveCert()` section 3 targeted `.modes-domain-tile` elements — a DOM class that no longer exists (removed during the v5.4.0 codex-home rebuild). The actual buttons use `.dgh-db` with inner spans `.dbk`/`.dbn`/`.dbm`. **Fix**: replaced the dead `.modes-domain-tile` section with `.dgh-db` targeting that updates each button's weight text (`.dbk`), domain label (`.dbn`), onclick handler (`applyDomainPreset('CERT_DOMAIN_KEY')`), and title attribute — all reading from the cert-aware `CERT_PACK.domainWeights` / `CERT_PACK.domainLabels`. Security+ users now see their 5 SY0-701 domains (General Concepts / Threats & Vulnerabilities / Security Architecture / Security Operations / Governance & Compliance) with correct weights. Network+ unaffected (function is a no-op when `CURRENT_CERT === 'netplus'`). **UAT**: 3 guards MIGRATED (`.modes-domain-tile` → `.dgh-db`, `.mdt-num`/`.mdt-name`/`.mdt-meta` → `.dbk`/`.dbn`/`.dbm`) + 1 tombstone (`.modes-domain-tile` must not reappear in `_renderTopicChipsForActiveCert`). **6372/6372 ALL PASS**. Fast-lane (JS-only cert-awareness fix; no DB/auth/money; sw.js = universal CACHE_NAME bump). |

Founder constraint honoured verbatim: **the sidebar/topbar were NOT touched** (the mockup ships its own sidebar markup — ignored entirely; only the `#page-setup` content area rebuilt). **HTML** (`index.html`): the v4.99.65 `.dgh-*` single-column editorial band stack replaced with a `.ch-wrap > .home-grid (minmax(0,1fr) / 372px) > .col-main + .col-side` 2-column layout. Every render-fn id + onclick + the chip `data-v` contract preserved (~50 ids, audited): hero `#setup-hero-v2`/`#hero-v2-eyebrow`/`-display`/`-lede` → `.col-main`; readiness `#readiness-card-v2` + `#rc-v2-*` → `.col-side` (hero+readiness no longer DOM siblings — `getElementById` is position-free so zero app.js render-logic change, grep-confirmed app.js references **0** of the renamed `.dgh-*` classes); `#hero-v2-cta` (NBM) accent-filled card → `.col-side`; `#rc-v2-whatif`/`#rc-v2-whatif-row` → `.col-main` drill-chips; `#diagnostic-cta-card`/`#pass-plan-tile` (`.diagnostic-cta-title` kept for `_renderCertAwareCopy` Sec+ swap) → `.col-main`; `#today-plan` + `#daily-challenge-card` PROMOTED out of `#today-section` into `.col-main`/`.col-side` (renderTodayPlan/renderDailyChallengeCard toggle their own `.is-hidden` — position-free; `#today-section` kept as a hidden wrapper for the CSS-clipped `#daily-goal-card` + `#streak-defender` legacy stubs still written by finish()/submitExam()); `#sr-review-card` → `.col-side`; `#modes-ladder` (`.dgh-sess` internals preserved verbatim) re-housed as a `.col-side-card`; `#domain-grid-section`/`#setup-domain-grid` moved to a **full-width row below the grid** (founder critique "squashed together, cant even see it" — a 5-domain matrix trapped in the narrower `.col-main` clipped; root cause was `1fr`==`minmax(auto,1fr)` forcing max-content min-width → long domain names overflowed; fixed with `minmax(0,1fr)` + `overflow-wrap:break-word` + full width); legacy `.hero`/`#readiness-card`/`#marathon-section`/`#wrong-preset-*`/`#hardcore-checkbox` stubs all preserved + CSS-clipped; the `#rc-v2-whatif` stray `is-hidden` class removed (render toggles its `hidden` attr only — would have stuck it hidden). The long-standing stray `</div>` now legitimately closes `.ch-wrap` (page structurally balanced). **CSS** (`dg-system.css`, scoped `#page-setup`): the mockup's card/grid/hero/stat/drill/readiness/nbm/sr/challenge/custom-row system, every hardcoded mockup hex mapped onto the app OKLCH tokens (`var(--accent/--surface/--bg/--text/--text-mid/--text-dim/--border/--green/--red/--yellow)`) so dark/light + cert all keep working (verified live: dark "just works", 0 purple leak, accent-filled NBM card readable). Retained-untouched: visibility guards, legacy-clip stubs, banners, `.dgh-sess`/`.dgh-opt`/`.dgh-db` tile rules (modes-ladder kept verbatim; wide 3-/5-col grids collapsed to fit the 372px rail via `html[data-theme] body` specificity-correct overrides — base `.dgh-opt`/`.dgh-db` carry an `html[data-theme] body` prefix so the col-side overrides needed it too), `#today-plan .tplan-*`, `#daily-challenge-card .dc-*`, `#streak-defender .sd-*`, `#setup-domain-grid .dg-*`, custom-quiz interior. Specificity-correct card overrides (`#today-plan.plan-card` / `#daily-challenge-card.challenge-card` = (2,2,2) to beat the retained flat-band `#…` (2,1,2) rules). **Animation (taste-skill → dashboards: purposeful + restrained, anti-slop)**: (1) load — a staggered IntersectionObserver fade-up (`.reveal` + `--d`×80ms) added as an isolated inline IIFE in `index.html` (no app.js dep, reduced-motion bypass, **hard 1.6s safety net** so a conditionally-rendered card can never get stuck invisible if IO misses a display:none→shown transition; readiness count-up + bar-sweep deliberately left owned by `renderReadinessCardV2()` by id — no JS conflict); (2) hover/press micro-interactions — clickable cards lift + accent-border + soft shadow, NBM accent-glow, buttons translateY + press-scale, domain-cell column highlight, whatif-chip + session-tile emphasis; all transition-tokened on `var(--dgh-ease)` and fully neutralised by an expanded `prefers-reduced-motion` gate. **UAT**: 6 structural guards MIGRATED (never bypassed — assert the new codex-home structure with regression strength: daily-challenge/today-plan column promotion + `.challenge-card`/`.plan-card` presence + ordering, Marathon-stub + bulk30/45 presets, By-Domain header + `#setup-domain-grid`/`#domain-grid-section`, `#setup-hero-v2`+`#readiness-card-v2` split across `.col-main`/`.col-side`, `#modes-ladder` `.dgh-sess` in `.col-side-card`). **6371/6371 ALL PASS**. dg-system.css braces 2334/2334 balanced, 0 malformed cubic-bezier. **Chrome MCP live-verify (localhost:3131, the data-safety-approved local-server env — 0 prod `nplus_*` writes; the giant-logo blowup first seen was the documented stale-SW gotcha → unregistered SW + cleared `netplus-v5.3.0` cache → true render)**: Network+ light + dark + Security+ all verified end-to-end — codex-home 2-col layout faithful, sidebar untouched, cert-gated banner swap (Net+ pass-proof ↔ Sec+ private-builder), cert-aware copy ("Where do you stand on N10-009?/SY0-701?"), full-width By-Domain matrix renders 5 N10-009 AND 5 SY0-701 domains with clean word-wrap (no squash, no clip), hover lift live. KNOWN pre-existing (NOT a regression, NOT in scope — preserved verbatim from the prior `.dgh-sess`): the session-picker static "Drill by domain" buttons show Network+ labels in Sec+ mode (the cert-aware domain surface is the separate full-width `#setup-domain-grid`; the static `.dgh-db` row was never cert-aware) — flagged for a separate follow-up. Fast-lane per ENVIRONMENT_STRATEGY (HTML/CSS/UAT-only; no DB/auth/money; sw.js touch = the universal per-deploy CACHE_NAME bump, zero fetch-logic change) → canonical trunk push, CI-gated. |
| v5.3.0 | TB V2 Ship #8 — Coach mode. AI topology review wired into V2's modebar. **V1 bridge** (`features/topology-builder.js`): `window.tbV2CoachTopology()` async bridge — validates (devices > 0, not pristine scenario, API key required), checks `TB_COACH_CACHE` via shared `tbTopologyHash` + `tbLoadCoachCache` + `tbSaveCoachCache` (interchangeable with V1's cache), constructs prompt (two paths: lab-aware tutor with step N/M context vs free-build reviewer), calls `_claudeFetch` (Sonnet Tier C), parses JSON payload `{tour, strengths[], concerns[], upgrades[], objectives[], studyTip}`, caches + returns `{status: 'cached'|'success'|'error', payload?, error?, scenario?}`. Prompt duplicated in bridge (strangler-fig — V1's working `tbCoachTopology()` untouched). **V2 Coach UI** (`features/topology-builder-v2.js`): `_showCoachUI()` renders right-side 380px slide-in panel, calls `_ensureEngine()` then `_runCoachAnalysis()` with `_activeMode !== 'coach'` race guard. `_renderCoachPanel()` builds editorial panel (eyebrow "AI review" + title "Topology Coach" + close button + scrollable body). `_runCoachAnalysis()` calls `tbV2CoachTopology()` → loading state → error rendering with retry button OR result rendering with 6 sections (tour summary, strengths=green, concerns=yellow, upgrades=accent, objectives list, study tip accent-tinted card). `_hideCoachUI()` removes panel + `.v2-coach-visible` class. `_setMode('coach')` wired to `_showCoachUI/_hideCoachUI`. `_updateStatus()` handles `coach` branch. `exit()` lifecycle calls `_hideCoachUI()`. **CSS** (`features/topology-builder-v2.css`): `.v2-coach-panel` absolute right-anchored 380px, `translateX(100%)` → `translateX(0)` on `.v2-coach-visible` with .2s cubic-bezier transition. `.v2-cp-head` flex header (eyebrow + title + 28px close). `.v2-cp-body` flex:1 scrollable. `.v2-cp-loading` + `.v2-cp-spinner` centered with `v2CpSpin` keyframe. `.v2-cp-error` centered with retry button (accent border → accent bg on hover). `.v2-cp-section` sections with uppercase dim headers + hairline borders. Semantic section colors: `.v2-cp-good` (green), `.v2-cp-warn` (yellow), `.v2-cp-upgrade` (accent). `.v2-cp-tip` accent-tinted card via `color-mix(in oklab, var(--accent) 8%, transparent)`. Reduced-motion gate for panel, spinner, buttons. UAT: 13 new guards (V1 bridge + validation + cache sharing + V2 lifecycle + panel render + mode guard + result rendering + _setMode wiring + exit lifecycle + CSS panel/loading/error/tip + slide-in transition + reduced-motion). Fast-lane (feature module changes only; no DB/auth/money; sw.js = universal CACHE_NAME bump). |
| v5.2.0 | TB V2 Ship #7 — 3D View mode. Immersive Three.js canvas in V2's modebar. **V1 bridge** (`features/topology-builder.js`): `window.tbV2Open3DView(opts)` — async, lazy-imports `/tb3d.js` on first call (caches on `window._tbV2_3dModule`), calls `_tb3dModule.enter(tbState, opts)`; `window.tbV2Close3DView()` — calls `_tb3dModule.exit()`. **V2 3D show/hide** (`features/topology-builder-v2.js`): `_showThreedUI()` creates `#tbv2-3d-overlay` positioned over V2's canvas, temporarily moves `#tb-3d-host` from V1's hidden page into the overlay (so tb3d.js's `clientWidth/Height` measurements are non-zero), saves `_3dHostOrigParent` for restoration. V2's own `.v2-3d-chrome` provides "← Back to Design" button + "3D Explorer" eyebrow (V1's chrome hidden via CSS). After overlay mounts, calls `_ensureEngine()` then `tbV2Open3DView()` with overlay-existence guard against race condition. `_hideThreedUI()` calls `tbV2Close3DView()`, restores `#tb-3d-host` to `_3dHostOrigParent` with `hidden=true`, removes overlay. `_setMode('threed')` wired to `_showThreedUI/_hideThreedUI`. `_updateStatus()` handles `threed` branch. `exit()` lifecycle hook now calls `_hideThreedUI()` + exits any active lab (previously empty). **CSS** (`features/topology-builder-v2.css`): `.v2-3d-overlay` (absolute inset flex-column z-30), `.v2-3d-chrome` (44px flex bar), `.v2-3d-overlay #tb-3d-host` overrides V1's `position:absolute;inset:0` → `position:relative;flex:1` so it flows below V2's chrome; `.v2-3d-overlay .tb-3d-chrome { display:none!important }` hides V1's chrome; `.v2-3d-overlay .tb-3d-canvas,.tb-3d-labels { top:0!important }` removes V1's 52px chrome offset; `.v2-3d-loading` centered loading text; `.v2-3d-back` hairline button with accent hover; reduced-motion gate. UAT: 12 new guards (V1 bridge + V2 show/hide + _setMode wiring + _3dHostOrigParent + CSS rules + V1-chrome-hidden + canvas-top-override + exit lifecycle). Fast-lane (feature module changes only; no DB/auth/money; sw.js = universal CACHE_NAME bump). |
