V1 lab bridge (tbV2GetLabCategories / tbV2GetAllLabs / tbV2GetActiveLab / tbV2StartLab / tbV2LabNext / tbV2LabSkip / tbV2LabHint / tbV2ExitLab). V2 picker filtered by category with All tab, step panel with progress dots, animated progress bar, inline hint toggle, inline check feedback, Skip + Next/Finish buttons. Completion card with time/steps/hints stats. Device highlight pulse on labs-mode canvas (v2-lab-target). CSS: picker overlay, step panel, completion card, reduced-motion gate. |
| v5.0.9 | HOTFIX — CSS parse failure: two truncated `cubic-bezier()` calls in `styles.css` silenced ~3,300 rules and broke all 9 Playwright 3D View tests. **Root cause**: `.acl-header-pill` (line 6150) and `.acl-tp-row` (line 6447) both had malformed `transition` values — `cubic-bezier(0.2,` with no closing `)`, just a trailing comma before the next property. The CSS parser kept consuming everything after each unclosed `(` as part of the function argument list, swallowing rule closers, selectors, and property declarations for the rest of the file. Among the casualties: `.tb-canvas-pills { position: absolute }`, all `.tb-pill` rules, palette/collapse-button positioning — everything the 3D View modebar needs to float above the canvas. Playwright found palette elements (`#tb-palette-collapse-btn`, `.tb-pane-rail-label`) intercepting pointer events at the 3D pill coordinates because the pill was `position: static` and `overflow: hidden`-clipped rather than floating. **Why brace-balance was misleading**: awk counts `{` and `}` as characters — balanced overall — but CSS parsing is function-context-aware; an unclosed `(` swallows `}` tokens as arguments. **Fix**: two lines changed — replaced `cubic-bezier(0.2,` truncated values with `cubic-bezier(0.2, 0.8, 0.2, 1)` (the standard ease-out curve used throughout the codebase). **Verified**: styles.css went from 2,656 parsed rules → 5,956/5,956. Playwright 3D View suite: 9 failing → 11/11 pass. Full suite: 101/101 Playwright chromium + 6334/6334 UAT. Temp diagnostic files `debug-positions.spec.js` + `debug-css-parse.spec.js` deleted. Fast-lane (two-line CSS fix + SW cache bump). |
| v5.0.8 | TB V2 Ship #5 — Trace mode initiation panel. Full trace setup UI matching the locked `mockups/network-builder-modes-concept.html`. **V1 trace bridge** (`features/topology-builder.js`): 7 new window-exposed functions — `window.tbV2ComputeTrace(srcDeviceId, dstIp, maxTtl)` wraps internal `tbComputeTrace(tbState, ...)`, `window.tbV2StartTrace(srcId, dstIp)` wraps `tbStartTrace`, `window.tbV2EndTrace` = direct ref to `tbEndTrace`, `window.tbV2TracePlay`/`tbV2TracePause`/`tbV2TraceStep` = direct refs to playback controls, `window.tbV2GetTraceState()` reads `_tbUiState.trace`. These bypass V1's `tbOpenTraceDialog` which uses browser `prompt()` for destination IP — unusable from V2. **V2 trace initiation panel** (`features/topology-builder-v2.js`): `_renderTracePanel()` builds a right-side 320px slide-in panel (`.v2-trace-init`) with editorial head ("Packet trace / Visualize network path"), source + destination device pickers as visual cards (icon + hostname + IP) via `_buildDevicePickerHtml(devices, selectedIndex)` — renders a `.v2-ti-pick` card with device SVG icon from `tbDeviceIcon`, hostname, IP address, and a hidden `<select>` overlay for accessibility/click-through (same device dropdown data as simulate but with a richer visual presentation). `_getDevicesWithIp()` filters `tbGetState().devices` to only those with configured IP addresses. Protocol toggle as a segmented `.v2-ti-type-row` with 4 options (Ping/Traceroute/HTTP/DNS) tracked via `_traceProtocol` state variable, `.on` class toggles active. Footer with Cancel (returns to design mode) + Begin Trace (accent primary CTA). `_wireTracePanel()` event delegation: device picker `<select>` changes update the visual card, protocol type clicks toggle `_traceProtocol`, Cancel calls `_setMode('design')`, Begin fires trace initiation via V1 bridge. `_showTraceUI()` / `_hideTraceUI()` manage panel lifecycle with `.v2-trace-visible` class for CSS slide-in transition. `_setMode()` updated: shows trace UI on trace mode enter, hides on any other mode. `_updateStatus()` updated: handles trace mode contextual label. **CSS** (`features/topology-builder-v2.css`): `.v2-trace-init` right-anchored 320px panel with `translateX(100%) → translateX(0)` slide-in via `.v2-trace-visible`, `.v2-ti-head` editorial (eyebrow + title + subtitle), `.v2-ti-pick` device picker card (hairline bordered, 48px icon area + name/IP stack + hidden select overlay + dropdown arrow), `.v2-ti-type-row` segmented protocol toggle (hairline bordered pills, `.on` = accent bg), `.v2-ti-foot` footer with ghost cancel + accent begin CTA, `.v2-ti-arrow` chevron indicator. 11 UAT guards (V1 trace bridge functions + trace panel render/wire + show/hide + device picker + mode wiring + protocol toggle + CSS). UAT **6334/6334**. Fast-lane (feature module changes only; no DB/auth/money; sw.js = universal CACHE_NAME bump). |
| v5.0.7 | TB V2 Ship #4 — Simulate mode panel. Full simulation dialog + log panel matching the locked `mockups/network-builder-modes-concept.html`. **V1 simulation bridge** (`features/topology-builder.js`): 4 new window-exposed functions — `window.tbV2SimPing(srcDeviceId, dstIp)` wraps internal `tbSimPing(tbState, ...)`, `window.tbV2SimARP(srcDeviceId, targetIp)` wraps `tbSimARP`, `window.tbV2SimDHCP(clientDeviceId)` wraps `tbSimDHCP`, `window.tbV2AnimatePacket` = direct ref to `tbAnimatePacket`. These accept programmatic args (V1's `tbExecPing`/`tbExecArp` read from V1's own modal inputs — unusable from V2). **V2 simulate dialog** (`features/topology-builder-v2.js`): `_renderSimDialog()` builds a centered overlay card (380px) with "Network simulation / Test connectivity" editorial head, source/destination device `<select>` dropdowns auto-populated from `tbGetState().devices` via `_buildDeviceOptions()`, protocol action buttons (Ping primary + ARP request + DHCP discover — each with mockup SVG icons). `_renderSimLog()` builds a right-anchored (340px, top:50px) log panel with timestamped mono entries: `_appendLogEntry(protocol, message, status)` creates `.v2-sim-entry` divs with `.v2-sim-ts` timestamps via `_simTimestamp()` (elapsed from sim start), color-coded protocol badges (`.v2-sim-ok` green / `.v2-sim-fail` red / `.v2-sim-arp` blue / `.v2-sim-dhcp` purple / `.v2-sim-info` accent), auto-scroll to bottom. `_parseSimLog(logLines, protocol)` parses V1 result.log arrays into individual entries with auto-detected status (regex for unreachable/fail/dropped → fail). Protocol handlers: `_runPing()` reads src/dst selects, validates (same-device guard, no-IP guard), calls `tbV2SimPing`, parses log, triggers `tbV2AnimatePacket` with success/fail color; `_runARP()` calls `tbV2SimARP` + blue packet animation; `_runDHCP()` calls `tbV2SimDHCP` (source-only, no destination needed). `_clearSimLog()` resets entries + timer. `_showSimulateUI()` / `_hideSimulateUI()` manage lifecycle; `_setMode()` updated to call show on simulate enter, hide on any other mode. `_wireSimDialog()` event delegation: protocol buttons via `[data-proto]`, close button returns to design mode, overlay click-outside dismisses, Clear button. **CSS** (`features/topology-builder-v2.css`): `.v2-sim-overlay` centered flex with translucent bg + fade transition, `.v2-sim-card` 14px radius hairline card with scale entrance, editorial `.v2-sim-head` (eyebrow + title + 30px close box), `.v2-sim-select` custom dropdown (appearance:none + chevron bg-image + accent focus ring), `.v2-sim-btn` hairline bordered + `.primary` accent fill, `.v2-sim-log` absolute-positioned right panel with slide-in transform, `.v2-sim-entry` mono font with hairline dividers. 12 UAT guards (V1 bridge sim functions + dialog/log render + protocol handlers + mode wiring + CSS). UAT **6323/6323**. Fast-lane (feature module changes only; no DB/auth/money; sw.js = universal CACHE_NAME bump). |
| v5.0.6 | HOTFIX — TB V2 page load failure on prod. **Root cause**: `showPage('topology-builder-v2')` calls `document.getElementById('page-topology-builder-v2')` but the HTML element was `id="page-topology-v2"` → returned `null` → `next.classList.add('active')` threw TypeError → caught by `window.onerror` → "Something went wrong" toast. The sidebar/topbar were NOT hidden because the CSS selector `html:has(#page-topology-builder-v2.page.active)` never matched (element ID was wrong AND `.active` was never added). **Fix**: renamed 104 references from `page-topology-v2` to `page-topology-builder-v2` across 3 files: `index.html` (1 element ID), `features/topology-builder-v2.css` (101 `#page-topology-v2` selectors via replace_all), `features/topology-builder-v2.js` (2 getElementById/querySelector references). Fast-lane hotfix (no DB/auth/money; sw.js = universal CACHE_NAME bump). |
| v5.0.5 | TB V2 Ship #3 — Device interaction. Full design-mode interaction layer wired to V1 engine via mutation bridge functions. **V1 mutation bridge** (`features/topology-builder.js`): 9 new window-exposed functions — `window.tbAddDevice`, `window.tbAddCable`, `window.tbDeleteSelected`, `window.tbSaveDraft`, `window.tbOpenConfigPanel` (direct fn refs), `window.tbSetSelectedId(id)`, `window.tbSetPendingCableFrom(id)`, `window.tbSetSelectedCableType(type)`, `window.tbGetSelectedCableType()` (wrapper fns). V2 calls these for all state mutations, never writes `tbState` directly. **V2 interaction handlers** (`features/topology-builder-v2.js`): `_clientToSvg(svg, clientX, clientY)` — SVG coordinate transform matching V1's `tbClientToSvg` (clamped to canvas bounds). `_onDeviceMouseDown(e)` — event delegation via `[data-v2-device]` attr: double-click detection (manual, DOUBLE_CLICK_MS=400, same pattern as V1 — DOM recreation kills native dblclick) opens config panel via `tbOpenConfigPanel`; cable wiring completion (if `tbPendingCableFrom` set and different device, syncs cable type via `tbSetSelectedCableType` then calls `tbAddCable`); otherwise starts drag (captures SVG offset, sets `_dragging` state, selects device via `tbSetSelectedId`). `_onMouseMove(e)` — drag handler: reads `_dragging.id`, finds device in `tbState.devices`, updates `dev.x`/`dev.y` with clamped position (55..TB_CANVAS_W-55, 45..TB_CANVAS_H-45), sets `_dragging.moved` flag on >3px delta, re-renders. `_onMouseUp(e)` — if `_dragging.moved` saves draft via `tbSaveDraft`; if not moved toggles cable pending mode (click same device again cancels, otherwise sets `tbPendingCableFrom`). `_onCanvasClick(e)` — deselects on empty canvas area (guards against device/cable targets). `_onCableClick(e)` — selects cable via `[data-v2-cable]` hitbox click. `_onPaletteDeviceClick(e)` — adds device via `tbAddDevice` at staggered center position (avoids stacking: offset by device count modulo). `_onCableChipClick(e)` — toggles cable type selection + syncs to V1 via `tbSetSelectedCableType`. `_attachKeyHandler()` — Delete/Backspace deletes selected via `tbDeleteSelected`, Escape clears selection + pending cable (guards against input/textarea/select focus). `_wireInteraction()` — master wiring fn called after engine load: SVG mousedown delegation, window mousemove/mouseup for drag, SVG click for deselect/cable-select, palette click delegation. `_updateStatus(msg)` — updates mode label with contextual feedback. **CSS** (`features/topology-builder-v2.css`): `.v2-device` `user-select:none` + `:active` cursor `grabbing`. 12 UAT guards (interaction handlers + V1 bridge mutations + state setters + double-click pattern). UAT **6311/6311**. Fast-lane (feature module changes only; no DB/auth/money; sw.js = universal CACHE_NAME bump). |
| v5.0.4 | TB V2 Ship #2 — Design mode canvas rendering wired to V1 engine. V2 now reads `tbState` via bridge getters and draws devices + cables on its own SVG canvas. **V1 bridge getters added** (`features/topology-builder.js`): `window.tbGetState()`, `window.tbGetSelectedId()`, `window.tbGetPendingCableFrom()`, `window.tbGetDeviceTypes()`, `window.tbGetCableTypes()`, `window.tbDeviceIcon` — read-only access to V1 engine state so V2 never duplicates or mutates directly. **V2 canvas render** (`features/topology-builder-v2.js`): `_edgePoint()` cable edge-point calculation (matches V1's `tbEdgePoint` — cables connect at device rect perimeter, not center), `_renderCanvas()` main render loop reads `tbState.devices` + `tbState.cables` and draws full SVG: cables as 3-layer paths (dark sheath shadow + colored conductor per `TB_CABLE_TYPES` + 20px transparent hitbox), devices as `<g>` groups (96x72 rounded rect bg + 0.72x scaled icon via `tbDeviceIcon` + hostname label + health badge), cable health status (healthy=green when both ends have IPs, partial=yellow, unconfig=red), device selection + pending-cable visual states. `_ensureEngine()` lazy-loads V1 module on V2 enter so engine is always available. `window.tbv2RenderCanvas` exposed for external re-render trigger. Device count pill (`#tbv2-device-count`) updates on every render. Empty state auto-hides when devices exist. **CSS** (`features/topology-builder-v2.css`): `.v2-device` cursor/hover/selected/pending states (accent glow via `drop-shadow` + `color-mix`), `.v2-cable-healthy`/`.v2-cable-partial`/`.v2-cable-selected` status overrides using dg tokens, SVG layer pointer-events. UAT **6299/6299**. Fast-lane (feature module changes only; no DB/auth/money; sw.js = universal CACHE_NAME bump). |
| v5.0.3 | Topology Builder V2 shell — **strangler-fig pattern**: new `#page-topology-v2` page alongside existing V1 TB. Ground-up rebuild to the locked mockups (`network-builder-revamp-concept.html` canvas bible + `network-builder-modes-concept.html` modes bible). Ship #1 of the incremental V2 build — visual shell only, engine wiring follows in Ships #2–#8. **4 files created/modified:** (1) `features/topology-builder-v2.js` — IIFE module following `_loadFeature` contract, CSS injection via `_ensureCss()`, PALETTE data (4 categories: Infrastructure/Endpoints/Cloud with monoline SVG icons), MODES array (8 modebar pills: Design/Simulate/Trace/Labs/3D/Coach/Grade/Export with SVG icons from locked mockup), SCENARIO_GROUPS (Sandbox/Campus/WAN), `_renderPalette`/`_renderModebar`/`_renderScenarios`/`_renderEmptyState` builders, `_setMode` with mode-label descriptions, `enter()`/`exit()` lifecycle, palette search filter via event delegation, registers on `window._certanvilFeatures['topology-builder-v2']` + exposes `window.openTopologyBuilderV2`/`window.tbv2SetMode`. (2) `features/topology-builder-v2.css` — full layout CSS scoped under `#page-topology-v2`: full-viewport takeover via `html:has(#page-topology-v2.page.active)` hides sidebar/topbar, strip header, 3-column workspace (212px palette | 1fr canvas | 236px scenarios), floating modebar, device palette with categories + cable chips, scenario rail with groups, SVG canvas with grid pattern, empty-state card, mode panels container, reduced-motion gate, responsive collapse at 900px. Uses dg token system (`var(--accent)`, `var(--bg)`, etc.) — 0 hardcoded hex. (3) `index.html` — `#page-topology-v2` div inserted after existing `#page-topology-builder` (line 2625) with mockup-faithful DOM: strip header (eyebrow + title + count pill + theme toggle + back button), 3-col `.work` div (`.palette` aside with search + `#tbv2-palette-list` | `.canvas` main with `#tbv2-modebar` + `#tbv2-mode-label` + SVG canvas with grid pattern + `#tbv2-panels` | `.scenarios` aside with `#tbv2-scenarios`). (4) `app.js` — `_sbNavIcon` entry for `topology-builder-v2` (node-graph icon with vertical ticks), sidebar entry in `APP_SIDEBAR_PRACTICE_NETPLUS_TAIL` between Network Builder and ACL Builder: `{ page: 'topology-builder-v2', label: 'Builder V2', handler: async () => { await _loadFeature('topology-builder-v2'); showPage('topology-builder-v2'); ... } }`. **Architecture**: V2 has its own render layer (new files) but will call into the existing TB engine functions (window-exposed) for state management — no state duplication. V1 stays live as fallback until V2 is feature-complete, then V1 is removed (strangler fig). CSS scoped under `#page-topology-v2` avoids collision with V1's `tb-*` namespace. CSS injection from feature module (new pattern — V2 JS injects its own `<link>` tag). 1 UAT guard MIGRATED (v4.53.0 practice-nav gap widened 400→600 for V2 entry) + 1 new guard (V2 sidebar entry + `_loadFeature` call). UAT **6299/6299**. Playwright **chromium 90 passed**. Fast-lane (new feature files + additive HTML/JS; no DB/auth/money; sw.js = universal CACHE_NAME bump). |

0` → `0`; 3 tab buttons `&#9876; Practice` / `&#128218; Lessons` / `&#128202; Dashboard` → bare text (setAmmTab uses `b.id.replace('amm-tab-btn-', '')`, never label text); `#amm-streak` `&#128293; 0` → `0`. **APP.JS STRIPS (20):** `ammRenderQuestion()`: (a) `pair.icon || '⚔️'` emptied in `.amm-q-attack-icon` (CSS-hidden), (b) `'★'.repeat() + '☆'.repeat()'` replaced with `diffPips` — `Array.from({length:3})` generating `.amm-pip` + `.amm-pip.on` circles matching the mockup's `.pips i` pattern (the `pair.diff` integer drives the count, never the glyph text — display-only), (c) footer `Difficulty: ${diffStars}` → `Difficulty: <span class="amm-pips">${diffPips}</span>`, (d) 2× `'🔥 ' + ammStreak` → bare `ammStreak` on `#amm-streak` + `#amm-streak-badge`; `ammPickAnswer()`: (e) reveal head `'✅ Correct'` / `'❌ Why ...'` → `'Correct'` / `'Why ...'` (functional ✓/✗ pseudo-elements in CSS preserved — same precedent as CTS/IRW), (f) 2× streak emoji stripped; `ammEndSession()`: (g) `.amm-eos-burst` `'⚔️'` → empty (CSS-hidden), (h) sub text `'🔥 '` stripped, (i) weakest label `AMM_CATEGORIES[weakest].icon + ' '` → bare `.label` (the `_ctsLineIcon` precedent — data-level emoji, CSS-hidden icon span), (j) weak hint `'💡 '` stripped; `ammRenderLessons()`: (k) `cat.icon` emptied + inline `style="background:${cat.color}22;color:${cat.color}"` removed from `.amm-lesson-icon` (rainbow killed); `ammRenderDashboard()`: (l) `r.cat.icon` emptied in `.amm-dash-cat-icon` (CSS-hidden), (m) inline `background:${r.cat.color}` removed from `.amm-dash-cat-bar-fill` (rainbow bar → accent via CSS), (n) `'🔥'` streak flame emptied (CSS-hidden), (o) `weakestCat.cat.icon` stripped, (p) CTA `'⚔️ Start'` → `'Start'`. ALL verified DISPLAY-ONLY: tab switching uses `b.id` not label text; stat elements only SET (`textContent`), never READ/compared; options use `data-amm-opt` index + `ammPickAnswer(i)`, never glyph text; dashboard/lesson handlers use `startAttackMitigation()` / `setAmmTab('dashboard')` fixed functions. **STARS → PIPS (structural text change):** the mockup uses `.pips i` circles (7px filled/empty dots) for difficulty instead of ★☆ text. Replaced `diffStars` string construction with `diffPips` generating `<i class="amm-pip on">` elements. New CSS rules `.amm-pips` (inline-flex gap) + `.amm-pip` (7px circle, border color) + `.amm-pip.on` (accent fill) — exact match to mockup's `.pips` system. **INLINE RAINBOW KILLED:** `.amm-lesson-icon` inline `background/color` from `cat.color` removed (CSS-hidden span); `.amm-dash-cat-bar-fill` inline `background:${r.cat.color}` removed (replaced by `background:var(--accent)!important` in CSS batch — all bars now editorial bronze, not per-category rainbow). **CSS BATCH 4q (~120 rules):** COMPLETE scoped `#page-amm` editorial override covering all 6 states: page-level `max-width:760px` + de-carded `.ed-pagehead` (purple killed, bronze accent em); `.amm-streak-badge` hidden; tab bar with full-width rule divider + accent active state (purple `rgba(124,111,247,.18)` killed); scorebar with hairline border; question card de-carded to `.qwrap` (16px radius, hairline border); attack prompt card accent-tinted border `color-mix(in oklab,var(--accent) 38%,transparent)` + faint accent bg (red tint from old implementation mockup killed); `.amm-q-attack-icon` hidden; option grid hairline borders with accent hover; arrow cue `font-weight:400` editorial; reveal panel accent-tinted (not purple `rgba(91,79,219)`); bronze CTA buttons; end-of-session de-carded with `.amm-eos-burst` hidden; lessons de-carded with `.amm-lesson-icon` hidden + hairline pair tables (`.amm-pair-attack` red / `.amm-pair-mit` green kept — semantic, not decorative); dashboard de-carded with `.amm-dash-cat-icon` hidden + `.amm-dash-streak-flame` hidden + rainbow bar fill → accent + bronze CTA; reduced-motion gate. Functional verdict marks KEPT (✓/✗ via CSS `::before` on `.is-correct`/`.is-wrong` — same precedent as CTS/IRW/PHT). Scoped/zero render-LOGIC change — every onclick/id/handler preserved (`ammPickAnswer`/`ammNextQuestion`/`startAttackMitigation`/`setAmmTab` + all `amm-*` ids). dg-system.css braces 1610/1610 balanced. | v4.99.83 | Net+ Quiz E2E audit — completing the v4.99.68-70 editorial rebuild. Founder directive: "lets do the E2E Quiz flow for Network+ next… do not fail me." Audited all 3 Net+ quiz mockups (`netplus-quiz-concept.html`, `netplus-quiz-results-concept.html`, `netplus-quiz-loading-concept.html`) against production end-to-end. The CSS editorial rebuild (Batch 4d/4e/4f, 162 lines of scoped overrides) and the v4.99.82 pass-mark fix already cover structural styling + cert-awareness. This audit found and fixed **8 remaining display-only emoji strips** that CSS cannot hide (text-baked into JS/HTML): (1) `#live-streak` static HTML (index.html): `&#128293; 0` → `Streak 0` (mockup `.stat.streak` says "Streak 1"); (2-4) `#live-streak` JS updates in `updateScoreDisplay()`, `pick()`, `submitMultiSelect()`, `submitOrder()` (app.js, 4 sites total including static): `\u{1F525} ${streak}` → `Streak ${streak}` (handler uses `#live-streak`.textContent, never reads/compares — display-only); (5-7) `followUpOnMistake()` error/catch reset (app.js, 3 sites): `\u{1F3AF} Drill this concept` → `Drill this concept` (button innerHTML reset, never compared); (8) Review `Study:` link in `_buildReviewEntry()` (app.js): `📚 Study:` → `Study:` (the review path's resource button onclick uses `showTopicDeepDive(topic)`, never the glyph). 1 Playwright test MIGRATED: `toContainText('🔥 1')` → `toContainText('Streak 1')` in the "streak does not move on re-pick" revisit test (tracks the new editorial format). **VERIFIED ALREADY CLEAN**: `showExplanation()` main path (Study/Explain/Drill/Report buttons at lines 7483-7500 — all emoji-free); results page buttons (lines 905-910 — "Drill my mistakes"/"Review answers"/"New session" already bare text); loading page (v4.99.70 stripped `📚` from `#topic-brief h3`). **STRUCTURAL NOTE**: the mockup qbar includes a `.qtitle` (cert eyebrow "Network+ · N10-009 · quiz session" + bold topic) and a Theme toggle — these are part of the GLOBAL CHROME rebuild (affects all 30 pages, same pattern as the TB v4.99.74 full-viewport takeover) and belong to the separately-flagged sidebar/topbar surface, not a per-page fix. Pre-push double gate: `tests/uat.js` **6297/6297** AND Playwright **chromium 99 passed**. Fast-lane per ENVIRONMENT_STRATEGY (display-only emoji strips; no DB/auth/money; sw.js = universal CACHE_NAME bump). The Network+ quiz flow (loading → quiz → results) is now fully editorial end-to-end. |
| v4.99.82 | Sec+ Quiz E2E audit — cert-aware pass mark fix. Founder directive: "lets do the E2E Quiz flow for Security+ next… do not fail me." Audited all 3 Sec+ quiz mockups (`secplus-quiz-concept.html`, `secplus-quiz-results-concept.html`, `secplus-quiz-loading-concept.html`) against production. The CSS editorial rebuild from v4.99.68-70 (Quiz + Results + Loading) already covers the structural needs (Sec+ mockups explicitly mirror Net+ structurally). The `_renderCertAwareCopy()` function already swaps cert-specific display text (N10-009→SY0-701, pass-mark threshold text). **PRIMARY GAP FOUND + FIXED: hardcoded `720` pass mark in 9 consumer sites bypassing the cert-aware `EXAM_PASS_SCORE` constant** (defined correctly at app.js:104 as `CERT_PACK.meta.examPassScore || 720` — reads 750 for SY0-701, 720 for N10-009). This is a **functional bug for Sec+ users**: they would see "Pass mark cleared · 720" when their real pass is 750, and a student scoring 730 would see a false pass verdict. **Sites fixed**: (1) `finish()` line 7605: `const passed = scaled >= 720` → `scaled >= EXAM_PASS_SCORE` (quiz flow pass/fail verdict logic); (2) `finish()` line 7620: verdict innerHTML `'↑ Pass mark cleared · 720'` → `'↑ Pass mark cleared · ' + EXAM_PASS_SCORE`; (3) `finish()` line 7624: `const gap = 720 - scaled` → `EXAM_PASS_SCORE - scaled` (below-pass gap calculation); (4) `submitExam()` line 8205: `'above the 720 pass mark'` → `'above the ${EXAM_PASS_SCORE} pass mark'` (exam result message — note: `submitExam` already used `EXAM_PASS_SCORE` for `const passed` at line 8114, but the DISPLAY STRING still said 720); (5) `showExamShortfallBanner()` line 6369: `'the 720 pass mark stays accurate'` → `'the ${EXAM_PASS_SCORE} pass mark stays accurate'`; (6-8) `MILESTONE_DEFS` lines 9151/9157/9177: `desc: 'Score 720+…'` → template literals `\`Score ${EXAM_PASS_SCORE}+…\`` for exam_pass, ready_720, and hardcore_pass milestones (note: the `MILESTONE_CHECKS` logic at lines 9423/9424/9430 was ALREADY using `EXAM_PASS_SCORE` — only the display descriptions were wrong); (9) readiness trajectory line 10502: `'(lower bound → 720)'` → `'(lower bound → ' + EXAM_PASS_SCORE + ')'`. **Class-of-bug**: the `EXAM_PASS_SCORE` constant was defined cert-aware from the start but 9 consumer sites never adopted it, hardcoding the Network+ value. The grep pattern was `\b720\b` across app.js — the fix is to use the constant everywhere. Remaining `720` references verified CORRECT: line 104 (the constant definition's fallback default), line 14648 (defensive `typeof EXAM_PASS_SCORE === 'number' ? EXAM_PASS_SCORE : 720` — correct pattern), and ~8 comments (no functional impact). 1 UAT guard MIGRATED: v4.54.3 `passed = scaled >= 720` → `passed = scaled >= EXAM_PASS_SCORE` (tracks the new cert-aware pattern). 3 new UAT tombstones: finish() must not hardcode 720 in verdict/gap; submitExam result msg must not hardcode 720; milestone descs must not hardcode 720 (prevents regression). Pre-push double gate: `tests/uat.js` **6297/6297** AND Playwright **chromium 99 passed**. Fast-lane per ENVIRONMENT_STRATEGY (cert-aware constant adoption; no DB/auth/money; sw.js = universal CACHE_NAME bump). The Sec+ quiz flow (loading → quiz → results) is now fully cert-aware end-to-end. |

| v4.99.80 | Rebrand Batch 4o — HOME PAGES E2E AUDIT (Net+ + Sec+) completing the v4.99.65 editorial rebuild. Founder directive: "do the Home Pages for Cert+ and Net+ E2E Full analysis as i suspect there is some parts there that needs a redesign." Audited both home mockups (`netplus-home-concept.html` + `secplus-home-concept.html`) against the production `#page-setup` page end-to-end, identified 8 gap categories, fixed all. GAPS FOUND + FIXED: (1) **Banner SVG icons** — Net+ pass-proof mockup specifies a monoline checkmark SVG; Sec+ private-builder mockup specifies a padlock SVG. Production had emoji (`✓`/`🔒`) CSS-hidden with NO replacement visible icon. Fixed: replaced emoji text content in `.pass-proof-icon` and `.secplus-private-icon` spans with the exact mockup inline SVGs, updated CSS from `display:none` to `display:inline-flex` with `color:var(--green)` for checkmark and `color:var(--text-dim)` for padlock. (2) **Secplus banner wording** — Production: `Private · Builder use` + em-dash separator. Mockup: `Private builder` + middot separator. Fixed: updated index.html text. (3) **Domain grid visual bar** — Production renders `.dg-bar-col` + `.dg-bar` (height-based coloured bar per domain cell). Mockup has NO bar. Fixed: `display:none!important` on `.dg-bar-col` scoped to `#page-setup`. (4) **Domain grid rainbow top-border** — styles.css applies 5-colour `border-top: 3px solid` per `[data-domain-idx]`. Mockup has clean hairline. Fixed: `border-top:0!important` on all `[data-domain-idx]` scoped to `#page-setup`. (5) **Domain grid topic dots** — Production renders `<ul class="dg-topic-list">` with `.dg-topic-dot` circle indicators (3 states: weak=accent glow, studied=normal, untouched=dim hollow). Mockup uses simple text with accent `.on` for weak topics. Fixed: `.dg-topic-dot{display:none!important}`, editorial text styling (weak=accent+bold, studied=text-mid, untouched=text-dim). (6) **Domain grid mastery colours** — Mockup uses semantic `.v.pass` (green) and `.v.warn` (amber) on the mastery percentage. Production had no colour differentiation. Fixed: added `data-tier` attribute (`mastered`/`proficient`/`developing`/`novice`/`none`) to each `.domain-cell` in `renderSetupDomainGrid()`, with CSS rules: mastered=`--green`, proficient=`--yellow`, developing+novice=`--text-dim`. Plus a hairline `border-top` separator on `.dg-pct-wrap` matching the mockup's `.dcm`. (7) **Domain grid Security+ support** — `renderSetupDomainGrid()` at line 19860 bailed entirely for non-netplus certs (`CURRENT_CERT !== 'netplus'` → section hidden). The Sec+ mockup shows a full 5-domain grid with SY0-701 domains. Fixed: removed the cert bail; made the function cert-aware by reading `domainOrder` from `Object.keys(DOMAIN_WEIGHTS)` (already cert-populated from the cert pack), computing `weightPct` dynamically from `DOMAIN_WEIGHTS`, reading `displayName` from `DOMAIN_LABELS` (was hardcoded `domainDisplay`), and splitting `CANONICAL_DOMAIN_TOPICS` into `_CANONICAL_NETPLUS` (existing N10-009 map) + `_CANONICAL_SECPLUS` (new SY0-701 map matching the Sec+ mockup: concepts→Security Controls/CIA Triad/Change Mgmt/Cryptography/PKI; threats→Threat Actors/Attack Vectors/Social Engineering/Malware/Mitigation; architecture→Architecture Models/Zero Trust & SDN/Cloud Security/Data Protection/Resilience; operations→Hardening/Vuln Mgmt/Monitoring & SIEM/IAM/Incident Response; governance→Governance/Risk Mgmt/Third-Party Risk/Compliance/Audits). Runtime selection via `CURRENT_CERT === 'secplus'` ternary. (8) **Trajectory emoji** — `renderReadinessCardV2()` baked `⏰` (`⏰`) and `✅` (`✅`) into trajectory innerHTML (lines 19753/19758). Text-baked, CSS can't hide. Both verified DISPLAY-ONLY (the trajectory text is `.textContent`-set, never read/compared; the functional countdown data is `r.daysToExam` + `r.targetGap` integers). Stripped both. **Already CSS-handled (verified, no action needed)**: `🎯` in `.dc-icon` (line 284), `🔥` in `.sd-icon` (line 284), `move.icon` in `#hero-v2-cta-icon` (line 129), `🎯`/`🕒`/`✨` in `.tplan-chip-icon` (line 271), bridge `.tplan-bridge-icon` (line 271). Full editorial domain grid interior CSS added: `.dg-weight` (clean text, no pill bg), `.dg-num` (10px uppercase dim), `.dg-name` (t0/800 ink), `.dg-topic-list` (block list, no bullets), `.dg-pct-wrap` (auto-pushed to bottom via `margin-top:auto` + hairline separator), `.dg-pct` (t1/800 with tier colour), `.dg-status` (10px uppercase dim). 8 UAT guards MIGRATED (never bypassed): v4.53.0 drillDomain window 7000→10000 (cert-aware canonical topics added ~3K chars), v4.54.8 `CANONICAL_DOMAIN_TOPICS`→`_CANONICAL_NETPLUS`, v4.54.10 covers-5-domains → asserts both `_CANONICAL_NETPLUS` (OSI→7-Step) and new `_CANONICAL_SECPLUS` (Security Controls→Audits), v4.54.10 weakSet window 6000→9000, v4.87.0 banner wording `Builder use`→`Private builder`, v4.88.1 cert-bail guards → cert-aware assertions (domainOrder from DOMAIN_WEIGHTS + _CANONICAL_SECPLUS exists). dg-system.css braces 1306/1306 balanced. Pre-push double gate: `tests/uat.js` **6294/6294** AND Playwright **chromium 99 passed, CHROMIUM_EXIT=0**. Fast-lane per ENVIRONMENT_STRATEGY (scoped CSS + cert-aware render refactor + display-only emoji strips; no DB/auth/money; sw.js = universal CACHE_NAME bump). The Home page is now fully editorial for BOTH certs. NEXT: remaining surfaces (Phishing Triage / Attack-to-Mitigation / Acronym Blitz Sec+ variants, landing surfaces, global sidebar/topbar); the founder TB END-OF-REBRAND revision pass (deferred to whole-site-complete). |
| v4.99.79 | Rebrand Batch 4m addendum — CTS E2E AUDIT completing the v4.99.77 editorial rebuild. Audited all 5 CTS UI states (question card, reveal panel, end-of-session, lessons matrix, dashboard) against the mockup spec + the editorial system. GAPS FOUND + FIXED: (1) 2 emoji strips in app.js — `✅` in `ctsSubmitAnswer()` reveal headline (line 17427, decorative emoji preceding the ✓/✗ functional checkmarks) + `CTS_TYPES[weakest].icon` data-level emoji in `ctsEndSession()` weakest-type label (line 17475, stripped to bare `.label` — the `_ctsLineIcon` SVG already renders the icon in the EOS weak card via CSS). Both verified DISPLAY-ONLY: handlers use `ctrl.type`/`ctrl.cat` string IDs and `ctsPickedType`/`ctsPickedCat` state, never glyph text. (2) 6 CSS rules for `.cts-show-correct`/`.cts-show-wrong` — these classes are applied by `ctsSubmitAnswer()` to `.cts-q-opt` type buttons + `.cts-q-cat-pill` category pills after submission (lines 17408-17419) to visually reveal which answer was correct/wrong, but had ZERO CSS coverage in the Batch 4m section. Added: correct = `var(--green)` border + 10% green tint bg + green text/icon; wrong = `var(--red)` border + 10% red tint bg + red text/icon. Both use `color-mix(in oklab)` consistent with the editorial system. Icon variants (`.cts-q-opt-icon`) included so SVG line icons track the semantic colour. Later source order beats the `:hover` and `[class*="cts-active-"]` rules at equal specificity — hovering a revealed correct/wrong button holds its semantic colour. (3) 6 CSS rules for reveal panel inner structure — `ctsSubmitAnswer()` emits `.cts-reveal` > `.cts-reveal-head` + `.cts-reveal-prose` + `.cts-reveal-actions` > `.cts-reveal-btn`, but only the parent `#cts-reveal:not(.is-hidden)` had a CSS rule (line 1684). Added: `.cts-reveal` flex-column gap; `.cts-reveal-head` bold t0 ink; `.cts-reveal-prose` dim t-1 with line-height 1.5; `.cts-reveal-actions` margin-top; `.cts-reveal-btn` bronze accent primary (matching `.cts-submit-btn` exactly) + hover brightness. (4) Inline rainbow VERIFIED NEUTRALIZED — `style="color:${t.color}"` on lessons matrix rows (line 17520) beaten by `.cts-lm-row{color:var(--text)!important}` (line 1715); `style="color:${...color}"` on lesson summaries (line 17530) beaten by `.cts-lesson-sum-label{color:var(--text)!important}` (line 1720); `style="background:${r.t.color}1a;border-left:3px solid ${r.t.color}"` on dashboard type rows (line 17562) beaten by `.cts-dash-type-row{background:none!important;border:0!important;border-left:0!important}` (line 1728). All three `!important` rules confirmed to beat inline styles. Functional state indicators kept: `✓`/`✗` in reveal headline + status line (these are semantic verdict marks, not decorative emoji — same precedent as IR War Room action cards). Inline `style="color:var(--green)"` / `style="color:var(--red)"` on status `<span>` uses app tokens via var() (not hardcoded hex) and applies to a child span (not the `#cts-q-status` parent that has `color:var(--text-dim)!important`), so the semantic pass/fail colour is visible — correct behaviour. Scoped/zero render-LOGIC change — every onclick/id/handler preserved. dg-system.css braces 1323/1323 balanced. (2) NEW `_ctsLineIcon(typeId)` render helper (app.js, before `ctsRenderQuestion`) — 6 monoline SVG path sets (padlock/magnifier/wrench/triangle-warning/cycle-arrows/clipboard) matching the mockup's exact icons, viewBox 24×24, stroke-only `currentColor`. 5 icon repoints across all 4 sub-views (question options, lessons matrix, lesson summary, dashboard type rows, dashboard weakest-type). (3) 7 emoji strips in app.js (streak 🔥 ×2 replace_all, badge 🔥 ×2 replace_all, end-of-session 🔥, end-of-session 💡, dashboard 🛡) + 7 emoji strips in index.html (streak badge 🔥, Practice tab ⚡, Matrix tab 📚, Dashboard tab 📊, MCQ mode ⚡, Sort mode 📁, streak counter 🔥) + 2 Settings emoji strips in app.js (📥 Download / ↩ Restore in `renderAutoBackupList`). All verified DISPLAY-ONLY — the handlers use `t.id`/fixed fns, never the glyph; CTS tab switching uses `data-tab`/`data-mode` attributes, not the label text. `.cts-eos-burst` (🛡 decorative div) + `.cts-lesson-icon` (decorative spans) CSS-hidden via `display:none`. Scoped `--dg-bg-2` token: `#page-cts{--dg-bg-2:oklch(0.205 0.009 275)}` dark / `oklch(0.945 0.008 85)` light — used for active tab bg + disabled submit bg (warm panel, not cool `color-mix`). The user-flagged fidelity issues (5 rounds of "exactly the same as mockup" / "this is not the sme as the mockup") were ALL resolved via systematic direct mockup-vs-render computed-value diffing: (a) column 842→760px border-box (the mockup `.content{max-width:760px}` is border-box including padding — NOT content-only + padding); (b) active tab bg cool→warm `--dg-bg-2` (not `color-mix(in oklab,var(--text) 5%,transparent)`); (c) missing full-width rule divider (`.cts-tab-bar{border-top:1px solid var(--border);width:100%;margin-top:14px;padding-top:18px}`); (d) cue arrow weight 600→400; (e) submit-disabled bg cool→warm; (f) flush-left column (`.ed-pagehead-back` position:absolute so it doesn't indent the title); (g) question card `max-width:none;margin:0` (kill styles.css 600px/80px-left); (h) browser `zoom:1.1` on `<html>` was a red herring (rect × 1.1 ≈ computed, affects both mockup and app uniformly). All sections confirmed EXACT match to the mockup in both themes via final computed-value comparison. Pre-push double gate: `tests/uat.js` **6293/6293** (NO migration — styles.css untouched, all CTS UAT guards check preserved ids/proximity/js) AND Playwright **chromium 99 passed, CHROMIUM_EXIT=0** (real exit captured from log, not pipe-masked). dg-system.css braces 1108/1108 balanced. Process lesson APPLIED: app.js staged on the version ship (bump-version writes APP_VERSION there); the v4.99.70 CI version-triad miss not repeated. Fast-lane per ENVIRONMENT_STRATEGY (scoped CSS + additive render helper + display-only emoji strips; no DB/auth/money; sw.js = universal CACHE_NAME bump). NEXT: remaining surfaces (IR War Room / Phishing Triage / Attack-to-Mitigation / Acronym Blitz Sec+ variants, landing surfaces, global sidebar/topbar); the founder TB END-OF-REBRAND revision pass (deferred to whole-site-complete). |
| v4.99.76 | Rebrand Batch 4l — SETTINGS (`#page-settings`) reskinned to the `mockups/account-page-concept.html` editorial SYSTEM. SCOPE DECISION (surfaced to founder, not guessed — the v4.48.0 Scope-Disambiguation lesson): the account-page mockup is a 7-section ACCOUNT-MANAGEMENT product (Profile · Subscription/entitlements · real-world Exam results · Security/active-sessions · Notification toggles · Delete account · Admin band) but **the app has no account page** — `index.html`/`app.js`/`auth-state.js` searched: `#page-settings` is the only settings-like page and it is a STUDY-CONFIG surface (study-setup health / exam date / daily goal / data export-import / auto-backups / clear-wrong-bank); no `page-account`/`page-admin` route, no "Member since"/"Sign out everywhere"/"Delete account", entitlements explicitly `auth-state.js`-commented "Future Phase G". So "exactly the same as the mockup" could not mean a reskin (the surface doesn't exist) — it would mean building net-new auth/billing/destructive-delete product (gated-lane, partly safety-bounded). Asked the founder via AskUserQuestion; founder chose **"Reskin real #page-settings"** = apply the mockup's editorial SYSTEM (hairline sections · bronze · numbered § · semantic chips · danger zone) to the content that actually exists, fast-lane like the prior 12 (NOT build the account product). The real page already used a numbered §-group structure (`§ 01 Study setup` / `§ 02 Data & backups` / `§ 03 Danger zone`) so it mapped cleanly to the mockup's numbered sections. APPROACH (the proven scoped pattern, identical to ACL-Builder/Concept-Labs): a COMPLETE scoped `#page-settings` dg-system.css override (Batch 4l, ~110 lines) of the static + emitted-render markup → the mockup's `.eyebrow`/`h1`/`.s-head`/`.s-no`/`.s-intro`/`.defrow`/`.subhead`/`.btn`/`.lnk`/`.danger-zone` language: `.ed-pagehead` de-carded → editorial eyebrow(bronze)/display(ink+accent em)/ghost-back; `.settings-group-head`→`.s-head` (baseline flex + hairline rule); `.settings-group-num`→`.s-no`; `.settings-group-h`/em→ink+accent (gradient/`-webkit-text-fill` killed); every `.card.settings-section` DE-CARDED (bg/border/radius/shadow→0 — the box-in-box kill); `.settings-section-title`→`.subhead`; the §01 health card `.settings-health-grid`→de-carded hairline `.defrow`-style label/value rows, the `.settings-health-icon` glyph SPAN `display:none` (✓⚠◐○ — zero render change), tier conveyed by semantic VALUE colour (ok=`--green`/mid=`--accent`/warn=`--text-dim`); the shared exam-date chip (`_buildExamDateChipHtml`, also Home/Analytics) editorialised + its decorative 🎯 `.ana-ready-datechip-icon` span hidden SCOPED to `#page-settings` (mirrors the Analytics v4.99.67 treatment — Home/Analytics keep their own; span-hide = zero render change); daily-goal input→hairline, Save→bronze `.btn`, presets/`.is-active`→quiet bronze-tint chips (the styles.css gradient untouched → its CSS guard stays green); §02 export/import/snapshot `.btn-ghost`→editorial hairline ghosts, `#autobackup-list`/`.ab-row`/`.ab-row-date`/`.ab-row-meta`/`.ab-row-actions`→de-carded hairline rows w/ mono meta + editorial action buttons; §03 `.settings-group-danger`→the mockup `.danger-zone` (1px `color-mix(--dg-danger 40%,transparent)` border + 12px radius), head/`#wrong-bank-clear`→`--dg-danger` outline `.btn-danger`, `.wrong-count-badge`→danger-tint — ONE scoped `--dg-danger` token (`#page-settings{--dg-danger:oklch(0.66 0.17 25)}` dark / `oklch(0.52 0.19 25)` light) used ONLY for the destructive zone (the mockup's impeccable discipline, the established Quiz-Results/ACL pattern). Scoped/zero render-LOGIC change — every id/onclick preserved (`goSetup`/`saveSettingsDailyGoal`/`pickSettingsDailyPreset`/`exportData`/`importData`/`downloadAutoBackup`/`restoreFromAutoBackup`/`_takeAutoBackup`/`clearWrongBank` + all `settings-*`/`autobackup-list`/`wrong-bank-clear` ids). 7 surgical display-only emoji strips (5 static index.html: 📥 Export / 📤 Import / 📥 Download-snapshot / 💾 Snapshot-now / 🗑 Clear-Wrong-Bank; 2 emitted app.js `renderAutoBackupList`: 📥 Download / ↩ Restore) — all verified DISPLAY-ONLY (the onclick handlers use `b.key`/fixed fns, never the glyph; the UAT-asserted substrings "Clear Wrong Answers Bank"/`exportData()`/`importData(`/`clearWrongBank` + their proximity windows are preserved — the glyph was a leading prefix). 2-round both-theme anti-slop audit (Chrome MCP localhost, SW unregistered for fresh CSS — the v4.99.65 stale-`?v` lesson, dev `?v=dg-dev5→8` cache-bust hand-normalised to 4.99.76 at ship) CAUGHT + FIXED 3 real leaks the programmatic pass alone would have shipped (the discipline working, as designed): (1) round-1 light — a visible 🎯 in the SHARED exam chip's `.ana-ready-datechip-icon` (fixed: scoped span-hide, the Analytics pattern); (2) round-1 — a legacy purple `.ed-pagehead::after` 80×2px accent BAR (`rgb(99,85,224)` = the #1 editorial-premium AI-tell; the scoped `.ed-pagehead` rule killed the element bg/border but not its `::after` — fixed: `::after`/`::before` content:none); (3) round-2 DARK — a `1.45px solid` bronze `.ed-pagehead-display` OUTLINE box + 4px radius from styles.css (the exact Analytics v4.99.67 h1-outline class-of-leak — fixed: scoped `outline:none/border-radius:0`). Re-verified clean BOTH themes end-to-end (header → §01 health/exam/daily → §02 export/backups → §03 danger): 0 purple, 0 gradient, 0 card, 0 visible emoji, hairline editorial, bronze accent, semantic `--dg-danger` only in §03. Founder reviewed the live dark render mid-audit → "looks great" (an interim "make it exactly the same as the mockup" push was retracted with "sorry, looks great" — the editorial-SYSTEM-on-real-content scope held). Pre-push double gate (the locked process): `tests/uat.js` **6293/6293** — **NO migration needed**: every settings UAT guard either reads `css`=styles.css (UNTOUCHED — `#page-settings max-width:720px`, `.settings-section`/`-title`, `.settings-daily-chip.is-active` gradient, `.settings-exam-row` flex all still present so green) or asserts preserved HTML ids/onclicks/ordered substrings (the emoji strips kept every asserted token + proximity) — same as every prior scoped-CSS surface; AND Playwright **chromium 99 passed, CHROMIUM_EXIT=0** (the real exit captured separately, not pipe-masked — the v4.99.74 lesson held). Process lesson APPLIED: app.js staged on the version ship (bump-version writes APP_VERSION there) though the functional change is the 2 app.js emoji strips + dg-system.css + index.html — the v4.99.70 CI version-triad miss not repeated. Fast-lane per ENVIRONMENT_STRATEGY (scoped CSS + display-only string de-slop; no DB/auth/money — the founder-deferred net-new account product is the gated-lane work; sw.js = universal CACHE_NAME bump). The settings/study-config surface is now the same calm editorial product as the rest of the app. NEXT: the founder TB END-OF-REBRAND revision pass (Network Builder + 3D view + toolbar/how-to bundled, deferred to whole-site-complete); remaining no-mockup surfaces → taste-skill; the net-new account/billing/security/notifications/delete-account product → revisit when Phase-G entitlements + Stripe + delete-account backend exists. |
| v4.99.75 | Rebrand Batch 4k — CONCEPT / TOPOLOGY LABS picker (`#tb-lab-picker`, the Guided Topology Labs modal opened by `tbOpenLabPicker()` from inside the Network Builder) **full structural build** to the locked `mockups/netplus-concept-labs-concept.html`. ORIGIN: the founder reviewed the rendered concept-labs mockup screenshot and said "need to look like this please. also i like the icon at the top left and the layout. i want **everything** you see in the screenshot to be developed" — so this is NOT the pure-scoped-CSS refine the todo originally scoped; it is a faithful structural build (the recolor→rebuild lesson, applied proactively at modal scale): index.html head rewrite + a features/topology-builder.js render tweak + a scoped dg-system.css Batch 4k. (1) HEAD REWRITE (index.html ~2519): the legacy `<div class="tb-grade-card-head"><h3>Guided Topology Labs</h3>` → the mockup `.tb-lab-head` system — a 34px bordered `.tb-lab-ic` flask-icon box (inline monoline SVG, `color:var(--accent)`), a `.tb-lab-ht` stack (`.tb-lab-eyebrow` "Guided learning" 10px/.16em uppercase dim + `h3` "Topology Labs"), a 32px boxed `.tb-grade-close`, and a NEW pinned `.tb-lab-foot` footer (star SVG + "**Auto-generated config variants** sit alongside these labs…" note) — the close `onclick` + `#tb-lab-picker-body` id preserved verbatim. (2) RENDER TWEAK (features/topology-builder.js ~12931, `tbOpenLabPicker`): the category loop went from every `<details class="tb-lab-category" open>` to first-category-only open via a `_tbLabCatIdx` counter — open group renders as the `.g-h` lab list, the rest as collapsed mockup `.grp` rows; still `<details>` so every group stays click-expandable — **zero behaviour change**, only the default open-state of N−1 `<details>`. (3) dg-system.css Batch 4k (scoped to the unique `#tb-lab-picker` id so it can't bleed into the `.tb-fix-tab`/`.tb-lab-card` classes SHARED with `#tb-fix-picker`): de-cards the lab grid → hairline rows (`.tb-lab-category-cards`→`display:block`), `.tb-fix-tabs`→a segmented control, `.tb-lab-diff`→semantic TEXT (beginner=`var(--green)`, else `var(--accent)` — rainbow pills killed), the per-row `.tb-lab-card::after`→a CSS border-chevron (dim→accent on hover), open `.tb-lab-category`→`.g-h` header, collapsed `.tb-lab-category:not([open]) > .tb-lab-category-head`→the mockup `.grp` row (15px pad, var(--t0)/700 text-mid, NOT uppercase, count hidden, chevron `::after`), plus the round-2 head CSS (`.tb-lab-ic` 34px box, `.tb-lab-eyebrow`, `.tb-lab-head h3`, `.tb-lab-foot`). 2-ROUND VISUAL/CODE ANTI-SLOP AUDIT (both themes, Chrome MCP localhost) caught + fixed TWO real bugs the programmatic pass alone would have shipped: (a) **dark near-white token** — the active-tab + lab-card-hover backgrounds used `var(--surface)`, which resolves to ~`oklab(0.945)` (near-white) in the dark lab-picker modal context → glaring light blocks in dark; fixed by swapping both to `color-mix(in oklab, var(--text) 8%/6%, transparent)`, a theme-safe self-correcting tint (LESSON LOCKED: never `var(--surface)` for subtle elevation in a scoped dark modal — use a `color-mix` of `--text` into transparent). (b) **uppercased title** — the global `h3{text-transform:uppercase}` rendered "TOPOLOGY LABS" vs the mockup's mixed-case "Topology Labs"; fixed with `text-transform:none!important` on `#tb-lab-picker .tb-lab-head h3` (re-verified `titleTransform:none`). Round-1 (light) verified faithful: flask box, "GUIDED LEARNING" eyebrow, mixed-case title, boxed close, segmented filter (All active), open FUNDAMENTALS group + "5 labs", hairline lab rows w/ green BEGINNER semantic text + per-row chevron, 7 collapsed `.grp` rows (Switching/Routing/Services/Security/Cloud & WAN/Wireless & QoS/Troubleshooting), pinned footer. Round-2 (dark) programmatic CLEAN (bronze icon box, title tt=none, hairline footer, grp 14px/700 text-mid, transparent card, green diff) + dark VISUAL confirmed faithful — no extra fix (clean both rounds after the 2 bug fixes, like Quiz-Results). Localhost SW stale-cache gotcha hit AGAIN + worked around honestly: `#tb-lab-picker` is a child of `#page-topology-builder` (Pro-gated + page-inactive `display:none`), so the harness had to unregister the SW, force a fresh lazy `_loadFeature('topology-builder')`, activate the page, and `.remove()` the transient Pro-gate overlay — all ephemeral DOM/`data-theme`/element-removal on **localhost:8000** (the approved local-server alt), ZERO `nplus_*` localStorage writes (the v4.81.x data-safety rule honoured); prod unaffected (every deploy bumps CACHE_NAME → fresh module). UAT: **NO migration needed** — guard 1242 asserts the preserved `#tb-lab-picker` id, guard 3976 reads `css.includes('.tb-lab-category')` against styles.css which is UNTOUCHED (the `.tb-lab-category` class is still emitted by the render); **6293/6293**. Playwright: chromium **99 passed, exit 0** (real `CHROMIUM_EXIT` captured, not pipe-masked — the v4.99.74 lesson held). dg-system.css `?v` hand-normalised dev→4.99.75 (braces 937/937 balanced). Process lesson APPLIED: app.js staged on the version ship (bump-version writes APP_VERSION there) though the functional change is index.html + features/topology-builder.js + dg-system.css — the v4.99.70 CI version-triad miss not repeated. Fast-lane (markup + CSS + feature-module render-default tweak; no DB/auth/money; sw.js = universal CACHE_NAME bump). The full Network-Builder → Concept-Labs flow is now editorial end-to-end. NEXT: 3D view (bundled with the founder-requested TB revision pass once the whole-site rebrand is complete) / toolbar-howto (Net+ & Sec+); no-mockup surfaces → taste-skill. |
| v4.99.74 | Rebrand Batch 4j — NETWORK BUILDER (`#page-topology-builder`, the ~14K-LOC Topology Builder flagship) **full structural rebuild** to the locked `mockups/netplus-network-builder-concept.html`. ORIGIN: the founder explicitly rejected the first attempt (a scoped-CSS-over-the-existing-DOM recolor) as "nothing like the mockup" — same lesson as the v4.99.64→65 Home pivot: the mockups are ground-up redesigns, not repaints. This ship is the actual rebuild. The mockup is a **standalone full-viewport workspace** (no app sidebar/topbar), so: (1) FULL-VIEWPORT TAKEOVER — scoped `html:has(#page-topology-builder.page.active)` hides `#app-sidebar`/`#app-topbar`/`#sb-mobile-toggle` and makes the page `position:fixed;inset:0`, ONLY when TB is active (every other page keeps global chrome; pure conditional CSS, zero JS, reversible). (2) the big `.ed-pagehead` → a thin full-width `.tb-strip` header (markup) with the exact mockup eyebrow "Network+ · Sandbox · design & simulate", `Network <em>builder.</em>`, `#tb-device-count` + a working Theme toggle (`toggleTheme()`) + account pill. (3) the 200px purple-gradient "Suggested" billboard → a ONE-LINE accent strip (the proven shared `_pageRecCard`→band transform, same as Home). (4) the two stacked "Full toolbar"/"How to build" accordions FOLDED (`#tb-toolbar-details`/`#tb-howto-details` `display:none` — engine ids stay in DOM + scriptable; UAT-required) + a mockup `.help` "?" affordance that reveals the how-to as a centered popover. (5) full-bleed 3-col `.work` workspace (212/1fr/236). (6) the device palette de-carded to the mockup's clean uniform 1-col list with a **new clean monoline icon set** — `tbPaletteLineIcon(type)` added in features/topology-builder.js (the mockup's exact icons for what it shows + mockup-consistent monoline for the other ~20 device types), PALETTE-ONLY (the palette template now calls it; canvas device nodes still use the untouched `tbDeviceIcon` — engine render preserved). (7) the floating modebar centered + every pill given the mockup's exact inline SVG line icon + label (Design/Simulate/Trace/Labs/3D View/Coach/Grade/Export — onclick/data-tb-pill/aria preserved). (8) the right scenario rail rebuilt value-for-value to the mockup `.scn/.scn-g/.scn-h/.scn-i/.dv/.on` (the founder's reference attachment — category headers + counts, "Free Build/open", "N dev" hairline chips, accent-tinted current). (9) canvas grid de-purpled to a `--tb-grid-stroke` token (was hardcoded `rgba(124,111,247,.08)`); every modal/panel de-carded + de-purpled (inline gradient heads overridden via `!important`). Decorative emoji icon-spans CSS-hidden; text-baked toolbar/modebar/howto/modal-title emoji stripped (display-only). Zero render-LOGIC change — every engine id/onclick/handler + the hidden `.tb-v2-header` UAT stub preserved; the only feature-file change is the additive `tbPaletteLineIcon` + repointing the palette template at it. UAT: 2 guards MIGRATED (never bypassed) — v4.54.13 `.ed-pagehead`→`.tb-strip` and v4.49.1 how-to-summary 📚→editorial-no-emoji (both keep regression strength: assert the new structure + that the emoji can't return); **6293/6293**. **Playwright: a real self-introduced regression was caught + fixed pre-ship** — the round-3 `.tb-pill{display:inline-flex!important}` (modebar icon layout) out-specified the `[hidden]` guard, forcing engine-`hidden`-toggled 3D-chrome pills (OSI/Exit/Trace) to leak VISIBLE → 2 TB-3D-OSI `toBeHidden()` failures (97/99 chromium). Root-caused (not re-run-and-hoped), fixed with `.tb-pill:not([hidden])` + an explicit `.tb-pill[hidden]{display:none!important}` belt-and-suspenders → chromium **99 passed, exit 0**. LESSON (locked): `npx playwright test | tail` masks Playwright's real exit code (the pipe returns `tail`'s 0) — exactly the `gh run watch` masked-exit class; always capture `PLAYWRIGHT_EXIT=$?` separately. (The "198 failed" on the full local run was purely a missing local WebKit binary `webkit-2272` — environment, not code; CI installs browsers fresh + gates on chromium.) Localhost-verification caveat: the SW stubbornly serves the stale lazy `features/topology-builder.js` on localhost (documented SW-cache gotcha — prod is unaffected, every deploy bumps CACHE_NAME so prod loads the fresh module); the palette-icon visual was confirmed by fetching the fresh server module (verified it serves the new `tbPaletteLineIcon` + template) and painting its exact output into the live DOM — i.e. the verified production render. dg-system.css `?v` hand-normalised dev→4.99.74. Fast-lane (CSS + additive feature-icon fn + markup de-slop; no DB/auth/money; sw.js = universal CACHE_NAME bump). **FOUNDER STATUS: explicitly not fully satisfied with TB — directed to ship now and queue a dedicated TB revision pass for once the whole-site rebrand is complete** (revision-pass todo tracked alongside: 3D-explorer internal chrome emoji, the deprecated full-toolbar `<select>` ~30 native-`<option>` emoji, cable token-tuning, canvas representative-state, device-icon exactness). The recolor→rebuild lesson is re-confirmed at flagship scale. NEXT: 3D view / concept-labs / toolbar-howto (Net+ & Sec+); then the founder TB revision pass; no-mockup surfaces → taste-skill. |
| v4.99.73 | Rebrand Batch 4i — ACL SCENARIO PICKER (`#acl-scenario-picker`) editorial rebuild to the locked `mockups/netplus-acl-scenario-picker-concept.html`. Founder directive: next surface, taste-skill audit, redesign any AI slop. Audited the mockup FIRST against editorial-premium: NOT slop (it explicitly de-slops the legacy soft-shadow 2-col card GRID — the identical-card-grid ban — + difficulty-coloured card borders + rainbow diff PILLS + the "Current" PILL into a hairline grouped LIST sharing the Concept-Labs vocabulary: group header, then rows = name + text difficulty tag (beginner=pass green, else accent) + one-line brief + quiet meta (tests · obj); the CURRENT scenario gets an accent left-bar + faint tint, not a pill; bronze, 0 emoji/hex; calm). The "Pick a scenario" modal is rendered by `aclOpenScenarioPicker()` (features/acl-builder.js) into `#acl-scenario-picker-body` = `.acl-picker-cat` > `.acl-picker-cat-title` + `.acl-picker-grid` > `.acl-picker-card(.-{difficulty}/.-active)` > `.acl-picker-ico` / `.acl-picker-title`(+`.acl-picker-current`) / `.acl-picker-desc` / `.acl-picker-meta` > `.acl-picker-diff` / `.acl-picker-tests` / `.acl-picker-obj`. Batch 4g (the ACL Builder ship) already de-carded the picker GENERICALLY (hairline-bordered boxes); this Batch 4i is a focused REFINEMENT (new dg-system.css block, later source order, equal `html[data-theme] body #page-acl` specificity → wins where it overrides 4g) into the dedicated grouped-list structure: `.acl-picker-grid` grid→`display:block` list; `.acl-picker-card`(+difficulty variants)→hairline-top rows (border box + radius + difficulty border-colour killed, `:first-child` no top rule, hover = quiet surface tint not transform); `.acl-picker-card-active`→faint accent tint + a 2px accent left-bar `::before` (the legacy border-accent killed); `.acl-picker-title`→baseline-flex name row; `.acl-picker-current`→small accent uppercase key (pill killed); `.acl-picker-diff`(+`-beginner/-intermediate/-advanced`)→colour TEXT only, beginner=`--green` / intermediate+advanced=`--accent` (rainbow bg/border/radius/padding pills killed — mockup `.tag`/`.tag.mid`); `.acl-picker-tests`/`.acl-picker-obj`→quiet dim tabular meta text (hairline chips from 4g killed); `.acl-picker-cat-title`→`.g-h` group header. The `.acl-picker-ico` per-scenario emoji span stays Batch-4g-CSS-hidden (the mockup uses SVG line icons which the zero-markup rule forbids injecting — the list reads name-led, the established `-ico` precedent across all 8 prior surfaces; verified ZERO *visible* emoji via a TreeWalker visible-text scan — the `.acl-picker-ico` glyphs are display:none so they are not slop, only present in `.textContent`). Modal motion stays CALM (no keyframe — consistent with the shipped surfaces; the mockup's optional center-origin scale is a flourish, not slop-critical). Scoped/zero render-logic change — every onclick (`aclLoadScenario('<id>')`)/id/handler preserved. 6 surgical display-only emoji strips in features/acl-builder.js: the 5 `ACL_CATEGORIES` labels (`🧪 Sandbox`/`📚 Fundamentals`/`🏢 Real-world`/`⚠️ PBQ Traps`/`🔧 Fix It` → bare text) + the `🧪` in `.acl-picker-tests` (`🧪 N tests` → `N tests`); all verified display-only — `cat.key` (the `s.category === cat.key` filter key) and `s.testPackets.length` (the count) are untouched, only the display label/prefix; the UAT `ACL Builder preserves ACL_SCENARIOS + ACL_CATEGORIES` structure guard stays green. 2-round visual/code anti-slop loop (Chrome MCP localhost, both themes, picker driven open via `_loadFeature('acl-builder')` + `aclOpenScenarioPicker()`, `#page-acl` made active so the fixed modal escapes the inactive-page `display:none`): round 1 programmatic — grid→block, card-box→hairline rows, rainbow diff→colour text, current=accent bar+tint, ico hidden, "4 tests"; round 2 visual CAUGHT what round 1 missed (the discipline working, like the ACL Builder `.ed-pagehead` purple catch) — the 5 `ACL_CATEGORIES` group headers still carried baked-in emoji (data-level, a text node CSS cannot hide) → stripped; re-verified zero visible emoji (TreeWalker) + faithful both themes. Pre-push double gate (the locked process): `tests/uat.js` 6293/6293 (the `.acl-picker-*` rules are dg-system.css scoped; styles.css is UNTOUCHED so its picker CSS guards stay green; the picker HTML ids are preserved; the `ACL_CATEGORIES`/`ACL_SCENARIOS` structure guard confirms the label strips were display-only → NO migration, same as the prior scoped-CSS surfaces) AND `npx playwright test` 99 passed, 0 fail/flaky, locally before push. dg-system.css `?v` hand-normalised dev→4.99.73. Process lesson applied: app.js staged on the version ship (bump-version writes APP_VERSION there) though the functional change is in features/acl-builder.js + dg-system.css. | v4.99.72 | Rebrand Batch 4h — PAYWALL / PRO-GATE MODAL (`.quota-exceeded-modal`) editorial redesign. Founder caught this during the v4.99.71 ACL Builder browser walkthrough ("this itself needs a redesign too") — the app's own Pro-gate / paywall modal, which the prior rows had explicitly flagged as a separate monetization surface out of the per-page reskin scope. No locked mockup exists for it → redesigned fresh with the taste-skill (editorial-premium) per the standing founder directive ("any areas that don't have a mockup → redesign with the taste-skill"). The legacy modal is textbook AI-slop on every editorial-premium tell: a ⚡ (`&#9889;`) emoji in a red-tinted glow square, a soft red-bordered card with a heavy `0 20px 60px` shadow, and a hardcoded PURPLE gradient CTA (`linear-gradient(135deg,#5b4fdb,#7c6ff7)` — the #1 AI-tell, hardcoded hex). Rendered by `_showProOnlyUI(detail)` (app.js ~671) which is fired by `_gateProOnly(featureLabel)` (~638) on Free/anon users hitting a Pro feature; the `.quota-exceeded-*` classes are SHARED with the daily-quota-exceeded twin modal (~570) and both are appended to `<body>` (no `#page-` ancestor). The job is singular: state the value, upgrade or dismiss. Taste-skill editorial: de-card to a calm hairline card on a quiet (no-blur) backdrop, hide the decorative ⚡ icon (it is the sole content of a dedicated `.pro-only-icon`/`.quota-exceeded-icon` div → CSS `display:none`, so ZERO render/markup/JS change), replace it with a typographic `PRO` eyebrow (scoped `::before`), promote the title to a 24px/800 ink display heading, left-align the layout (was generic-centered), swap the purple gradient CTA for the solid bronze `--accent` (matches every other primary across the 7 prior surfaces), and make the dismiss an editorial hairline ghost (text-dim → accent on hover). New dg-system.css Batch 4h: a scoped `.quota-exceeded-modal` override (scoped to the modal's own class since there is no page ancestor — and intentionally so it covers BOTH the pro-gate and the quota twin: same slop, same fix, desirable consistency, not a scope leak). PURE CSS — zero render-logic / markup / JS change; every id (`pro-only-modal`/`pro-only-dismiss`), the `certanvil.com/pricing` href, the dismiss/backdrop click handlers all preserved. 2-round anti-slop audit (Chrome MCP localhost, both themes, modal triggered via `_showProOnlyUI({feature:'ACL Builder'})`): round 1 programmatic — purple gradient→bronze `oklch(0.5 0.155 55)`, soft-card+heavy-shadow+red-border→hairline, ⚡ `display:none`, 24px/800 title, ghost dismiss; round 2 visual (light + dark) — calm editorial paywall, faithful, no slop, AA both themes; clean BOTH rounds (no extra fix needed). Pre-push double gate (the locked process): `tests/uat.js` 6293/6293 (the `.quota-exceeded-modal` CSS guards read styles.css which is UNTOUCHED, the gate-shows-modal-when-blocked behavioral guard reads JS which is UNCHANGED → NO migration; same as the prior scoped-CSS surfaces) AND `npx playwright test` 99 passed, 0 fail/flaky, locally before push. dg-system.css `?v` hand-normalised dev→4.99.72. Process lesson applied: app.js staged on the version ship (bump-version writes APP_VERSION there) though this is a pure-CSS surface — the v4.99.70 CI version-triad miss not repeated. Fast-lane (CSS only; no DB/auth/money logic — the gate JS + pricing href untouched; sw.js = universal CACHE_NAME bump). The paywall is the conversion-critical surface a free user hits before subscribing; it now reads as the same calm editorial product as the rest of the app instead of a generic purple SaaS upsell. NEXT: ACL scenario picker (own mockup) / network-builder / 3D view / concept-labs / toolbar-howto (Net+ & Sec+); no-mockup surfaces → taste-skill. |

The 2-round skill-audit/visual loop (the explicit anti-slop discipline) caught what the programmatic pass missed: a persistent styles.css `outline` on the analytics h1, stray `::before` glyphs (em-dash on eyebrows, `▶` on the milestones `<details>`), a 4-col 86px giant-block activity calendar (→ compact 14-col contribution grid like the mockup `.hm`), a residual `.ana-streak-big` box + bordered `.ana-milestone` tiles (→ de-carded editorial), and — critically — INLINE emoji baked into render strings (CSS can't hide a text node): the 4 readiness tier labels (`🟢 Exam Ready`/`🟠 Getting Close`/`🟡 Building`/`🔴 Not Ready`), the action-eyebrow (`🎯 Your next 30 minutes`), the empty-state CTA (`⚡ Start 5-min Warmup`). Fixed via 6 SURGICAL app.js string strips — verified DISPLAY-ONLY first (the tier label is interpolated once into `.ana-ready-badge` at 14604 and never compared; the other `tier` vars in app.js are unrelated quota/diagnostic/heatmap vars) so zero behaviour/logic change, same precedent + bar as Home's Port-Numbers chip / summary-gear edits (emoji = the #1 editorial-premium AI-tell; mandatory removal). All other emoji were `-icon` spans already CSS-hidden. Pre-push gate (locked in after Home CI#1): ran BOTH `tests/uat.js` (6293/6293 — the strips tripped no guard, readiness logic intact) AND `npx playwright test` (99 passed, 0 fail/flaky) locally before push. dg-system.css `?v` hand-normalised dev→4.99.67. Fast-lane (CSS + display-string de-slop; no DB/auth/money; sw.js = universal CACHE_NAME bump). Three primary daily surfaces (Home/Progress/Analytics) now on the proven skill-audited per-surface pattern. NEXT: quiz×3 / ACL×2 / network-builder / 3D / concept-labs / toolbar (Net+ & Sec+). |

Every onclick preserved (`drillTopic`/`focusTopic`/`setProgressFilter`/`setProgressSort`/`filterProgressPage`/`goSetup`) + all ids. ORIGIN: founder caught the v4.99.64 token-recolor as "AI slop, nothing like the mockup" — correct: a zero-markup token-redefinition layer can only repaint the OLD card-stack DOM; the locked mockups are ground-up editorial redesigns (own DOM/IA, no soft-card stack, hairline bands). v4.99.64 recolor ship HALTED; the dg OKLCH 4-block cascade is kept as the COLOR system *under* the real per-surface reskin (it is infra, not the deliverable). This ship is the first surface actually rebuilt. APPROACH: `#page-setup` markup replaced with the editorial `.dgh-*` structure (`.dgh-wrap/.dgh-top/.dgh-greet/.dgh-rd/.dgh-stats/.dgh-band[.is-nbm]/.dgh-sess/.dgh-grp/.dgh-opts/.dgh-opt/.dgh-dbd/.dgh-db/.dgh-strict/.dgh-dom/.dgh-customq`); every render-fn id + onclick + chip `data-v` contract preserved (49 ids, mapped via a full app.js home-render wiring audit); legacy dual-DOM (`.hero`/`#readiness-card`/`#daily-goal-card`/`#marathon-section`/`#wrong-preset-tile`) kept as CSS-clipped stubs so `finish()`/`submitExam()`/`renderTodaySection` still write them; ZERO app.js render-logic change. DOM balanced 119/119 (the long-standing 112-over-113 stray "/Custom quiz" `</div>` now legitimately pairs the new `.dgh-wrap`). dg-system.css gained a scoped `#page-setup` editorial layer (mockup `--dg-*` mapped onto the app tokens the v4.99.64 cascade already redefines → ONE color system); a visibility-guard re-asserting `[hidden]`/`.is-hidden` with `!important` so render-fn toggles hold under the scoped display rules; and COMPLETE high-specificity overrides for the three innerHTML-painted surfaces — `renderTodayPlan` (purple-gradient card + quote glyph + unreadable purple-on-purple subtext), `renderDailyChallengeCard` (glass card + emoji), `renderStreakDefender` (amber card) — plus the entire Custom Quiz interior (hardcoded purple literals `rgba(124,111,247)`/`#6355e0`/`#5b4bdc`/`#7c6ff7`, `!important` tier-colour chips, `[data-theme=light]` white/purple, per-domain colour-coding, dark summary bar → editorial; emoji icons hidden; domain accordions de-boxed to a hairline-divided list = box-in-box AI-tell fix). The render fns emit clean semantic classes; the purple/glass/emoji came from styles.css, so a COMPLETE scoped override is the correct low-risk fix (zero app.js change, all click-wiring preserved) — the earlier partial pass was an INCOMPLETE override, not a wrong approach. 15 UAT structural guards MIGRATED (never bypassed) to assert the new editorial structure with regression strength preserved. taste-skill → editorial-premium audit applied (programmatic + visual) and caught what screenshots missed: a self-introduced regression (the Security+ private banner force-shown in Network+ → two stacked banners; cert gate restored), emoji slop (🔌 Port Numbers chip, ⚙ summary, ✓/🔒 banner dingbats — markup+CSS, `data-v` intact), and the box-in-box. Verified clean: 0 purple, 0 gradient-text, 0 visible emoji, one bronze accent + one neutral family, literary hero, hairline readiness box, paced editorial bands. ROOT-CAUSE LESSON (bought a double false-"done"): the browser HTTP-cached `dg-system.css?v=4.99.64` (the `?v` was static across edits) so CSS fixes were not loading during verification; fixed with a changing `?v` cache-bust during dev — bump-version only rewrites a semver `?v`, so the `dg-dev5` dev token was hand-normalized to `?v=4.99.65` at ship (else prod serves stale CSS, same bug class as a stale SW cache name). Founder approved live on localhost. UAT 6293/6293 (the 2 version assertions resolved by the bump). Fast-lane per ENVIRONMENT_STRATEGY (HTML/CSS/UAT; no DB/auth/money; sw.js touch = the universal per-deploy CACHE_NAME bump + the v4.99.64 `./dg-system.css` SHELL_ASSETS entry, zero fetch-logic change) → canonical trunk push, CI-gated, not blind-pushed. SCOPE: the global sidebar/topbar is a SEPARATE surface (own mockups, all 30 pages), deliberately not bundled. NEXT: remaining cert surfaces (progress/analytics/quiz×3/ACL×2/network-builder/3D/concept-labs/toolbar, Net+ & Sec+) rebuilt on this proven skill-audited per-surface pattern, each verified vs its mockup before ship. |
| v4.99.64 | Rebrand Batch 3 — cert-app dg-system FOUNDATION (the 30-page exam app, after B1 pricing/privacy/terms + B2 homepage both LIVE on certanvil.com). The cert-app is FULLY token-driven (every component uses var(--accent/--surface/--bg/--text/--green/--red/--yellow…)), so unlike landing (per-class override layer) the foundation REDEFINES the app's OWN token names to the dg OKLCH palette. New dg-system.css at repo root, loaded by index.html AFTER styles.css: 4-block cascade — html:root (dark base) → @media prefers-color-scheme:light html:root (light) → html[data-theme=dark] → html[data-theme=light] LAST. Out-specifies styles.css's own token layer (:root/[data-theme]=spec(0,1,0); ours html:root/html[data-theme]=(0,1,1) + later source order = wins). One file recolours all ~30 pages coherently. Plus de-card primitives over the structural class contract (.card → hairline border + no glass/shadow/backdrop; .btn*/.option/.chip/h3) — flattens the glassy soft-card system into the dg hairline aesthetic; html[data-theme] body … specificity beats styles.css's ~500 per-component [data-theme] rules (the Batch-1 lesson at 10x scale). --accent-rgb set to sRGB approximations of the dg accent (dark 225,170,95 / light 150,95,40) so legacy rgba(var(--accent-rgb)) glows track the rebrand. ZERO markup + ZERO render-JS change. Purely additive (new file + one <link> in index.html + one SHELL_ASSETS entry in sw.js, removes/renames nothing) so NO UAT structural guard tripped + NO migration needed → UAT 6293/6293 green first run. 0 hardcoded hex in COLOR values, 0 em-dash, 0 emoji in the CSS. SW touch is the universal per-deploy CACHE_NAME bump + one additive SHELL_ASSETS precache entry for the same-commit-committed CSS (zero fetch-logic change) — rides the canonical trunk push (git→main→CI UAT+Playwright+tech-debt→Vercel prod), CI-gated, not blind-pushed. Per-surface fine-tuning + the ~500 residual hardcoded-hex/[data-theme] cleanup + Net+/Sec+ per-page polish are Batches 4+. |
| v4.99.63 | Diagnostic flow DUAL-THEME — founder reported they primarily use light mode; v4.99.60/61 shipped dark-only (my miss: scene-reasoned dark, never weighted the actual primary user). Retrofitted diagnostic-system.css to a 4-block cascade: html:root (dark base + theme-invariant vars) → @media prefers-color-scheme:light html:root (light) → html[data-theme=dark] → html[data-theme=light] LAST (ordering = the cascade-correctness fix: equal specificity, toggle must be last to beat OS default). LIGHT = warm-ivory paper oklch(0.975 0.007 85) + cool ink + forged-bronze; DARK = unchanged v4.99.60 charcoal/brass. Both AA-clean, same forge soul. The 4 pages' bootstrap evolved from forced `var theme='dark'` (overrode OS pref) to flash-free persisted-or-prefers (sets data-theme only if a choice persisted, else @media drives) — founder on light OS now gets ivory editorial automatically. Explicit toggle UI deferred to fast-follow (prefers already delivers the need). CSS link ?v=4.99.63 across all 4 (content changed → cache-bust). Mockup-first: dual-theme proven on the homepage concept (canvas-AA + toggle + persist + prefers), founder locked light, then retrofitted. Verify caught a real cascade bug (toggle losing to OS) + a tooling limit (canvas mis-parses Chrome oklab() serialization → use color(srgb)/token assertions). Render-independent verify conclusive: prefers→ivory, data-theme=dark→exact charcoal, =light→exact ivory, no landing collision, D.4/D.6 behaviour intact. UAT: stale forced-dark tombstone migrated → prefers-aware + 2 dual-theme guards. 6293/6293. Fast-lane — token+bootstrap only, NO asset-path touch (picker stays v4.99.62-fixed ../diagnostic/), cleanUrls risk N/A, render-deterministic localhost↔prod. NEXT: toggle UI fast-follow; landing homepage port + sweep (dual-theme, Vercel-preview-gated). |
| v4.99.62 | PROD HOTFIX (founder-reported) — diagnostic picker (certanvil.com/diagnostic, the funnel ENTRY) rendered completely unstyled in prod after the v4.99.61 sweep. Root cause: Vercel cleanUrls + trailingSlash:false relative-path trap. v4.99.61 set the picker's shared-CSS link to `./diagnostic-system.css?v=4.99.61`; prod serves the picker at `/diagnostic` (no trailing slash) so `./` resolved against base dir `/` → certanvil.com/diagnostic-system.css → 404 → zero stylesheet. CSS file itself fine (/diagnostic/diagnostic-system.css = 200); only the picker href wrong. results/intake/quiz use `../diagnostic-system.css` from /diagnostic/network-plus/* → /diagnostic/diagnostic-system.css = 200, unaffected; bug isolated to picker. Fix: picker href `./` → `../diagnostic/diagnostic-system.css` (resolves correctly in BOTH prod cleanUrls and localhost /landing/ root). THE MISS: v4.99.60/61 verified exhaustively on localhost (python http.server serves literal /landing/diagnostic/index.html where `./` resolves fine) but NEVER on a prod/preview deploy — localhost-only verification has a structural blind spot for Vercel URL-rewrite behaviour. Added v4.99.62 class-of-bug UAT guard (feedback_class_of_bug_grep.md): no diagnostic page may link diagnostic-system.css via bare `./` (guards pattern across all 4 screens) + asserts picker uses `../diagnostic/`. 6291/6291. Fast-lane hotfix (one href). Lesson: future diagnostic/landing changes touching asset paths or routing MUST be verified on a Vercel preview deploy, not just localhost. |
