Also relabeled the two overlapping front-page cards: the `#todays-focus` chip row is now "🎯 Weak spots" (reactive wrong-bank drill, 1 tap) and the `#session-banner` card is now "📚 STUDY PLAN" / "Start Study Plan" (proactive 3-topic coverage plan) — removes the confusing today/today collision. Internal IDs, classes, and function names unchanged. Daily Challenge card now hides entirely once done for the day (previously showed a "complete" state that lingered until tomorrow). Study Plan banner now hides once addressed for the day — new `isStudyPlanDoneToday()` helper returns true if any history entry today has `mode: 'session'` OR if every topic in today's plan has been drilled individually today. Both banners come back automatically at the next day rollover. **Topic Progress v2** — rewrote the `#page-progress` page: summary strip at top with counts by bucket (Strong/Solid/Weak/Untouched) + coverage bar (`touched/40`), toolbar with search input + filter chips (All/Weak/Untouched/Strong) + sort select (Worst first/Most recent/Most studied/A–Z), topics now grouped into 5 collapsible N10-009 domain sections via `TOPIC_DOMAINS` showing the domain's exam weight + average % in the header, objective badges (e.g. `1.2`) pulled from `topicResources` on each row. New `progressState` object holds filter/sort/search + cached rows; new helpers `_buildProgressRows`, `_sortProgressRows`, `_progressRowMatches`, `_progressRowHtml`, `_renderProgressSummary`, `_renderProgressGrouped`, `_bucketOf`; new public fns `setProgressFilter`, `setProgressSort`, `filterProgressPage`. Topic rows on Topic Progress now render the **short chip label** (from the setup page chip `textContent`) instead of the canonical `data-v` id — previously users saw unfamiliar long names like "Integrating Networked Devices" that didn't match the "Networked Devices & IoT" chip on the setup page. Canonical id still used for history lookups/drilling/grouping; only the display text changed. **Explanation panel + Deep Dive panel restyled to forced white background + black text** (hardcoded `#ffffff` / `#111111`, bypassing theme vars) with a colored left-border accent (green/red/purple) to preserve correct/wrong/deep-dive signaling — user feedback that their brain reads white-on-black explanations better than the green/red tinted dark panels. `drillTopic()` now also opens the target chip's collapsed domain `<details>` accordion on the setup page, `scrollIntoView` centers it, and applies a temporary `.chip-flash` animation (1.3s pulse + scale) so you can see exactly where you landed — previously tapping a Topic Progress row would select a chip invisibly hidden inside a closed accordion. Extended the same click-through behavior to the analytics page's **Topic Mastery** and **Priority Study Areas** sections — both now render rows with `ana-row-clickable` class + `onclick="drillTopic(...)"` so tapping any row jumps straight to setup with the chip selected, accordion opened, scrolled, and flashing. **Tech debt #18 fixed**: split 3 long functions out of the >80-line bucket. Extracted `renderResultsDifficultyBreakdown()` from `finish()` (84 → ~65 lines), `buildTopicDivePrompt()` from `showTopicDeepDive()` (83 → ~60 lines), and `buildWeightedTopicMap(historyEntries, now)` from `getReadinessScore()` (86 → ~73 lines). Long-function count: **11 → 8** (under tightened threshold of 8). Remaining offenders for a future refactor session: `fetchQuestions` (120), `submitExam` (117), `evaluateMilestones` (89), `renderCliSim` (92), `renderTopology` (131), `aiValidateQuestions` (86), `explainFurther` (101), and the elephant `renderAnalytics` (482). **Tech debt #17 fixed**: bulk-replaced inline `.style.*` assignments with CSS utility classes — `.is-hidden { display: none !important; }` and `.is-dimmed { opacity: 0.5 }`. All 74 `style.display = 'none'/'block'/'flex'/''` toggles became `classList.add/remove/toggle('is-hidden', ...)`, all 7 `style.opacity = '0.5'/'1'` toggles became `classList.toggle('is-dimmed', ...)`. Inline `.style.*` count: **117 → 37** (-80). Tech-debt monitor threshold tightened from 105 to 50 to lock in the win. **Important follow-up**: 17 elements in `index.html` had `style="display:none"` baked into the markup; a `classList.remove('is-hidden')` from JS could not override that inline style, so Playwright tests waiting on `subnet-next-btn`, `port-game`, `weak-banner`, `stats-card`, etc. all failed. All 17 inline `display:none` HTML attributes were converted to `class="... is-hidden"` so JS toggling actually shows the elements. Lesson: when you migrate JS toggling to a CSS class, audit the matching HTML markup for inline styles that would shadow the class. **Tech debt #23 fixed**: dead code sweep — removed unreferenced `ipToArr()` helper (replaced long ago by `octetsFromIp()`-style usage) and the unused `N10_009_OBJECTIVE_RE` constant (the actual objective validation in `validateQuestions` uses an inline substring regex with capture group for "Obj 1.4" / "1.4 — Routing" extraction, so the anchored constant was a duplicate that drifted out of use). UAT swapped the `N10_009_OBJECTIVE_RE` symbol assertion for a regex-pattern presence check. **Tech debt #20 fixed**: service worker now hard-caps the runtime cache at `CACHE_MAX_ENTRIES = 60` and FIFO-evicts via a new `trimCache()` helper called after every `cache.put`. 5xx responses are no longer cached and now fall back to the cached copy when available, so a Vercel/origin outage no longer breaks the offline app. Cache bumped to `netplus-v4.11.8`. **Tech debt #24 fixed**: new `.github/workflows/auto-add-to-board.yml` listens for `issues.opened/labeled/reopened` and adds the issue to Board #1 (`tech-debt`/`bug`) or Board #2 (`feature-idea`) via GraphQL `addProjectV2ItemById`. Replaces the unreliable built-in GitHub Projects auto-add workflow that did not fire for REST-API-created issues. Project IDs are looked up at runtime by project number so they're never hardcoded. Both this workflow and the existing tech-debt issue-creation block in `ci.yml` now use `secrets.PROJECT_TOKEN || secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN` — `PROJECT_TOKEN` is a PAT with the `project` scope (the default `GITHUB_TOKEN` lacks it, which is why the existing inline GraphQL block in the tech-debt job had been silently failing) |
| v4.12 | **Port Drill family multi-select questions (#27)** — added a third question type alongside the existing "what port?" / "what protocol?" single-answer prompts. `nextPortQ()` now rolls 40/40/20 and the 20% bucket calls `nextPortFamilyQ()`, which picks a random eligible category from `portCategories` (filtered by `getFamilyEligibleCategories()` to families with ≥2 protocols, so Routing and Database are skipped) and renders all members of the family + 4 distractors as toggle buttons. New helpers `togglePortFamilyPick(btn)` flips the `.port-opt-selected` class on a button, and `submitPortFamilyAnswer()` reads the selected set and compares to `correctKeys`. Scoring is **exact match** — every correct port selected and no incorrect ones; partial matches count as wrong. Same penalties as single-Q (`-1s` in timed, ends run in endless). Per-port adaptive-focus stats are updated for every option in the family Q (correct+picked → positive, correct+missed → negative, wrong+picked → negative). `portMissed.push()` for family wrongs records `mode:'family'`, `proto:'<Family> family'`, `port:'<comma-separated correct ports>'`, `tp:'multi'`; the missed-review renderer in `endPortDrill()` branches on `m.mode === 'family'` to display the row without the standard `port/tp` formatting. UI: prompt uses one of three randomized phrasings ("Select all N ports used by the X family" / "Which N ports are X-related?" / "Pick the N ports that belong to the X family") with the count hint included to mirror CompTIA exam style. New CSS: `.port-opt-multi` (flex layout for port + protocol label), `.port-opt-selected` (accent border + tinted background), `.port-submit-family` (full-width accent submit button spanning the grid). Cache bumped to `netplus-v4.12.0` |
| v4.13 | **Hardcore exam mode (#48)** — optional checkbox under the "Simulate Full Exam" button that flips the exam page into a stricter, no-safety-net configuration. Persisted in `localStorage` (`STORAGE.HARDCORE_EXAM`) so the choice sticks across sessions; new `setHardcoreMode(on)` writes the pref. `startExam()` reads the flag at exam start, sets `examHardcore`, and toggles a `hardcore-active` class on `#page-exam`. CSS hides the flag button (`#exam-flag-btn`) and the question navigator (`#qnav-toggle` + `#qnav-grid`) entirely, and `visibility:hidden`s the Prev button so layout doesn't shift. JS guards in `examPrev()`, `examToggleFlag()`, and `toggleNav()` early-return when `examHardcore` so the F-key shortcut and any leftover handlers also no-op. History entries from hardcore runs include `hardcore: true` for analytics. Final exam-results hero shows a `🔥 HARDCORE` badge next to PASS/FAIL when the run was hardcore. New milestone `hardcore_pass` (icon 🔥): score 720+ in hardcore mode — evaluated in `evaluateMilestones()` against `e.hardcore && scaled >= 720`. Time limit deliberately unchanged (issue notes "shorter time?" needs discussion). Cache bumped to `netplus-v4.13.0` |
| v4.25.0 | **Cloud Networking — VPCs, Transit Gateways, IPSec VPN, Security Groups, NACLs, SASE** — massive cloud networking expansion to the topology builder, bringing cloud infrastructure concepts alongside the existing L2/L3 simulation. (1) **8 new device types**: VPC (dashed border grid icon), Cloud Subnet (/24 segment), Internet Gateway (globe with bidirectional arrows), NAT Gateway (address translation box), Transit Gateway (hub with 4 radiating connections), VPN Gateway (lock with dashed tunnel lines), On-Prem Data Center (building with rack servers), SASE Edge (shield with cloud inset). All get SVG icons, interface defaults (eni/att/tun/zt naming), and full config panels. (2) **Security Groups tab** (stateful, instance-level): add/remove security groups with named rulesets, each containing inbound/outbound rules with protocol/port/source-dest CIDR/action. Allow-only with implicit deny-all. CRUD: `tbAddSecurityGroup`, `tbRemoveSecurityGroup`, `tbAddSgRule`, `tbRemoveSgRule`, `tbSetSgField`, `tbSetSgRuleField`. Visible on all cloud devices + server + PC. (3) **NACLs tab** (stateless, subnet-level): numbered rules sorted by rule number (first match wins), inbound/outbound sections with allow/deny actions, implicit deny-all shown as greyed-out row. CRUD: `tbAddNaclRule`, `tbRemoveNaclRule`, `tbSetNaclField`. Visible only on cloud-subnet. (4) **VPC Config tab**: CIDR block input with size hint, DNS Support/Hostnames/Flow Logs toggles, tenancy selector (default/dedicated), connected subnets list auto-populated from cable graph. (5) **VPN/IPSec tab**: full tunnel configuration — peer IP, pre-shared key (password field), IKE version (v1/v2), encryption (AES-128/AES-256/3DES), hash algorithm (SHA-1/SHA-256/SHA-384), DH group (2/5/14/19/20), local/remote subnet selectors, tunnel status indicator (green UP/red DOWN/yellow NEGOTIATING). `tbNegotiateVpn()` finds the peer device via cable graph and calls `tbCheckVpnTunnel()` which validates all 5 crypto parameters match + PSK match — mismatches produce specific error messages. Success triggers green packet animation, failure triggers red. (6) **SASE tab**: ZTNA policy dropdown (verify-always/verify-once/disabled), SWG toggle, CASB toggle, MFA toggle, Identity Provider input, FWaaS rules table (protocol/port/source/action with add/remove). (7) **Simulation helpers**: `tbCidrContains(cidr, ip)` for CIDR matching, `tbEvalSecurityGroups(dev, proto, port, ip, dir)` evaluates SG rules (stateful — return traffic auto-allowed), `tbEvalNacl(dev, proto, port, ip, dir)` evaluates NACL rules (stateless — lowest rule number first match wins). (8) **4 new CLI commands**: `show security-groups`/`show sg`, `show nacl`/`show network-acl`, `show vpn-status`/`show vpn`/`show crypto`, `show sase`/`show ztna`. Help command updated. (9) **6 new grading rules**: `igw-on-vpc` (critical), `nat-gw-needs-subnet` (warning), `vpg-has-peer` (warning), `tgw-connects-vpcs` (info), `cloud-has-sg` (warning), `subnet-has-nacl` (info). (10) **4 new scenarios**: Cloud VPC Architecture, Hybrid Cloud (VPN), Multi-VPC with Transit Gateway, SASE Architecture. (11) **2 new guided labs**: Cloud VPC with Security Controls (7 steps, intermediate), SASE & Zero Trust Architecture (6 steps, advanced). (12) **State migration**: 5 new per-device properties (`securityGroups`, `nacls`, `vpcConfig`, `vpnConfig`, `saseConfig`) with backward-compat defaults. Config tab bar made horizontally scrollable for 11 total tabs. CSS: `.tb-sg-table`, `.tb-sg-row-allow`, `.tb-nacl-row-deny`, `.tb-cloud-card`, `.tb-cloud-card-head`, `.tb-cloud-badge`. 69 new UAT assertions (996 total). Cache bumped to `netplus-v4.25.0`. |
| v4.29.0 | **Interactive Labs v3, 5 New Labs, Builder Visual Feedback** — major interactivity upgrade across the topology builder. (1) **Live lab step validation**: `tbSaveDraft()` now calls `tbRenderLabStep()` on every state change, so when you configure an IP, add a gateway, create a VLAN, or disable a port, the lab panel updates **instantly** — no need to click Next to see if your step is complete. Step completion tracking via `_completedSteps` Set prevents re-triggering celebrations. (2) **Step completion celebration**: when a step transitions from incomplete to complete, the check mark animates with a scale+glow effect (`tb-lab-step-just-completed` CSS animation). The Next button pulses green (`tb-lab-next-ready` with `tbNextPulse` keyframes) to draw attention when ready to advance. Pending text updated to "updates live as you work" so users know the panel is reactive. (3) **Cable status coloring**: cables now show connectivity health — green (`tb-cable-healthy`) when both endpoints have IPs (or are switches/clouds which don't need IPs), amber (`tb-cable-partial`) when only one end is configured, default color when neither has IPs. Computed per-render by checking `interfaces.find(i => i.cableId === c.id)` for IP presence. (4) **Device health badges**: small colored dots on the top-right of each device showing config status — green (fully configured: endpoint has IP+gateway, router has IP, switch has cable), amber (partially configured: has IP but missing gateway), red (cabled but no config). Uses SVG `<circle>` with class `tb-health-badge`. (5) **5 new labs** (11 total): **Static Routing Between Subnets** (obj 1.4, intermediate, 7 steps) — build 2-router, 2-switch, 4-PC network, configure /30 backbone, add static routes for cross-subnet reachability. **ACL Traffic Filtering** (obj 4.3, advanced, 5 steps) — build Internet→Firewall→LAN, configure HTTP/HTTPS allow rules, implicit deny-all. **Site-to-Site VPN** (obj 3.3, advanced, 5 steps, autoSetup) — pre-built 2-DC + 2-VPG + cloud topology, configure matching IPSec crypto params, negotiate tunnel, verify with `show vpn`. **Wireless Network with WLC** (obj 2.4, intermediate, 5 steps) — deploy WLC + 3 WAPs + router + switch, add uncabled wireless clients, covers CAPWAP/802.11ax/WPA3 exam concepts. **Cloud VPC with Security Groups** (obj 1.8, advanced, 6 steps) — VPC + IGW + 2 subnets + NAT GW + servers with security groups, covers stateful vs stateless and SG vs NACL. **Network Hardening** (obj 4.1, intermediate, 6 steps, autoSetup) — pre-built insecure network, create management VLAN 99, disable 10+ unused ports, add IDS + firewall, defense-in-depth checklist. 33 new UAT assertions (1124 total). Cache bumped to `netplus-v4.29.0`. |
| v4.33.0 | **Analytics + Topic Progress redesign** — functional UI improvements across both data pages, zero fluff. **Analytics page**: (1) **Sticky section nav** — 6 jump-pill buttons (Readiness/Trend/Topics/Activity/Drills/Milestones) sticky at top, eliminates scroll-hunting across 13 cards. CSS `.ana-nav` + `.ana-nav-pill` with overflow-x scroll on mobile. (2) **Removed 3 redundant cards** — Weekly Volume (redundant with 30-day calendar), All-Time Stats (merged into hero), Priority Study Areas (merged into Topic Mastery). Net -3 cards, ~40% less scrolling. (3) **All-Time Stats merged into hero** — 4-stat strip (Sessions/Questions/Accuracy/Study Days) now renders inside the Readiness hero card as `.ana-hero-stats` grid below the domain breakdown. (4) **Priority Areas merged into Topic Mastery** — weak topics (<70%) now render in a highlighted `.ana-topic-alert` box at the top of the Topic Mastery card with amber border, separated from strong topics below. Removes the standalone Priority card while making weak topics MORE visible. (5) **2-column grid** — Streak, Weak Spots, Difficulty Breakdown, Heatmap, Question Types, and Exam vs Quiz cards now sit in `.ana-grid-2col` (2-column on ≥700px, 1-column on mobile), cutting scroll distance ~30% on desktop. Section IDs (`ana-s-readiness` etc.) on all major cards for nav targeting. **Topic Progress page**: (6) **Redesigned summary strip** — replaced 5 separate bordered cards with a single clean card containing a `.ps-row` flex strip (Strong/Solid/Weak/Untouched/Coverage) with divider borders instead of separate boxes. Coverage bar sits below as a full-width progress bar. Lab section uses matching `.ps-lab-row` flex layout. Much cleaner than the old boxy grid. (7) **Play button on topic rows** — circular accent `▶` button (`.topic-play-btn`) on each topic row that calls `focusTopic()` directly to start a quiz, bypassing the setup page entirely. `event.stopPropagation()` prevents the row's `drillTopic` click from also firing. (8) **Domain header mini-bars** — 60px progress bar (`.pd-bar` + `.pd-bar-fill`) on each domain `<summary>` header showing the domain's average accuracy at a glance, colored green/yellow/red. 35 new UAT assertions (1442 total). Cache bumped to `netplus-v4.33.0`. |
| v4.32.0 | **Setup page restructure — nav toolbar, Today section, collapsible Custom Quiz** — complete overhaul of the home page layout to reduce scrolling and improve discoverability as the app has grown to 15+ sections. (1) **Today section**: all conditional daily cards (Daily Goal ring, Streak Defender, Daily Challenge, Weak Spots, Study Plan, Weak Banner) grouped inside a single `#today-section` wrapper card with a "Today" heading — gives them a visual home and eliminates layout jank from independent show/hide. `renderTodaySection()` manages wrapper visibility. (2) **Nav toolbar**: 5-button horizontal `<nav class="setup-nav">` bar (Progress, Subnet, Port Drill, Analytics, Builder) replaces the scattered bottom buttons — tools are now accessible without scrolling past 40 topic chips. Responsive: wraps to 2-row grid on narrow viewports. (3) **Quick Start + Marathon presets**: two preset grids now have clear `<h4>` headings ("Quick Start" / "Marathon Mode") inside a single `.presets-section` wrapper. Uses `grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(130px, 1fr))` for responsive fit. (4) **Wrong Answers preset tile**: new `.preset-wrong` tile surfaces inside Quick Start row when wrong bank has entries — shows count (e.g. "12 wrong answers saved"), replaces the buried full-width button. `renderWrongBankBtn()` updated to populate both the legacy row and the new tile. Red gradient styling via `.preset-wrong` CSS. (5) **Collapsible Custom Quiz**: Topic chips, Difficulty, Questions, and Generate button moved inside `<details id="custom-quiz-section">` with "Custom Quiz" summary — power users can expand, casual users just tap presets. Difficulty/Questions cards inside a 2-column grid within the body. `#setup-err` moved inside for error visibility. (6) **Auto-open on error**: new `showSetupError()` helper auto-opens both Custom Quiz and Advanced sections and scrolls to the error box when API key validation fails — called from `startQuiz()`, `startExam()`, `startBulkQuiz()`, `startSession()`. (7) **drillTopic()` updated**: now opens `#custom-quiz-section` before scrolling to the selected chip. (8) **Exam section**: Simulate Full Exam + Hardcore toggle grouped in `.exam-section` div. (9) **History panel relocated** below exam section for better context. (10) **Lab difficulty counts horizontal fix**: removed `flex-wrap:wrap` from lab stats container so Beginner/Intermediate/Advanced cards always sit in a single horizontal row. 37 new UAT assertions (1412 total). Cache bumped to `netplus-v4.32.0`. |
| v4.31.1 | **Fix quiz question count bug** — Quiz sometimes generated 1 or 6 questions instead of the requested 5. Root cause: AI returns variable question counts, and the validation pipeline (`aiValidateQuestions` + `validateQuestions`) filters additional questions with no count enforcement. Fix: after validation, truncate to `qCount` if AI returned extras; if too many filtered out (<50% remaining), auto-retry with a fresh `fetchQuestions` call to backfill. Also enforces count cap on bulk quiz mode and cached fallback path. Cache bumped to `netplus-v4.31.1`. |
| v4.31.0 | **A+ Topology Builder — Packet Inspection, STP Convergence, QoS Enforcement, BGP/EIGRP, Attack Scenarios, DNSSEC** — 6-feature expansion achieving A+ grade on the topology builder. (1) **BGP routing protocol**: full BGP tab on routers with ASN, router ID, neighbor management (eBGP/iBGP), network advertisement, and live peer negotiation with route exchange animation. `tbNegotiateBgp()` validates reciprocal neighbors, AS matching, and exchanges routes with AS_PATH. 8 new CLI commands: `show ip bgp`, `show ip bgp summary`, `show ip eigrp neighbors`, `show ip eigrp topology`. Show running-config includes BGP/EIGRP blocks. (2) **EIGRP routing protocol**: EIGRP tab on routers with AS number, network/wildcard configuration, live neighbor discovery from cable graph. DUAL algorithm concepts in educational notes. (3) **Packet inspection panel**: Wireshark-lite `#tb-packet-inspect` panel shows L2 (MAC, EtherType, 802.1Q), L3 (IP, protocol, TTL), L4 (ports, flags) headers during packet animation. `tbBuildPacketHeaders()` constructs headers from device interfaces. TTL decrements and MAC addresses swap at each hop. Ping and ARP simulations now pass packet info to `tbAnimatePacket()`. (4) **STP convergence animation**: `tbRunStpConvergence()` performs root bridge election (`tbCalcRootBridge` by priority then MAC), calculates port roles (`tbCalcPortRoles` — root/designated/blocking), animates BPDU exchange, and applies port states with timed transitions. "Run Convergence" button on STP tab. New `show spanning-tree detail` CLI command. (5) **QoS enforcement**: `tbQosClassify()` matches packets against policies by protocol/port, `tbQosEnqueue()` returns queue-based delay factors (priority=0ms, best-effort=200ms). QoS classification runs during packet animation at each hop, updating the inspection panel. New `show qos counters` / `show qos queue` CLI commands. (6) **Attack scenarios**: Attack/Security tab on switches with DHCP Snooping (trusted/untrusted ports), DAI (Dynamic ARP Inspection), and Port Security (max MAC, violation mode). Three attack simulations: `tbSimArpSpoof()` (blocked by DAI), `tbSimVlanHopping()` (blocked by non-default native VLAN), `tbSimRogueDhcp()` (blocked by DHCP snooping). Red packet animations for attacks, green for defense blocks. New CLI: `show ip dhcp snooping`, `show ip arp inspection`. (7) **DNSSEC**: toggle on DNS tab auto-generates DNSKEY, RRSIG, and DS records. `tbValidateDnssecChain()` walks DNS servers validating chain of trust. New `dig +dnssec` CLI command shows AD flag and validation status. `show dnssec` shows signing status. (8) **6 new labs** (28 total): Packet Anatomy (obj 1.1, Beginner), STP Convergence (obj 2.1, Intermediate, autoSetup), QoS Voice Priority (obj 4.6, Intermediate), BGP Peering (obj 1.4, Advanced), Network Attack & Defense (obj 4.2, Intermediate), DNSSEC Chain of Trust (obj 1.6, Advanced). (9) **AI prompt updated**: ADVANCED FEATURES section expanded with bgpConfig, eigrpConfig, dnssecEnabled, dhcpSnooping, daiEnabled, portSecurity documentation + schema. `tbBuildFromAiPayload` copies all new properties. `tbExpandScenario` adds 11 new semantic patterns (BGP, eBGP, iBGP, EIGRP, AS, DNSSEC, ARP spoof, DHCP snooping, port security, network hardening). (10) **3 new grading rules**: `bgp-has-neighbor` (warning), `switch-has-snooping` (info), `dnssec-on-dns` (info). State migration: 6 new per-device properties (`bgpConfig`, `eigrpConfig`, `dnssecEnabled`, `dhcpSnooping`, `daiEnabled`, `portSecurity`). 115 new UAT assertions (1375 total). Cache bumped to `netplus-v4.31.0`. |
| v4.30.2 | **4 Beginner Labs, Lab Milestones & Progress Tracking** — (1) **4 new beginner labs** (22 total, 6 beginner): **IP Addressing 101** (obj 1.5, 10 min, 5 steps — build router+switch+2PCs, learn IPs/masks/gateways, test with ping), **Cable Types & Topologies** (obj 1.3, 8 min, 5 steps — build star topology using Fiber/Cat6/Cat5e/Console cables, learn cable specs and topology types), **Your First Firewall** (obj 4.2, 10 min, 5 steps — build Internet→Firewall→LAN, configure ACL rules, learn firewall types and zones), **Troubleshooting with CLI Tools** (obj 5.2, 12 min, 5 steps, autoSetup — pre-built 2-subnet network, use ipconfig/ping/traceroute/arp to diagnose, learn CompTIA 7-step methodology). (2) **Lab completion tracking**: `STORAGE.LAB_COMPLETIONS` (`nplus_lab_completions`) persists per-lab completion data (first/last completed timestamp, completion count). `tbEndLab()` writes to localStorage on every lab finish and calls `evaluateMilestones()`. (3) **4 lab milestones**: `first_lab` (🧪 Lab rat), `labs_5` (🔬 Lab regular), `labs_10` (🏗️ Lab master), `labs_all` (🧬 Lab completionist). Evaluated in `evaluateMilestones()` against `LAB_COMPLETIONS` keys count vs `TB_LABS.length`. (4) **Lab progress in Topic Progress page**: `_renderProgressSummary` now appends a lab section showing total labs completed with coverage bar, plus per-difficulty breakdown (🟢 Beginner / 🟡 Intermediate / 🔴 Advanced). 32 new UAT assertions (1260 total). Cache bumped to `netplus-v4.30.2`. |
| v4.30.1 | **Lab Device Highlighting** — when a lab step is active and not yet complete, devices that need attention pulse with an amber glow on the canvas. Auto-detects targets by extracting bold text (`**Router**`, `**SW-Core**`) from step instructions and matching against device hostnames and type labels — works across all 18 labs with zero per-step configuration. `tbRenderLabStep` extracts bold terms, matches them against `TB_DEVICE_TYPES` labels (including plurals like "3 Routers") and device hostnames, stores matched IDs in `tbActiveLab._highlightIds`. `tbRenderCanvas` applies `tb-device-lab-target` class to matched devices. CSS `@keyframes tbLabTargetPulse` animates a 1.5s amber glow cycle. Highlights automatically clear when the step passes. Triggers `tbRenderCanvas()` after every step render to sync highlights with step state. 9 new UAT assertions (1228 total). Cache bumped to `netplus-v4.30.1`. |
| v4.30.0 | **Advanced Networking — STP/RSTP, OSPF, QoS, Wireless, DNS Records, IPv6, CLI Config Mode** — 8-feature expansion adding deep L2/L3/application-layer protocols to the topology builder. (1) **DNS Server device type**: new `dns-server` in `TB_DEVICE_TYPES` (cyan `#06b6d4`, short "DNS") with 2x eth interfaces, custom SVG icon, full DNS record management tab. (2) **STP/RSTP tab**: mode selector (STP/RSTP/MSTP), bridge priority input, per-port state dropdowns (forwarding/blocking/learning/listening/disabled). CRUD: `tbSetStpField()`, `tbSetStpPortState()`. Visible on switches. (3) **OSPF tab**: enable checkbox, router ID, area management with network lists, live neighbor discovery. CRUD: `tbSetOspfField()`, `tbAddOspfArea()`, `tbRemoveOspfArea()`, `tbSetOspfAreaNetworks()`. Visible on routers. (4) **QoS tab**: enable checkbox, policy CRUD with DSCP markings (EF/AF/CS) and queue types (priority/bandwidth/fair/best-effort). CRUD: `tbSetQosField()`, `tbAddQosPolicy()`, `tbRemoveQosPolicy()`. Visible on routers and switches. (5) **Wireless tab**: SSID, security (WEP→WPA3), 802.11 mode (a/b/g/n/ac/ax), band (2.4/5/6 GHz), channel planning, TX power. CRUD: `tbSetWirelessField()`. Visible on WAP/WLC. (6) **DNS tab**: full record management with all 10 record types (A, AAAA, CNAME, MX, PTR, NS, SOA, TXT, SRV, CAA) each with name/value/TTL and detailed descriptions. CRUD: `tbAddDnsRecord()`, `tbRemoveDnsRecord()`, `tbSetDnsRecord()`. Visible on dns-server and server. (7) **IPv6 support**: dual-stack on all interfaces with IPv6 address + /64 prefix fields. `tbSetIfaceIpv6()` helper. Migration adds `ipv6`/`ipv6Prefix` defaults. (8) **15 new CLI commands**: `show spanning-tree`, `show ip ospf`, `show ip ospf neighbor`, `show qos`/`show policy-map`, `show wireless`/`show ap`, `show dns records`/`show zone`, `nslookup <name>`/`dig <name>` (queries DNS servers in topology), `show ipv6 interface`, `show ipv6 route`, `configure terminal`/`config t`, `hostname <name>` (actually changes device hostname), `ip route <net> <mask> <nh>` (actually adds static route), `show running-config`/`show run` (generates full IOS-style config output). (9) **AI prompt updated**: ADVANCED FEATURES section with dns-server, stpConfig, ospfConfig, qosConfig, wirelessConfig, IPv6 documentation. Schema updated. `tbBuildFromAiPayload` copies all new properties. Semantic expansion updated with DNS/OSPF/STP/QoS/IPv6/dual-stack patterns. (10) **3 new labs** (14 total): OSPF Dynamic Routing (obj 1.4, Advanced, 18 min, 5 steps — build 3-router triangle, configure IPs, enable OSPF, set router IDs, verify neighbors), DNS Infrastructure & Records (obj 1.6, Intermediate, 15 min, 6 steps — build DNS server, configure all record types, test with nslookup), STP Loop Prevention (obj 2.1, Intermediate, 12 min, 4 steps, autoSetup — 3-switch triangle, set root bridge, block redundant port, verify). State migration: 5 new per-device properties (`stpConfig`, `ospfConfig`, `qosConfig`, `wirelessConfig`, `dnsRecords`) with backward-compat defaults. 95 new UAT assertions (1219 total). Cache bumped to `netplus-v4.30.0`. |
| v4.28.1 | **AI Generation Reliability Fix** — fixes "invalid topology" error when generating complex scenarios like "2 data centres connected via VPN". Root causes: (1) `max_tokens` was 4096 — complex topologies with VPN configs routinely exceeded this, causing truncated JSON that the parser couldn't handle. Bumped to 8192. (2) No retry mechanism — a single parse failure immediately showed an error. Now uses a two-attempt strategy: first attempt with full prompt, second attempt with a simplified focused prompt if the first fails. (3) Parser couldn't handle truncated JSON — added Stage 5 truncation repair that counts open/close braces/brackets and attempts to close them to salvage partial results. (4) Semantic expansion was too verbose — "data centres" expanded into a paragraph-long description that overwhelmed Haiku. Shortened all 16 expansions to concise device-type references. (5) "Output ONLY valid JSON" rule was buried at the end of the prompt (Rule 12) — moved to the very first line of the base prompt so the AI sees it before the lengthy device guide. (6) No debugging — added `console.log` for raw AI response text, stop_reason, and response length so truncation and parse failures are diagnosable in the browser console. New `_tbCallAiAndParse()` helper encapsulates the fetch+parse+logging cycle. 10 new UAT assertions (1091 total). Cache bumped to `netplus-v4.28.1`. |
| v4.28.0 | **VXLAN Support, Deep AI Generation Pipeline, Cloud Property Fix** — three major areas. (1) **Critical bug fix**: `tbBuildFromAiPayload()` was silently dropping all cloud properties (securityGroups, nacls, vpcConfig, vpnConfig, saseConfig) from AI-generated topologies — they were never copied from the AI response to the device object. Fixed by adding explicit copies of all 6 cloud/overlay properties. This is why "2 data centres connected via VPN" previously failed. (2) **VXLAN overlay networking**: new `vxlanConfig` array on all devices (migration defaults to `[]`). Each entry: `{ vni, vtepIp, mappedVlan, mcastGroup, remoteVteps[], floodAndLearn, bgpEvpn }`. New **VXLAN tab** on switches and routers (`tbRenderVxlanTab`) with full CRUD: `tbAddVxlan()`, `tbRemoveVxlan(idx)`, `tbSetVxlanField(idx, field, value)`. VNI range 1-16777215, VTEP source IP, mapped VLAN ID, multicast group, remote VTEP list, flood-and-learn toggle, BGP EVPN toggle. New CLI command `show vxlan` / `show nve` / `show vxlan vtep`. Overview tab shows VXLAN tunnel count. AI prompt updated with VXLAN SUPPORT section and vxlanConfig in JSON schema. (3) **Deep AI generation pipeline** — two-phase approach: **Phase 1** (semantic expansion): `tbExpandScenario()` preprocesses the user's description, mapping 16 shorthand patterns to explicit device types and configs (e.g. "data centre" → "onprem-dc", "VPN tunnel" → "vpg with vpnConfig", "fabric" → "spine-leaf with VXLAN", "spine-leaf" → exact topology description, "3-tier" → "core→distribution→access", "HA" → "redundant dual devices"). **Phase 2** (deep validation): `tbDeepValidateAndFix(state)` runs 6 auto-fix passes after generation: (a) auto-assign IPs to cabled router interfaces missing them, (b) auto-set gateways on endpoints by walking cables to nearest router, (c) auto-initialize + sync VPN crypto params on VPG↔DC pairs, (d) auto-initialize vpcConfig on VPC devices, (e) spread devices stuck at default (400,400) across canvas, (f) add cross-subnet static routes between routers with shared links. Fix count shown in status bar. CSS: `.tb-vxlan-row`, `.tb-vxlan-row-head`. 34 new UAT assertions (1081 total). Cache bumped to `netplus-v4.28.0`. |
| v4.27.0 | **AI Add-to-Existing Topology, Interactive Labs v2** — two major feature areas. (1) **AI "Add to Existing" mode**: when devices exist on the canvas, `tbGenerateAiTopology()` now offers a choice — type `ADD` to merge new devices into the existing topology, or `NEW` to start fresh. ADD mode serializes the current topology via `tbSerializeForAiContext()` (device list with hostnames, types, positions, IPs + cable list) and includes it in the AI prompt with EXISTING TOPOLOGY context, occupied canvas region analysis, hostname conflict warnings, and device cap calculation (`50 - existing`). The AI response is merged into `tbState` via `tbBuildFromAiPayload()` with pre-populated `hostnameToId` mapping so cables can reference existing devices. Refactored: extracted `tbParseAiTopologyJson()` (JSON parsing with 4-stage fallback), `tbBuildFromAiPayload()` (device + cable builder), and `tbAiBasePrompt()` (shared prompt template) as reusable helpers. (2) **Interactive Labs v2**: all 9 labs (7 existing rewritten + 2 new) now have: **hints** (collapsible `💡 Show Hint` button with exam-relevant tips via `tbToggleLabHint()`), **detailed feedback** (`feedback(state)` function that explains exactly what's missing/wrong, e.g. "PC2 is still on 192.168.2.20 — wrong subnet"), **progress bar** (gradient fill showing step completion percentage), and richer multi-paragraph instructions with networking theory woven into every step. New labs: **"Troubleshoot: Why Can't I Ping?"** (obj 5.3, intermediate) — `autoSetup` pre-builds a broken network (PC2 on wrong subnet 192.168.2.x, PC3 with wrong gateway 192.168.1.254) and the student must diagnose with CLI tools (`ipconfig`, `ping`) and fix the issues. **"Multi-Site WAN with ISP"** (obj 1.2, advanced) — build 3 office sites connected through an ISP Router with fiber WAN links, configure static routing on the ISP for cross-site reachability, test with traceroute. Lab picker now shows "Pre-built" badge for labs with `autoSetup`. `tbStartLab` calls `autoSetup(state)` + `tbMigrateState()` before rendering. `tbActiveLab` now tracks `hintsUsed`. CSS: `.tb-lab-step-feedback`, `.tb-lab-hint`, `.tb-lab-hint-toggle`, `.tb-lab-hint-body`, `.tb-lab-progress-bar`, `.tb-lab-progress-fill`, `.tb-lab-badge-auto`. 32 new UAT assertions (1047 total). Cache bumped to `netplus-v4.27.0`. |
| v4.26.0 | **ISP Router, WAN icon, VPC Peering, Routing Tables in Overview, AI Generate v2** — (1) **ISP Router device type**: new `isp-router` in `TB_DEVICE_TYPES` (label "ISP Router", color `#818cf8`, short "ISP") with 6x Gi0/x interfaces, custom SVG icon (rack with port lights + crossing arrows + ISP label), included in `isRouter` checks for routing tab visibility. (2) **Internet/WAN icon redesign**: `cloud` device renamed from "Cloud" to "Internet/WAN" with short label "WAN", color changed to `#60a5fa`, SVG icon replaced with globe (circle + latitude/longitude arc lines + "WAN" text). (3) **VPC Peering**: `tbAddVpcPeering()` creates bidirectional peering between two VPC devices (both sides get a `peerings` entry with `peerId` + `status: 'active'`), `tbRemoveVpcPeering(idx)` removes from both sides. UI in VPC Config tab shows status dots (green active/red pending), peer dropdown filtered to exclude already-peered VPCs. (4) **Routing Table in Overview**: all devices now show their routing table as a scrollable monospace block in the Overview tab, with route count in stats grid. Cloud stats section extended with peering count. (5) **AI Generate prompt v2**: completely rewritten expert-level prompt with full DEVICE TYPE GUIDE (all 29+ types explained), TOPOLOGY TYPES section (exact coordinate placement patterns for star/bus/ring/mesh/star-bus/point-to-point), full JSON schema including routingTable/securityGroups/vpcConfig/vpnConfig, 12 detailed rules, max 50 devices. `max_tokens` bumped from 3000 to 4096 for complex topologies. Prompt dialog shows usage examples. 19 new UAT assertions (1015 total). Cache bumped to `netplus-v4.26.0`. |
| v4.24.0 | **Network Simulator: Explain modal, CLI commands, AI topology improvements** — three fixes and enhancements. (1) **Explain button fix**: clicking Explain on a device's config panel previously sent output to the hidden sim log panel — users saw nothing happen. Replaced with a proper modal (`#tb-explain-modal`) that opens immediately with a loading spinner, then renders the AI walkthrough in a styled overlay with amber gradient header showing device hostname and type. New `tbCloseExplainModal()` helper. (2) **New CLI commands**: `traceroute <ip>` / `tracert <ip>` — hop-by-hop path trace using routing tables with loop detection, random RTT values, max 30 hops. `ipconfig` / `ifconfig` — shows all interfaces with IP, mask, MAC, gateway, VLAN, mode, link status, and DHCP server info. `netstat` / `netstat -an` / `ss -tuln` — simulates listening ports based on device type (HTTP/HTTPS on web servers, SSH/FTP on file servers, DHCP on DHCP servers, SNMP on routers, SIP on VoIP, IPP on printers) plus ESTABLISHED connections from ARP table entries. `help` / `?` — lists all available commands. Unknown command error now says "Type help" instead of listing commands inline. (3) **AI topology generation**: prompt now explicitly supports topology type requests (star, bus, ring, mesh, star-bus, hybrid, point-to-point) so asking for "star bus topology" no longer confuses the AI. Added JSON comment stripping fallback (`// ...` removal + trailing comma cleanup) for when the AI returns slightly malformed JSON. Error message improved to suggest simpler descriptions. 17 new UAT assertions (927 total). Cache bumped to `netplus-v4.24.0`. |
| v4.23.1 | **Hotfix: double-click device to open config panel** — root cause: `tbOnDeviceMouseDown` called `e.preventDefault()` which suppresses the browser's native `dblclick` event, AND `tbRenderCanvas()` destroys/recreates all device DOM nodes after every mousedown, so the second click of a double-click lands on a freshly created element that never saw the first click. Fix: replaced the unreliable native `dblclick` listener (`tbAttachDoubleClick`) with a manual timestamp-based double-click detector inside `tbOnDeviceMouseDown` — tracks `tbLastClickDevId` + `tbLastClickTime`, fires `tbOpenConfigPanel(id)` if same device clicked within 400ms. Removed dead `tbAttachDoubleClick` function and its call site in `tbRenderCanvas`. Cache bumped to `netplus-v4.23.1`. |
| v4.23.0 | **Network Simulator v2 — Device GUI, VLAN Trunking/DTP, Guided Labs, Ping UX** — four feature upgrades to the network simulator. (1) **Device Overview GUI**: new "Overview" tab is now the default when double-clicking a device — shows a visual dashboard with device icon hero, editable hostname, port status LED strip (green=connected+IP, yellow=connected no IP, grey=down), stats grid (routes/ARP/VLANs/MAC table/DHCP status), connected interface cards with IP/MAC/mode/peer info, and quick-action buttons to jump to specific tabs. Replaces the raw form-first experience with a Cisco-like device management screen. (2) **VLAN Trunking/Access/DTP**: Interfaces tab now has full L2 switching config — mode column (Access/Trunk), DTP mode dropdown (On/Desirable/Auto/NoNeg) on switches, and when a port is set to Trunk mode, an inline detail row expands showing Allowed VLANs (comma-separated input) and Native VLAN. New `tbSetTrunkAllowed()` helper. Mode change auto-refreshes the tab to show/hide trunk details. (3) **Guided Topology Labs**: 5 step-by-step labs with real-time topology checks — Basic LAN Setup (1.2, beginner, 7 steps), VLAN Segmentation (2.1, intermediate, 7 steps), DHCP Server & Relay (1.6, intermediate, 6 steps), DMZ & Firewall Design (4.5, advanced, 6 steps), ARP & MAC Learning (2.1, beginner, 5 steps). Lab picker modal (`#tb-lab-picker`) with difficulty badges + objective + duration. Active lab panel (`#tb-lab-panel`) slides in below sim toolbar with step title, markdown-formatted instructions (bold + code), real-time check (validates topology state against step condition), back/next navigation with progress counter. Labs button added to sim toolbar. (4) **Ping/ARP UX**: destination changed from free-text IP input to device dropdown (filtered to exclude source device via `tbFilterPingDst`). Ping and ARP buttons merged into single dialog with both actions. New `tbExecArp()` function with blue packet animation. 36 new UAT assertions (910 total). Cache bumped to `netplus-v4.23.0`. **Hotfix**: config panel grid layout — `.tb-workspace` grid was 2-column (`200px 1fr`), so the config panel rendered off-screen. Added `:has(.tb-config-panel:not(.is-hidden))` + `.tb-config-open` JS fallback to expand to 3 columns (`200px 1fr 360px`). Fixed SVG icon in Overview tab (wrapped `tbDeviceIcon` output in `<svg>` element). |
| v4.22.0 | **Network Simulator — full L2/L3 simulation engine for Topology Builder** — transforms the visual topology builder into an interactive network simulator. (1) **Foundation**: device limit 50, per-device interface model (`{name, cableId, ip, mask, mac, vlan, mode, trunkAllowed, gateway, enabled, subInterfaces}`), MAC auto-generation via djb2-style hash (`tbGenerateMac`), auto-hostname (`tbAutoHostname`), per-device-type interface templates (`TB_IFACE_DEFAULTS` for 17 types), `tbMigrateState()` backward-compat migration on every load, cable-interface binding in `tbAddCable`/`tbUnbindCable`. (2) **Config Panel**: slide-out panel (`#tb-config-panel`) opens on double-click via `tbAttachDoubleClick()` wired into `tbRenderCanvas()`. Five tabs: Interfaces (edit IP/mask/VLAN/mode/gateway per port), Routing (view connected routes, add static routes), VLANs (add/remove VLANs on switches), DHCP (enable server pools, configure relay/helper-address on routers/servers), CLI (interactive terminal with `show arp`, `show ip route`, `show mac address-table`, `show vlan brief`, `show interfaces`, `show ip interface brief`, `ping <ip>`, `arp <ip>`). Tab visibility context-sensitive: routing only on routers/firewalls, VLANs only on switches, DHCP only on routers/servers. (3) **Subnet/IP engine**: `tbIpToArr`, `tbSubnetOf`, `tbBroadcastOf`, `tbSameSubnet`, `tbMaskToCidr`, `tbRebuildConnectedRoutes`. (4) **Broadcast domain**: `tbGetBroadcastDomain(state, srcDeviceId, vlan)` — VLAN-aware L2 flood-fill traversal across cables respecting trunk/access port modes. (5) **ARP simulation**: `tbSimARP` — broadcast ARP request across broadcast domain, reply from matching IP, table updates on source+target, switch MAC-address-table learning. (6) **Ping simulation**: `tbSimPing` — direct delivery if same subnet, longest-prefix-match routing for cross-subnet, recursive hop-by-hop forwarding with TTL, ARP resolution at each hop. (7) **DHCP DORA**: `tbSimDHCP` — full Discover→Offer→Request→Ack cycle with relay/helper-address support for cross-subnet DHCP. (8) **Packet animation**: `tbAnimatePacket` — animated dot via requestAnimationFrame along quadratic bezier cable paths, green for success, red for failure. (9) **Simulation UI**: persistent sim toolbar (Ping/ARP/DHCP/Clear Log buttons), ping dialog modal with source device selector + destination IP input, simulation log panel with timestamped entries. (10) **AI Topology Generation**: `tbGenerateAiTopology()` — user describes a network in plain text, Claude Haiku generates full JSON topology with devices, cables, IPs, routing tables. New purple AI Generate button in toolbar. (11) **AI Device Walkthrough**: `tbExplainDevice(deviceId)` — Explain button in config panel header, Claude explains device role + N10-009 concepts. Intro banner updated to describe simulator capabilities. 4-step howto strip. 59 new UAT assertions (874 total). Cache bumped to `netplus-v4.22.0`. |
| v4.21.0 | **Topology Builder Tier 3 — AI Coach (closes the TB 3-tier plan)** — ships the last tier on the original Topology Builder roadmap: an instructor-style AI walkthrough of whatever the user just built. Complements the Tier 2 static rules engine — grader for "is this design broken?", coach for "teach me *why* this design is good and how it maps to the exam." Tier 1+2+3 all now live in a single session today. (1) **Topology serializer** — new `tbSerializeTopology(state)` walks the device graph and emits a compact text description the LLM reads well: devices are grouped by type and assigned stable labels (e.g. `Firewall #1`, `Firewall #2` when duplicates exist), then two sections are emitted — `INVENTORY:` (one line per device type with counts) and `CONNECTIONS:` (one line per device listing neighbors with their cable type inline, e.g. `- Router → Firewall #1 (Fiber), Switch (Cat6)`). Orphan devices render as `(not connected)` so the coach can call them out. This shape avoids dumping raw JSON at the model, which tends to get LLMs to produce shallow observations. (2) **Response caching** — new `tbTopologyHash(state, scenarioId)` produces a deterministic short hash (djb2-style) over a simplified payload: scenario id + device types/positions (rounded to a 20px grid so micro-drags don't bust the cache) + sorted cable endpoints/types. Hash is used as a key in `STORAGE.TB_COACH_CACHE` (new `nplus_tb_coach_cache` localStorage entry). Responses are cached with a timestamp; `tbSaveCoachCache` trims to the 10 most recent entries. Cache hit → modal renders instantly with a `cached` pill next to the scenario title, no API call. This means re-opening a modal you just closed is free, and iterating a topology only bills for the meaningful changes. (3) **Coach entry point** — new async `tbCoachTopology()` (a) short-circuits on empty canvas with a toast, (b) short-circuits with a toast pointing to Settings if `STORAGE.KEY` is empty (no API call attempted), (c) checks cache and early-returns on hit, (d) shows the modal in loading state via `tbShowCoachModalLoading(scenario)` which renders a spinning ring + "Coach is analyzing your topology… (usually 3-5 seconds)" sub-line, (e) calls `CLAUDE_API_URL` with `CLAUDE_MODEL` (haiku 4.5), `max_tokens: 1500`, and a prompt that sets the instructor persona, feeds the scenario context + serialized topology, and asks for a strict JSON response with six keys: `tour` (2-3 sentence walkthrough), `strengths[]` (2-4 wins tied to objectives), `concerns[]` (2-4 things the static grader might miss), `upgrades[]` (2-3 actionable upgrades with rationale), `objectives[]` (2-4 N10-009 objectives exercised), `studyTip` (1 sentence pointing to what to drill next). Response is cleaned of any stray markdown fences (`\u0060\u0060\u0060json ... \u0060\u0060\u0060`), parsed with a JSON.parse + fallback `{...}` regex extract so the modal still works if the model wraps its output. Cached + rendered on success, `tbShowCoachModalError(msg)` on any failure path with a Retry button that just re-invokes `tbCoachTopology()`. (4) **Coach modal** — new `#tb-coach-modal` shares the overlay/card/backdrop styling from Tier 2's grade modal (class-reuse: `.tb-grade-modal`) but with a sky-blue accent palette distinct from the purple grader. Structure: coach head (cyan gradient band with scenario title + tour paragraph) → 4 severity-colored sections (Strengths green, Concerns amber, Upgrades sky, Objectives purple) each with bullet lists → final "Study next" callout in a dashed purple border box. New CSS block (~140 lines) covering `.tb-coach-loading`, `.tb-coach-spinner` (4px ring with top-color accent + `tb-coach-spin` keyframe), `.tb-coach-error`, `.tb-coach-head`, `.tb-coach-cached` (pill badge), `.tb-coach-section` + 4 section variants, `.tb-coach-list`, `.tb-coach-tip`, `.tb-tool-btn-coach` (sky-blue gradient toolbar button mirroring the purple `.tb-tool-btn-primary` from Tier 2). New 🤖 Coach button added to the toolbar between 🎓 Grade and 📷 Export PNG. Intro banner copy updated to drop the "Tier 3 adds an AI coach" promise since it's now live. 35 new UAT assertions covering the serializer output sections, all 7 new functions, cache trim behavior, API call shape, error handling, HTML wiring, and CSS presence. Total UAT: 780 → 815. Cache bumped to `netplus-v4.21.0`. |
| v4.20.0 | **Topology Builder Tier 2 — Auto-Grader + Scenario Mode + PNG Export** — closes the original Tier 2 scope (design validation) from the TB parent plan. Three features shipped together. (1) **Rules engine**: new `TB_GRADE_RULES` array of 12 rules (id/severity/label/hint/test), each `test(state)` inspecting `tbState.devices` + `tbState.cables` via three new graph helpers — `tbNeighborsOf(state, id)` returns the device objects on the other end of every cable touching `id`, `tbIsConnectedTo(state, id, type)` checks for a one-hop neighbor of a given type, `tbDevicesOfType(state, type)` filters the device list. Rules: `min-devices` (3+), `no-orphans`, `has-firewall` (critical), `cloud-behind-firewall` (critical — every cloud node must touch a firewall), `dmz-exists-if-public` (critical), `public-on-dmz` (critical — every public-* device must neighbor a dmz-switch and NOT a regular switch), `dmz-behind-firewall` (critical), `internal-behind-firewall` (warning — internal switch must touch a firewall or router), `wlc-wired-to-wap` (warning — does a 2-hop WLC→switch→WAP search so WLC/AP/switch triangle passes), `lb-fronts-servers` (warning — LB must neighbor 2+ servers or public-*), `ids-positioned` (info), `endpoints-on-internal` (warning — PC/printer/voip/iot must not touch dmz-switch). Severity-weighted deductions: critical -20, warning -10, info -5, floor at 0. Letter grade map: A (93+) / A- (87+) / B+ (80+) / B (73+) / C+ (65+) / C (58+) / D (50+) / F. `tbGradeTopology()` entry point runs the scenario-selected rule subset, appends synthetic critical rules for scenario device-count requirements, and calls `tbShowGradeModal()` which renders a fixed-overlay modal (`#tb-grade-modal`) with a score circle (color flips green/yellow/red at 87/70), pill counts (passed/critical/warnings/info), and 4 stacked sections (Critical / Warnings / Suggestions / Passed) each showing rule label + actionable hint. `tbCloseGradeModal()` closes the overlay; backdrop click also closes. (2) **Scenario mode**: new `TB_SCENARIOS` catalog with 5 scenarios — Free Build (all rules), Small Office (cloud→firewall→switch with 2+ PCs + printer), DMZ / Screened Subnet (full DMZ rule chain + public-* server requirement), Enterprise w/ IDS + LB (all rules + 2+ firewalls, IDS, LB, 2+ servers), Branch Office w/ Wireless (WLC + 2+ WAPs + 2+ PCs). Each scenario has `ruleIds` (which rules to run), `requires` (device-count hard requirements — `type: 'public-*'` wildcard matches any public-web/file/cloud), `requirements` (string list for display). New `tbSelectedScenario` state (defaults `'free'`). New `tbSetScenario(id)` updates state and calls `tbRenderScenarioPanel()` which populates `#tb-scenario-panel` with the scenario title, description, and requirements `<ul>` — hidden entirely in Free Build, shown above the canvas otherwise. Scenario device-count failures appear as critical rule rows labeled "Scenario requires N+ X (you have M)". New `<select id="tb-scenario-select">` in the toolbar wired to `tbSetScenario()`. `openTopologyBuilder()` now calls `tbRenderScenarioPanel()` on open so the panel restores with the current scenario. (3) **PNG export**: new `tbExportPNG()` clones the live `#tb-canvas` SVG, stamps explicit width/height attrs, prepends a dark `#0b1020` `<rect>` background (since the grid pattern is transparent), serializes with `XMLSerializer().serializeToString()`, base64-encodes via `btoa(unescape(encodeURIComponent(...)))` to survive multi-byte chars, feeds it through an `<img>` → `<canvas>` → `canvas.toBlob()` pipeline, and triggers a hidden `<a download>` click. Filename is `{topology_name_sanitized}_{timestamp}.png`. Zero external deps, pure browser APIs. New **🎓 Grade** primary button (`.tb-tool-btn-primary` gradient) and **📷 Export PNG** ghost button added to the toolbar. Intro banner copy updated to document the new Grade + Export capabilities. New CSS block (~170 lines) for `.tb-grade-modal` + `.tb-grade-backdrop` + `.tb-grade-card` + `.tb-grade-hero` + `.tb-grade-circle` + `.tb-grade-counts` + `.tb-grade-section` (severity-colored left borders for crit/warn/info/pass) + `.tb-scenario-panel` + `.tb-scenario-reqs` + `.tb-tool-btn-primary`. 44 new UAT assertions covering every rule id, every scenario id, all 3 new functions, HTML wiring, and CSS presence. Total UAT: 736 → 780. Cache bumped to `netplus-v4.20.0`. |
| v4.19.1 | **Topology Builder: DMZ Switch + clickable cable hitbox** — follow-up fixes after v4.19.0 shipped and the user built their first DMZ topology. (1) New `'dmz-switch'` device type added to `TB_DEVICE_TYPES` (red `#f43f5e`, short `DMZ`) with a dedicated `tbDeviceIcon` case that reuses the regular switch shape but stamps a red "DMZ" text label above it so it's visually unambiguous. Total device types: 16 → 17. (2) **Fat invisible cable hitbox** — clicking the thin curved conductor path was nearly impossible to hit, so cable rendering now emits three stacked paths per cable: the dark `#0b1020` sheath (pointer-events: none), the inner colored conductor with the cable type's width/color (pointer-events: none), and a third transparent `tb-cable-hit` path on top with `stroke="transparent" stroke-width="20"` that carries the click handler. The fat hitbox makes even thin fiber cables trivially clickable. The click handler now binds on `.tb-cable-hit` instead of `.tb-cable`, so once a cable is selected the user can press Del/Backspace to remove it (delete-selected logic was already in place, just unreachable due to the click target issue). Cursor is set to pointer on the hitbox via inline style. Cache bumped to `netplus-v4.19.1`. |
| v4.19.0 | **Topology Builder: cable type picker, realistic cables, public-server devices for DMZ, compact icons** — five pieces of feedback after v4.18.3 shipped, bundled into one release. (1) **Cable type picker in the palette** — new `TB_CABLE_TYPES` catalog with 5 entries (Cat6, Cat5e, Fiber, Coax, Console) each with `color`/`width`/`dash`. New `tbSelectedCableType` state (defaults `cat6`) + `tbSelectCableType(type)` function. A new "Cables" sub-section in the palette renders clickable chips with a color swatch + label; clicking a chip sets the selected type and the next cable drawn adopts it. The active chip gets a highlighted border + inset shadow so it's obvious which type is armed. `tbAddCable()` stamps `type: tbSelectedCableType` onto each new cable so saved topologies remember per-cable types. (2) **Realistic cable rendering** — cables were straight `<line>` elements, now they're `<path>` elements with a slight downward Q-curve drape (midpoint +16px) that makes them look like cables that sag under their own weight. Each cable renders as two stacked paths: a dark `#0b1020` sheath (width + 5px, 70% opacity, no pointer events) and an inner colored conductor at the type's `meta.width` and `meta.color`. Console cables render dashed (`stroke-dasharray: 6 4`) to distinguish rollover/mgmt from data. CSS moved stroke color/width out of the `.tb-cable` rule and into inline SVG attrs, since each cable now needs per-type styling. (3) **Public server device types for DMZ designs** — 3 new device types added to `TB_DEVICE_TYPES`: `public-web` (globe + WWW label, yellow `#fde047`), `public-file` (stacked rack + folder glyphs, orange `#fb923c`), and `public-cloud` (cloud silhouette with upload arrow, sky blue `#38bdf8`). Icons drawn in `tbDeviceIcon` following the same 56x40 SVG box pattern as other types. Total device types: 13 → 16. User can now wire a firewall → DMZ switch → public-web/file/cloud cluster → another firewall → internal switch to model a classic screened subnet. (4) **Compact canvas devices for 30-device layouts** — device rect shrunk 124×96 → 96×72, label font 16 → 13, icons wrapped in `<g transform="scale(0.72) translate(0,4)">` to fit the smaller box, label y-offset 34 → 26. `HALF_W`/`HALF_H` for cable edge-intersection dropped from 62/48 → 48/36. `tbOnMouseMove` bounds clamp dropped from 75/60 → 55/45 padding. The 1400×820 canvas now comfortably fits all 30 devices without cramming. (5) **Intro banner copy** — updated to name all the new device types and explicitly call out DMZ / screened subnet designs + Cat6/Cat5e/Fiber/Coax/Console cable picking, so users discover the DMZ use case from the banner. Cache bumped to `netplus-v4.19.0`. |
| v4.18.3 | **Topology Builder: discoverable wiring UX** — user feedback after v4.18.2: "i need to connect these devices somehow lol". The click-A → click-B wiring flow existed but was buried in tiny hint text inside the palette. Added (1) a **persistent 3-step how-to strip** right above the canvas with big numbered circles: Drag device, Click A then B to cable, Drag to move / Del to remove — impossible to miss. (2) A **floating "🔗 Wiring…" banner** that pops up center-top of the canvas the instant a user picks a first device, pulsing green with "click a second device to draw the cable, or press Esc to cancel." New `tbUpdateWireOverlay()` helper toggles `#tb-wire-overlay` visibility from `tbPendingCableFrom`, hooked into both `tbUpdateStatus` and `tbRenderCanvas` so it stays in sync with every state change. The existing pending-device dashed green ring stays as secondary confirmation. No behavioral changes to the wiring code — this is purely a discoverability fix. Cache bumped to `netplus-v4.18.3`. |
| v4.18.2 | **Topology Builder: real icons, visible cables, 13 device types, 30-device cap** — second polish pass after v4.18.1 feedback. (1) **Emoji icons replaced with inline SVG shapes** — new `tbDeviceIcon(type, color)` function returns a per-type `<g>` fragment (router = rack with port lights + antennas, switch = port grid, WAP = concentric wifi arcs, firewall = brick wall, cloud silhouette, server = stacked rack, load-balancer = splitting arrows, IDS = eye-in-box, WLC = wifi + base, printer = tray+paper, VoIP = keypad phone, IoT = camera/sensor, PC = monitor+stand). Palette items and canvas devices both call the helper so the look is consistent across drag source and drop target; `TB_DEVICE_TYPES` no longer carries an emoji char. (2) **Cable visibility fix** — new `tbEdgePoint(cx,cy,tx,ty,halfW,halfH)` computes where a ray from device center hits the 124×96 rect edge, so cables now render edge-to-edge instead of disappearing under device rects. Stroke bumped 3px→6px, base color flipped from muted grey to `var(--accent-light)` with a purple drop-shadow; hover 6px→8px; selected cables render amber (`#fbbf24`) so the delete target is obvious. (3) **6 new Network+ device types** — Load Balancer, IDS/IPS, WLC (wireless LAN controller), Printer, VoIP Phone, and IoT Device added to `TB_DEVICE_TYPES` bringing the total from 7 → 13. Palette gets `max-height: 760px` + `overflow-y: auto` so the extra items scroll gracefully in the fixed 200px column. Intro banner copy updated to name all the new device types. (4) **Device cap 15 → 30** — `TB_MAX_DEVICES = 30` per user request; the device-count pill (`#tb-device-count`) now shows `0 / 30 devices`. Every existing guardrail (toast on full, cap enforced in `tbAddDevice`) still flows through the constant so nothing else had to change. Cache bumped to `netplus-v4.18.2`. |
| v4.18.1 | **Topology Builder polish pass** — first-use feedback after v4.18 shipped: (1) **Devices were tiny and unreadable** — bumped rect from 72x60 to 124x96, icon font 24→40, label font 11→16 + bold, stroke 2→2.5. (2) **Canvas/panel too small** — viewBox 1200x700→1400x820, canvas min-height 560px→720px, palette column 180px→200px, page max-width overridden from default 800px to 1500px specifically for `#page-topology-builder` so the builder gets real estate. Bounds-clamp updated (50/40→75/60) to account for larger devices. (3) **No intro text** — added `.tb-intro-banner` above the toolbar with a 🔧 icon, title "Design your own network — visually", and 2-line summary explaining drag-to-place, cable wiring, 5-slot save, and what Tier 2/3 will add. Purple gradient + accent left border, non-collapsible (always visible). (4) **No clear-all button** — added `tbClearCanvas()` + 🧹 Clear button in the toolbar (next to Delete). Unlike `tbNewTopology` which creates a fresh topology with a new id, Clear wipes devices+cables in the CURRENT topology but keeps the id/name intact — useful when you want to restart the layout without losing the save slot. Confirms before wiping, no-ops on empty canvas. Cache bumped to `netplus-v4.18.1`. |
| v4.18 | **Topology Builder Tier 1 — SVG canvas + device palette + cables + save/load (#74)** — first of 3 planned tiers for the visual network topology builder feature (parent #73, Tier 2 = config panels #75, Tier 3 = AI coach #76). New top-level `🔧 Topology Builder` menu button on the setup page launches a new `#page-topology-builder` with a 1200x700 SVG canvas (grid pattern background) and a left-side device palette containing 7 device types: Router, Switch, WAP, PC, Server, Firewall, Cloud. Each device type is defined in a single `TB_DEVICE_TYPES` map with `label`, `icon`, `color`, `short` — adding a new type is one entry. State is a single `tbState` object: `{ id, name, devices: [{id, type, x, y}], cables: [{id, from, to}], created, updated }`, single source of truth, all rendering derives from it. Interactions: **drag from palette** (HTML5 drag API) drops device at cursor position (coordinates translated from client space to SVG viewBox via `createSVGPoint` + `getScreenCTM`); **click device A → click device B** draws a cable (second click completes, clicking the same device cancels); **drag a placed device** moves it within canvas bounds (50/40 padding enforced); **click empty canvas** deselects; **Delete/Backspace** removes selected device (cascade removes any cables touching it) or selected cable; **Escape** cancels current selection and pending cable. Guardrails: **15-device cap** (toast when full), **self-loop prevention**, **duplicate cable detection** (either direction), **device cap enforced before add**, **input field detection** so Delete inside an input doesn't nuke the topology. Persistence: **`STORAGE.TOPOLOGY_DRAFT`** auto-saves on every change (drop, move, cable, delete) so accidental reload never loses work; **`STORAGE.TOPOLOGIES`** holds up to 5 named saves with FIFO eviction by `updated` timestamp when the 6th is added; **Save As** prompts for a name and upserts by topology id; **Load** dropdown rebuilds from saves with device-count badges; **New** prompts to confirm if the canvas has devices. Mobile nudge: viewport < 900px width shows a friendly "Topology Builder works best on desktop" callout with an "Open Anyway" override that sets `tbMobileOverride = true` and continues. Builder state is prefixed `tb*` (tbState, tbSelectedId, tbPendingCableFrom, tbDragging, tbPaletteDrag, tbMobileOverride, tbCanvasHandlersAttached, tbKeyHandlerAttached) to avoid collision with the existing PBQ `topoDevices`/`selectedTopoDevice` state used by the drag-into-zones question type. No device configuration, no validation, no AI coach yet — that's Tier 2 and Tier 3. Cache bumped to `netplus-v4.18`. |
| v4.17 | **Guided Terminal Labs — remaining 5 shipped (#70)** — closes the original v4.16 (#69) vision by adding the 5 labs that were deliberately scoped out of the MVP: (1) **TLS Handshake & Secure Protocols** (obj 4.3, 6 steps) — full cert chain dump via `openssl s_client`, SNI walkthrough, `curl -vI` TLS handshake, cert expiry check, badssl.com expired-cert recognition, cipher suite inspection. Aliased to `Securing TCP/IP` + `PKI & Certificate Management`. (2) **ARP & Layer 2 Adjacency** (obj 2.1, 6 steps) — MAC address + OUI lookup, live ARP cache walk, forced ARP via ping, gateway MAC resolution. Aliased to `Switch Features & VLANs` + `Cabling & Topology`. (3) **Subnetting Your Own Network** (obj 1.4, 6 steps) — pulls user's real IP/mask via `ifconfig`, hex-to-CIDR conversion, by-hand network/broadcast/usable math, `ipcalc` verification at /24 and harder /26, optional IPv6 branch. Aliased to `Subnetting & IP Addressing` + `IPv6`. (4) **Network Monitoring with netstat, lsof, and tcpdump** (obj 3.2, 6 steps) — protocol counters via `netstat -s`, connection table via `netstat -an`, process mapping via `lsof -i`, port narrowing, live rates via `nettop`, packet-level truth via `sudo tcpdump -c 10 port 53`. Aliased to `Network Monitoring & Observability` + `Network Operations`. (5) **The 7-Step Troubleshooting Methodology — Live** (obj 5.1, 8 steps — all 7 CompTIA steps with an extra Step 3 for confirm-with-current-resolver) — real outage walkthrough using ping/nslookup/networksetup, uses read-only commands (`-getdnsservers`) so the lab never actually breaks the user's network. Aliased to `CompTIA Troubleshooting Methodology` + `Network Troubleshooting & Tools` — this replaces the temporary alias from v4.16 that pointed those topics at `_routingLab`. `primaryKeys` map in `renderPortLabsList` extended with 5 new entries so each new lab has a stable launch key on the Port Drill pregame panel. Total lab count: **3 → 8**. All 8 labs appear as deduped cards in the Port Drill pregame "🖥️ Guided Terminal Labs" panel and as the purple `td-lab-callout` on every Topic Deep Dive whose topic is in the map. Cache bumped to `netplus-v4.17`. Closes #70. |
| v4.16.2 | **Bugfix: Guided Lab Back button returns to Port Drill** — after v4.16.1 added lab launch buttons to the Port Drill pregame page, hitting Back from a lab dumped users onto `page-topic-dive` showing its stale "Generating topic guide…" skeleton. Root cause: `openGuidedLab()` tracked return pages from a whitelist (`page-topic-dive`, `page-quiz`, `page-review`, `page-results`, `page-exam-results`) that didn't include `page-ports`, so the `pages.find(...)` call fell through to the `page-topic-dive` default. Fix: added `page-ports` to the whitelist and changed the fallback from `page-topic-dive` to `page-ports`. Cache bumped to `netplus-v4.16.2`. |
| v4.16.1 | **Dedicated Port Drill sections for Try It In Terminal + Guided Terminal Labs** — user feedback after v4.16 shipped: both #68 and #69 should have their own standalone panels on the Port Drill pregame page rather than living solely inline inside the Port Reference card list and on the Topic Deep Dive panel. Added two new collapsible `<details>` panels below the existing Port Reference: (1) **💻 Try It In Terminal** — dedicated scrollable list grouped by the same `portCategories` used by Port Reference, showing every port with a curated command in a larger, more readable `.port-term-row` format (port number badge + protocol label + transport pill + embedded terminal card with copy button). New `renderPortTerminalList()` renderer called from `startPortDrill()` right after `renderPortReference()`. (2) **🖥️ Guided Terminal Labs** — dedicated panel showing all 3 labs (DNS, Routing, Ports) as full-width cards with title, meta pills (objective / duration / step count), intro paragraph, and a big "Start Lab" button that calls `openGuidedLab()` with a stable primary topic key (DNS Records & DNSSEC / Routing Protocols / Port Numbers). New `renderPortLabsList()` dedupes the `guidedLabs` map by lab title so multi-aliased labs only appear once. Inline port-ref-cmd strips on Port Reference cards are preserved (both surfaces now coexist — the inline version for quick scan while browsing, the dedicated panel for focused command study). Cache bumped to `netplus-v4.16.1`. |
| v4.16 | **Try It In Terminal (#68) + Guided Terminal Labs (#69)** — curated shell-command reinforcement layer across Port Drill, Topic Deep Dive, and a brand-new Guided Lab page. Three new static data maps: `portCommands` (33/40 ports get a curated macOS/iOS Terminal demo command + one-liner note — e.g. HTTPS → `curl -I https://example.com`, DNS → `dig google.com`, NTP → `sntp time.apple.com`, SNMP → `snmpwalk -v2c -c public demo.snmplabs.com system`, BGP → `whois -h whois.radb.net AS15169`), `topicCommands` (14 N10-009 topics mapped to 2-5 commands each — DNS, Routing, Troubleshooting Methodology, NAT, Port Numbers, Subnetting, TLS, IPv6, Monitoring, Switching, Wireless, IPsec/VPN, TCP/IP Apps), and `guidedLabs` (3 full coached walkthroughs keyed by 7 topic aliases — DNS Records & Recursive Resolution, Routing & Your Real Default Gateway, Ports & Listening Services, each 15 min / 5-7 steps with narration → command → "what you should see" per step). New reusable `.terminal-card` component with a fixed dark GitHub-style palette (#0b1020 bg, #7ee787 prompt, monospace) and a one-tap copy button via `copyCmd(event, cmd)` → `navigator.clipboard.writeText` + ✓ feedback + 1.2s revert. `_terminalCardHtml(cmd, note)` helper renders the same card across all three surfaces so the look stays consistent. `_portCard()` now detects `portCommands[p.proto]` and flips to a vertical layout (`.port-ref-card-has-cmd`) with a tight `.port-ref-cmd` strip showing the command + copy button under each port number/protocol block on the Port Reference panel. `renderTopicDive()` appends two new `td-section` blocks via `_renderTopicTerminalSection(topicName)` (💻 blue-tinted, "Try It In Terminal" with command list) and `_renderTopicLabSection(topicName)` (🖥️ purple-tinted "Guided Terminal Lab" callout button) — both conditional on whether curated data exists for the topic, so sections only render when they'd actually add value. New `#page-guided-lab` page in index.html with a dedicated layout: meta pills row (objective, duration, step count) + lead-in intro card + stepped list where each step has a step number + narration + embedded terminal card + a green-bordered "What you should see" expectation block + closing "wrap-up" card that tells the user to head back and drill the quiz. `openGuidedLab(topicName)` handles rendering, remembers return page (topic dive / quiz / review / results / exam-results) so the Back button always returns correctly. Static lab data = no backend, no AI, fully offline. Cache bumped to `netplus-v4.16.0`. Closes #68 and #69 — the two features filed earlier today. |
| v4.15 | **Port Drill: Secure Pairs mode (#30)** — fourth Port Drill mode joining Timed/Endless/Family. Dedicated drill for the Network+ secure↔insecure protocol pairings (HTTP↔HTTPS, FTP↔FTPS/SFTP, Telnet↔SSH, SMTP↔SMTPS/587, LDAP↔LDAPS, POP3↔POP3S, IMAP↔IMAPS). New `securePairs` static dataset with 9 mappings — FTP and SMTP appear twice (each has two valid secure equivalents) with `qualifier` strings ("over SSL/TLS", "over SSH", "with implicit TLS", "using STARTTLS submission") to disambiguate the prompt and `siblingProto` to exclude the alternate from distractors. New `nextPortPairsQ()` randomizes between two question formats: **port-pick** ("HTTPS is the secure version of HTTP. Which port does HTTPS use?") and **proto-pick** ("Which protocol replaces FTP (port 21) over SSH?"). Distractors come from the secure/insecure pool, deduped by the answered field, with the sibling protocol excluded so users never have to choose between two technically-correct answers. Timeless like family mode — one wrong answer ends the streak. New `STORAGE.PORT_PAIRS_BEST` (`nplus_port_pairs_best`) tracks pairs-mode best independently from Timed/Endless/Family. `setPortMode()`, `beginPortDrill()`, `pickPort()`, and `endPortDrill()` all extended for the new mode; `pickPort()` ends the run on wrong for both endless and pairs. New "&#128274; Secure Pairs" button on the pregame mode toggle. Cache bumped to `netplus-v4.15.0` |
| v4.14 | **Bulk Mixed quiz presets (30 / 60 / 100 questions)** — three new preset tiles below the existing 5/15/30-min row on the setup page that fire large mixed-topic Exam-Level quizzes. Single `fetchQuestions` calls become unreliable above ~20 Qs, so the new `startBulkQuiz(count)` mirrors `startExam`'s batching: chunks the request into 18-Q batches with 2 retries each, drives the existing `#load-progress` bar, then runs the same `aiValidateQuestions` → `validateQuestions` → `injectPBQs` pipeline as `startQuiz` before flipping to the quiz page. `applyPreset()` routes `bulk30/bulk60/bulk100` to `startBulkQuiz` and clears all `#count-group` chip highlights (no chip matches 30/60/100). Topic forced to Mixed, difficulty forced to Exam Level. Cache bumped to `netplus-v4.14.0` |
| v4.12.1 | **Family Drill split into its own Port Drill mode** — user feedback on v4.12.0 was that the 20% family-Q sprinkle into the existing Timed/Endless flow felt jarring and broke the rhythm of single-port memorization. `nextPortQ()` reverted to the original 50/50 port/proto split with no family branch. New `'family'` value in `portMode` joins `'timed'` and `'endless'`; new "Family Drill" button on the pregame mode toggle (`#port-mode-family`). `setPortMode()` handles all 3 modes — family hides the timer block, shows "BEST STREAK" label, swaps in a family-specific description ("No timer. Each question asks you to select every port in a protocol family — one wrong submission ends the streak."). `beginPortDrill()` routes to `nextPortFamilyQ()` when `portMode === 'family'` and never starts the countdown timer. `submitPortFamilyAnswer()` treats family as endless-style: one wrong submission ends the streak, never deducts time, and re-rolls another family Q on success (no fall-through to single-Q rotation). New `STORAGE.PORT_FAMILY_BEST` (`nplus_port_family_best`) tracks the family-mode best independently from `PORT_BEST` (timed) and `PORT_STREAK_BEST` (endless). `endPortDrill()` labels the final score "family streak" in family mode. Cache bumped to `netplus-v4.12.1` |
